t_id.jalalayn-id: (Supaya kami dapat bertasbih kepada-Mu) yakni melakukan tasbih (dengan banyak).
t_id.iwq: supaya kami banyak bertasbih kepada Engkau,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كي نسبحك» تسبيحا «كثيرا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَيۡ نُسَبِّحَكَ كَثِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Supaya kami dapat bertasbih kepada-Mu) yakni melakukan tasbih (dengan banyak)

ar: وَنَذۡكُرَكَ كَثِیرًا
ar_simple: ونذكرك كثیرا

t.sahih-int: And remember You much
t.asad: and remember Thee without cease
t.yusuf-ali: And remember Thee without stint
t.pickthall: And much remember Thee
t.arberry: and remember Thee abundantly
t.hilali-khan: And remember You much
t.itani: And remember You much.
t.mubarakpuri: "And remember You much,"
t.qarib: and remember You much
t.wahiduddin: and remember You much
t.shakir: And remember Thee oft
t.haleem: and remember You often
t.maariful: and remember You in abundance
t.khattab: and remember You much
t.ghali: And remember You much
t.irving: and mention You often
t.iwq: And remember You much

t_id.kemenag: dan banyak mengingat Engkau.
t_id.sabiq: dan banyak menyebut nama-nama-Mu yang terbaik.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan dapat mengingat-Mu) berzikir kepada-Mu (dengan banyak pula).
t_id.iwq: dan banyak mengingat Engkau.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونذكرك» ذكرا «كثيرا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَذۡكُرَكَ كَثِيرًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan dapat mengingat-Mu) berzikir kepada-Mu (dengan banyak pula)

ar: إِنَّكَ كُنتَ بِنَا بَصِیرࣰا
ar_simple: إنك كنت بنا بصیرا

t.sahih-int: Indeed, You are of us ever Seeing
t.asad: Verily, Thou seest all that is within us
t.yusuf-ali: For Thou art He that (ever) regardeth us
t.pickthall: Lo! Thou art ever Seeing us
t.arberry: Surely Thou seest into us
t.hilali-khan: Verily! You are of us Ever a Well-Seer
t.itani: You are always watching over us.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Verily, You are ever seeing us."
t.qarib: You see and know all about us
t.wahiduddin: You are surely watching over us
t.shakir: Surely, Thou art seeing us
t.haleem: You are always watching over us.’
t.maariful: You are certainly watchful over us.”
t.khattab: for truly You have ˹always˺ been overseeing us.”
t.ghali: Surely You have been Ever-Beholding us
t.irving: since You have been Observant of us
t.iwq: Indeed, You are of us ever Seeing

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Engkau adalah Maha Melihat (keadaan) kami".
t_id.sabiq: Ya Tuhan kami, Sesungguhnya Engkau selalu mengetahui kami dan menjamin urusan kami."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Engkau adalah Maha Mengetahui keadaan kami)" Maha Mengetahuinya, oleh sebab itu maka Engkau akan memberikan nikmat-Mu kepadaku dengan mengangkat Harun menjadi Rasul.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Engkau adalah Maha Melihat (keadaan) kami".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنك كنت بصيرا» عالما فأنعمت بالرسالة
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّكَ كُنتَ بِنَا بَصِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Engkau adalah Maha Mengetahui keadaan kami)" Maha Mengetahuinya, oleh sebab itu maka Engkau akan memberikan nikmat-Mu kepadaku dengan mengangkat Harun menjadi Rasul

ar: قَالَ قَدۡ أُوتِیتَ سُؤۡلَكَ یَـٰمُوسَىٰ
ar_simple: قال قد أوتیت سؤلك یموسى

t.sahih-int: [Allah] said, "You have been granted your request, O Moses
t.asad: Said He: "Thou art granted all that thou hast asked for, O Moses
t.yusuf-ali: (Allah) said: "Granted is thy prayer, O Moses
t.pickthall: He said: Thou art granted thy request, O Moses
t.arberry: Said He, 'Thou art granted, Moses, thy petition
t.hilali-khan: Allah said: "You are granted your request, O Musa (Moses)
t.itani: He said, “You are granted your request, O Moses.
t.mubarakpuri: (Allah) said: "You are granted your request, O Musa!"
t.qarib: [God] said, "You have been granted your request, Moses
t.wahiduddin: God said, "You have been granted your request, Moses
t.shakir: He said: You are indeed granted your petition, O Musa
t.haleem: God said, ‘Moses, your request is granted
t.maariful: He said, “You have been granted your request O Mūsā
t.khattab: Allah responded, “All that you requested has been granted, O Moses
t.ghali: Said He, "You are already granted (Literally: brought) your request, O Musa (Moses)
t.irving: He said: "You have been granted your request, Moses
t.iwq: Allah said, "You have been granted your request, O Moses

t_id.kemenag: Allah berfirman: "Sesungguhnya telah diperkenankan permintaanmu, hai Musa".
t_id.sabiq: Allah berfirman kepada Mûsâ, "Sesungguhnya Aku telah mengabulkan apa yang kamu minta. Ini adalah sebuah karunia untukmu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Allah berfirman, "Sesungguhnya telah dikabulkan permintaanmu, hai Musa) sebagai anugerah Kami kepadamu.
t_id.iwq: Allah berfirman: "Sesungguhnya telah diperkenankan permintaanmu, hai Musa".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال قد أوتيت سُؤلك يا موسى» منا عليك
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ قَدۡ أُوتِيتَ سُؤۡلَكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Allah berfirman, "Sesungguhnya telah dikabulkan permintaanmu, hai Musa) sebagai anugerah Kami kepadamu

ar: وَلَقَدۡ مَنَنَّا عَلَیۡكَ مَرَّةً أُخۡرَىٰۤ
ar_simple: ولقد مننا علیك مرة أخرى

t.sahih-int: And We had already conferred favor upon you another time
t.asad: And, indeed, We bestowed Our favour upon thee at a time long since past
t.yusuf-ali: And indeed We conferred a favour on thee another time (before)
t.pickthall: And indeed, another time, already We have shown thee favour
t.arberry: Already another time We favoured thee
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We conferred a favour on you another time (before)
t.itani: We had favored you another time.
t.mubarakpuri: "And indeed We conferred a favor on you another time (before)."
t.qarib: And We have already favored you in a previous time
t.wahiduddin: Indeed, We showed Our favour to you before also
t.shakir: And certainly We bestowed on you a favor at another time
t.haleem: Indeed We showed you favour before
t.maariful: And We had bestowed Our favour on you another time
t.khattab: And surely We had shown You favour before
t.ghali: And indeed already another time We have been bounteous to you
t.irving: We endowed you another time
t.iwq: And We had already conferred favor upon you another time

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberi nikmat kepadamu pada kali yang lain,
t_id.sabiq: Sebelumnya, Kami juga telah berkenan memberimu sebuah karunia yang lain tanpa diminta.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberi nikmat kepadamu pada kali yang lain).
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberi nikmat kepadamu pada kali yang lain,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد مننا عليك مرة أخرى»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ مَنَنَّا عَلَيۡكَ مَرَّةً أُخۡرَىٰٓ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberi nikmat kepadamu pada kali yang lain

ar: إِذۡ أَوۡحَیۡنَاۤ إِلَىٰۤ أُمِّكَ مَا یُوحَىٰۤ
ar_simple: إذ أوحینا إلى أمك ما یوحى

t.sahih-int: When We inspired to your mother what We inspired
t.asad: when We inspired thy mother with this inspiration
t.yusuf-ali: Behold! We sent to thy mother, by inspiration, the message
t.pickthall: When we inspired in thy mother that which is inspired
t.arberry: when We revealed what was revealed unto thy mother
t.hilali-khan: When We inspired your mother with that which We inspired
t.itani: When We inspired your mother with the inspiration.
t.mubarakpuri: "When We inspired your mother with that which We inspired."
t.qarib: when We inspired your mother with this
t.wahiduddin: when We revealed Our will to your mother, saying
t.shakir: When We revealed to your mother what was revealed
t.haleem: We inspired your mother, saying
t.maariful: when We revealed to your mother what was to be revealed
t.khattab: when We inspired your mother with this
t.ghali: As We revealed what was revealed to your mother
t.irving: when We revealed whatever was revealed to your mother
t.iwq: When We inspired to your mother what We inspired

t_id.kemenag: yaitu ketika Kami mengilhamkan kepada ibumu suatu yang diilhamkan,
t_id.sabiq: Yaitu, ketika Kami mengilhamkan kepada ibumu sebuah ilham yang kelak merupakan sumber hidupmu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika) lafal Idz di sini mengandung makna Ta'lil (mengilhamkan kepada ibumu) di dalam mimpi, atau berupa inspirasi, yaitu sewaktu ibumu melahirkan dirimu, dan ia merasa khawatir Firaun akan membunuhmu bersama-sama dengan anak-anak lelaki lainnya yang baru dilahirkan saat itu (suatu yang diilhamkan") mengenai urusanmu. Selanjutnya dijelaskan ilham tersebut dalam firman selanjutnya:
t_id.iwq: yaitu ketika Kami mengilhamkan kepada ibumu suatu yang diilhamkan,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إذ» للتعليل «أوحينا إلى أمك» مناما أو إلهاما لما ولدتك وخافت أن يقتلك فرعون في جملة من يولد «ما يوحى» في أمرك ويبدل منه
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِذۡ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika) lafal Idz di sini mengandung makna Ta'lil (mengilhamkan kepada ibumu) di dalam mimpi, atau berupa inspirasi, yaitu sewaktu ibumu melahirkan dirimu, dan ia merasa khawatir Firaun akan membunuhmu bersama-sama dengan anak-anak lelaki lainnya yang baru dilahirkan saat itu (suatu yang diilhamkan") mengenai urusanmu. Selanjutnya dijelaskan ilham tersebut dalam firman selanjutnya

ar: أَنِ ٱقۡذِفِیهِ فِی ٱلتَّابُوتِ فَٱقۡذِفِیهِ فِی ٱلۡیَمِّ فَلۡیُلۡقِهِ ٱلۡیَمُّ بِٱلسَّاحِلِ یَأۡخُذۡهُ عَدُوࣱّ لِّی وَعَدُوࣱّ لَّهُۥۚ وَأَلۡقَیۡتُ عَلَیۡكَ مَحَبَّةࣰ مِّنِّی وَلِتُصۡنَعَ عَلَىٰ عَیۡنِیۤ
ar_simple: أن ٱقذفیه فی ٱلتابوت فٱقذفیه فی ٱلیم فلیلقه ٱلیم بٱلساحل یأخذه عدو لی وعدو له وألقیت علیك محبة منی ولتصنع على عینی

t.sahih-int: [Saying], 'Cast him into the chest and cast it into the river, and the river will throw it onto the bank; there will take him an enemy to Me and an enemy to him.' And I bestowed upon you love from Me that you would be brought up under My eye
t.asad: Place him in a chest and throw it into the river, and thereupon the river will cast him ashore, [and] one who is an enemy unto Me and an enemy unto him will adopt him. "And [thus early] I spread Mine Own love over thee -and [this] in order that thou might be formed under Mine eye
t.yusuf-ali: Throw (the child) into the chest, and throw (the chest) into the river: the river will cast him up on the bank, and he will be taken up by one who is an enemy to Me and an enemy to him': But I cast (the garment of) love over thee from Me: and (this) in order that thou mayest be reared under Mine eye
t.pickthall: Saying: Throw him into the ark, and throw it into the river, then the river shall throw it on to the bank, and there an enemy to Me and an enemy to him shall take him. And I endued thee with love from Me that thou mightest be trained according to My will
t.arberry: Cast him into the ark, and cast him into the river, and let the river throw him up on the shore. An enemy of Mine and his shall take him." And I loaded on thee love from Me, and to be formed in My sight
t.hilali-khan: Saying: 'Put him (the child) into the Tabut (a box or a case or a chest) and put it into the river (Nile), then the river shall cast it up on the bank, and there, an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his shall take him.' And I endued you with love from Me, in order that you may be brought up under My Eye
t.itani: `Put him in the chest; then cast him into the river. The river will wash him to shore, where an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his will pick him up. And I have bestowed upon you love from Me, so that you may be reared before My eye.
t.mubarakpuri: "Saying: `Put him (the child) into the Tabut and put it into the river; then the river shall cast it up on the bank, and there, an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his shall take him.' And I endued you with love from Me, in order that you may be brought up under My Eye."
t.qarib: Put him into the chest and cast it into the river. Let the river wash it to the bank, where he will be taken by an enemy of Mine and his." And I bestowed upon you My love, so that you would be brought up under My watchful eye
t.wahiduddin: Put him into a chest, then cast it into the river. The river will cast it on to the bank, and there he shall be taken up by an enemy of Mine and his.' I showered My love on you so that you might be reared under My watchful eye
t.shakir: Saying: Put him into a chest, then cast it down into the river, then the river shall throw him on the shore; there shall take him up one who is an enemy to Me and enemy to him, and I cast down upon you love from Me, and that you might be brought up before My eyes
t.haleem: “Put your child into the chest, then place him in the river. Let the river wash him on to its bank, and he will be taken in by an enemy of Mine and his.” I showered you with My love and planned that you should be reared under My watchful eye
t.maariful: that is, ‘Put him (the baby) in the chest, then cast it into the river, then let the river throw it by the shore, and it will be picked up by one who is enemy to Me and enemy to him.’ And I had cast love on you from Myself (so that you might be favourite of all), and that you might be brought up under My eye
t.khattab: ‘Put him into a chest, then put it into the river. The river will wash it ashore, and he will be taken by ˹Pharaoh,˺ an enemy of Mine and his.’ And I blessed you with lovability from Me ˹O Moses˺ so that you would be brought up under My ˹watchful˺ Eye
t.ghali: (Saying), ‘"Hurl him in the coffer, then hurl it in the main; so let the main cast it on the shore. An enemy of Mine and his will take him." And I cast on you belovedness from Me, and to be worked out under My Eye (i.e., to be chosen for my Revelation)
t.irving: Cast him into the chest, and toss it into the river. The current will throw him up on the shore where an enemy of Mine as well as an enemy of his will pick him up." "I have lavished love of My own on you so that you might be made into My darling
t.iwq: Saying, 'Cast him into the chest and cast it into the river, and the river will throw it onto the bank; there will take him an enemy to Me and an enemy to him.' And I bestowed upon you love from Me that you would be brought up under My eye

t_id.kemenag: Yaitu: "Letakkanlah ia (Musa) didalam peti, kemudian lemparkanlah ia ke sungai (Nil), maka pasti sungai itu membawanya ke tepi, supaya diambil oleh (Fir'aun) musuh-Ku dan musuhnya. Dan Aku telah melimpahkan kepadamu kasih sayang yang datang dari-Ku; dan supaya kamu diasuh di bawah pengawasan-Ku,
t_id.sabiq: Kepada ibumu, Kami mengilhamkan agar ia meletakkanmu--ketika kamu masih seorang bayi dan menyusu--ke dalam peti dan melemparkannya ke sungai Nil, agar kamu selamat dan tidak dibunuh oleh Fir'aun. Sebab, saat itu, Fir'aun selalu membunuh semua bayi laki-laki yang lahir dari kalangan Banû Isrâ'îl. Air sungai itu pun Kami tundukkan untuk membawa peti sampai ke tepian. Kemudian, atas kehendak Kami, Fir'aun yang menjadi musuhmu dan musuh-Ku itu mengambil peti tersebut. Aku mencintaimu dengan penuh kasih sayang, supaya kamu dicintai oleh setiap orang yang melihat, dan supaya kamu diasuh secara terhormat di bawah pengawasan-Ku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu, "Letakkanlah ia) tarohlah ia (di dalam sebuah peti, kemudian lemparkanlah ia) yakni peti itu (ke sungai) yakni sungai Nil (maka pasti sungai itu membawanya ke tepi) ke pinggirnya. Kata perintah di sini mengandung makna kalimat berita (supaya diambil oleh musuh-Ku dan musuhnya) yaitu raja Firaun. (Dan Aku telah melimpahkan) sesudah Firaun mengambil anakmu darimu (kepadamu kasih sayang yang datang daripada-Ku) supaya semua orang merasa kasih sayang kepadamu, lalu Firaun akan merasa sayang kepadamu, demikian pula setiap orang yang melihatmu (dan supaya kamu diasuh di bawah pengawasan-Ku) kamu dipelihara di bawah asuhan dan penjagaan-Ku.
t_id.iwq: Yaitu: "Letakkanlah ia (Musa) didalam peti, kemudian lemparkanlah ia ke sungai (Nil), maka pasti sungai itu membawanya ke tepi, supaya diambil oleh (Fir'aun) musuh-Ku dan musuhnya. Dan Aku telah melimpahkan kepadamu kasih sayang yang datang dari-Ku; dan supaya kamu diasuh di bawah pengawasan-Ku,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أن اقذفيه» ألقيه «في التابوت فاقذفيه» بالتابوت «في اليم» بحر النيل «فلْيُلقه اليم بالساحل» أي شاطئه والأمر بمعنى الخبر «يأخذه عدو لي وعدو له» وهو فرعون «وألقيت» بعد أن أخذك «عليك محبة مني» لتحب في الناس فأحبك فرعون وكل من رآك «ولتُصنع على عيني» تربى على رعايتي وحفظي لك
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَنِ ٱقۡذِفِيهِ فِي ٱلتَّابُوتِ فَٱقۡذِفِيهِ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ فَلۡيُلۡقِهِ ٱلۡيَمُّ بِٱلسَّاحِلِ يَأۡخُذۡهُ عَدُوّٞ لِّي وَعَدُوّٞ لَّهُۥۚ وَأَلۡقَيۡتُ عَلَيۡكَ مَحَبَّةٗ مِّنِّي وَلِتُصۡنَعَ عَلَىٰ عَيۡنِيٓ (الْيَمِّ: نَهْرِ النِّيلِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu, "Letakkanlah ia) tarohlah ia (di dalam sebuah peti, kemudian lemparkanlah ia) yakni peti itu (ke sungai) yakni sungai Nil (maka pasti sungai itu membawanya ke tepi) ke pinggirnya. Kata perintah di sini mengandung makna kalimat berita (supaya diambil oleh musuh-Ku dan musuhnya) yaitu raja Firaun. (Dan Aku telah melimpahkan) sesudah Firaun mengambil anakmu darimu (kepadamu kasih sayang yang datang daripada-Ku) supaya semua orang merasa kasih sayang kepadamu, lalu Firaun akan merasa sayang kepadamu, demikian pula setiap orang yang melihatmu (dan supaya kamu diasuh di bawah pengawasan-Ku) kamu dipelihara di bawah asuhan dan penjagaan-Ku

ar: إِذۡ تَمۡشِیۤ أُخۡتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَن یَكۡفُلُهُۥۖ فَرَجَعۡنَـٰكَ إِلَىٰۤ أُمِّكَ كَیۡ تَقَرَّ عَیۡنُهَا وَلَا تَحۡزَنَۚ وَقَتَلۡتَ نَفۡسࣰا فَنَجَّیۡنَـٰكَ مِنَ ٱلۡغَمِّ وَفَتَنَّـٰكَ فُتُونࣰاۚ فَلَبِثۡتَ سِنِینَ فِیۤ أَهۡلِ مَدۡیَنَ ثُمَّ جِئۡتَ عَلَىٰ قَدَرࣲ یَـٰمُوسَىٰ
ar_simple: إذ تمشی أختك فتقول هل أدلكم على من یكفله فرجعنك إلى أمك كی تقر عینها ولا تحزن وقتلت نفسا فنجینك من ٱلغم وفتنك فتونا فلبثت سنین فی أهل مدین ثم جئت على قدر یموسى

t.sahih-int: [And We favored you] when your sister went and said, 'Shall I direct you to someone who will be responsible for him?' So We restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve. And you killed someone, but We saved you from retaliation and tried you with a [severe] trial. And you remained [some] years among the people of Madyan. Then you came [here] at the decreed time, O Moses
t.asad: [And thou wert under Mine eye] when thy sister went forth and said [to Pharaoh's people], 'Shall I guide you unto [a woman] who might take charge of him?'' And so We returned thee unto thy mother, so that her eye be gladdened, and that she might not sorrow [any longer]. "And [when thou camest of age, thou didst slay a man: but We did save thee from all grief, although We tried thee with various trials. "And then thou didst sojourn for years among the people of Madyan; and now thou hast come [here] as ordained [by Me], O Moses
t.yusuf-ali: Behold! thy sister goeth forth and saith, 'shall I show you one who will nurse and rear the (child)?' So We brought thee back to thy mother, that her eye might be cooled and she should not grieve. Then thou didst slay a man, but We saved thee from trouble, and We tried thee in various ways. Then didst thou tarry a number of years with the people of Midian. Then didst thou come hither as ordained, O Moses
t.pickthall: When thy sister went and said: Shall I show you one who will nurse him? and we restored thee to thy mother that her eyes might be refreshed and might not sorrow. And thou didst kill a man and We delivered thee from great distress, and tried thee with a heavy trial. And thou didst tarry years among the folk of Midian. Then camest thou (hither) by (My) providence, O Moses
t.arberry: when thy sister went out, saying, "Shall I point you to one to have charge of him?" So We returned thee to thy mother that she might rejoice, and not sorrow. Then thou slewest; a living soul, and We delivered thee out of grief, and We tried thee with many trials. Many years among the people of Midian thou didst sojourn, then camest hither, Moses, according to a decree
t.hilali-khan: When your sister went and said: 'Shall I show you one who will nurse him?' So We restored you to your mother, that she might cool her eyes and she should not grieve. Then you did kill a man, but We saved you from great distress and tried you with a heavy trial. Then you stayed a number of years with the people of Madyan (Midian). Then you came here according to the fixed term which I ordained (for you), O Musa (Moses)
t.itani: When your sister walked along, and said, ‘Shall I tell you about someone who will take care of him?' So We returned you to your mother, that she may be comforted, and not sorrow. And you killed a person, but We saved you from stress; and We tested you thoroughly. And you stayed years among the people of Median. Then you came back, as ordained, O Moses.
t.mubarakpuri: "When your sister went and said: `Shall I show you one who will nurse him' So We restored you to your mother, that she might cool her eyes and she should not grieve. Then you did kill a man, but We saved you from great distress and tried you with a heavy trial." Then you stayed a number of years with the people of Madyan. Then you came here according to the fixed term which I ordained (for you), O Musa!
t.qarib: Then your sister walked by and said, "Shall I direct you to someone who will take care of him?" We returned you to your mother, so that she could rejoice and not grieve. Later, you killed someone, but We rescued you from distress but put you through other severe tests. And you remained some years among the people of Midian. Then you came [here] as I ordained, Moses
t.wahiduddin: Recall when your sister walked along and said, "Shall I guide you to one who will take care of him?" Thus We returned you to your mother, so that her eyes might be cooled and that she might not grieve. And you killed a man and We delivered you from sorrow. We tested you with various trials. You stayed for a number of years among the people of Midian, then you came upto the standard, Moses
t.shakir: When your sister went and said: Shall I direct you to one who will take charge of him? So We brought you back to your mother, that her eye might be cooled and she should not grieve and you killed a man, then We delivered you from the grief, and We tried you with (a severe) trying. Then you stayed for years among the people of Madyan; then you came hither as ordained, O Musa
t.haleem: Your sister went out, saying, “I will tell you someone who will nurse him,” then We returned you to your mother so that she could rejoice and not grieve. Later you killed a man, but We saved you from distress and tried you with other tests. You stayed among the people of Midian for years, then you came here as I ordained
t.maariful: (Remember) when your sister was going (to the family of Pharaoh) and was saying, ‘Shall I lead you to one who nurses him?’ Thus We brought you back to your mother, so that her eyes might have comfort and she might not grieve. And you had killed a person, then We brought you out of the trouble; and We tested you with a great ordeal. Then you lived a number of years amidst the people of Madyan. After all this, you came O Mūsā, to a destined point of time
t.khattab: ˹Remember˺ when your sister came along and proposed, ‘Shall I direct you to someone who will nurse him?’ So We reunited you with your mother so that her heart would be put at ease, and she would not grieve. ˹Later˺ you killed a man ˹by mistake˺, but We saved you from sorrow, as well as other tests We put you through. Then you stayed for a number of years among the people of Midian. Then you came here as pre-destined, O Moses
t.ghali: As your sister walked about, (and) so said, ‘"Shall I indicate to you one to sponsor him?"' So We returned you to your mother so that she might comfort her eye (Literally: that her eye might settle down) and might not grieve. And you killed a self, (i.e., a person) yet We safely delivered you out of suffering, and We tempted you with many temptations. Then you lingered years among the population of Madyan. Thereafter you came (here), according to a determined (estimate), O Musa
t.irving: So your sister was walking along, and said: Shall I lead you to someone who will take care of him?´ Thus We returned you to your mother to comfort her and so she would not feel so sad." "You killed a soul and We saved you from grief. We tested you severely, and you stayed among the people of Midian for years. Then you came just as fate [decreed], Moses
t.iwq: And We favored you when your sister went and said, 'Shall I direct you to someone who will be responsible for him?' So We restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve. And you killed someone, but We saved you from retaliation and tried you with a severe trial. And you remained some years among the people of Madyan. Then you came here at the decreed time, O Moses

t_id.kemenag: (yaitu) ketika saudaramu yang perempuan berjalan, lalu ia berkata kepada (keluarga Fir'aun): "Bolehkah saya menunjukkan kepadamu orang yang akan memeliharanya?" Maka Kami mengembalikanmu kepada ibumu, agar senang hatinya dan tidak berduka cita. Dan kamu pernah membunuh seorang manusia, lalu Kami selamatkan kamu dari kesusahan dan Kami telah mencobamu dengan beberapa cobaan; maka kamu tinggal beberapa tahun diantara penduduk Madyan, kemudian kamu datang menurut waktu yang ditetapkan hai Musa,
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Mûsâ, ketahuilah pertolongan Kami kepadamu, saat saudara perempuanmu berjalan mengawasi dirimu. Ketika kamu telah berada di istana Fir'aun, dan saudaramu itu mengetahui bahwa mereka mencari orang yang dapat menyusuimu, ia menunjukkan mereka kepada ibumu. Kami mengembalikan kamu kepangkuan ibumu, supaya ia senang melihat kamu hidup dan kembali serta tidak bersedih dan menangis lagi. Ketika kamu menginjak dewasa, dan membunuh seorang laki-laki dari kaum Fir'aun tanpa sengaja, Kami menyelamatkanmu dari kesusahan yang menimpa. Kami menyelamatkanmu dari kejahatan mereka. Kemudian, kamu pergi ke Madyan dan tinggal di sana untuk beberapa tahun lamanya. Setelah itu, kamu kembali dari Madyan pada waktu yang telah Kami tentukan untuk mengangkatmu sebagai rasul.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika) lafal Idz di sini bermakna Ta'lil (saudaramu yang perempuan berjalan) namanya Maryam untuk menyelidiki beritamu. Karena sesungguhnya Firaun dan keluarganya telah mendatangkan orang-orang perempuan yang menyusui, sedangkan kamu tidak mau menerima air susu seorang pun di antara mereka (lalu ia berkata, 'Bolehkah saya menunjukkan kepada kalian orang yang akan memeliharanya?') kemudian usulnya itu ternyata diperkenankan oleh keluarga Firaun, maka segera Maryam mendatangkan ibunya, lalu Nabi Musa mau menerima air susunya. (Maka Kami mengembalikanmu kepada ibumu, agar senang hatinya) karena bertemu kembali denganmu (dan tidak berduka cita) sejak saat itu. (Dan kamu pernah membunuh seorang manusia) yaitu seorang bangsa Qibti di Mesir. Maka kamu merasa susah dan khawatir setelah membunuh orang itu terhadap pembalasan raja Firaun (lalu Kami selamatkan kamu dari kesusahan dan Kami telah mencobamu dengan beberapa cobaan) Kami telah mengujimu dengan beberapa cobaan selain dari peristiwa itu, kemudian Kami selamatkan pula kamu daripadanya (maka kamu tinggal beberapa tahun) yakni selama sepuluh tahun (di antara penduduk Madyan) sesudah kamu datang ke tempat itu dari negeri Mesir, yaitu kamu tinggal di tempat Nabi Syuaib yang kemudian ia mengawinkanmu dengan putrinya (kemudian kamu datang menurut waktu yang ditetapkan) di dalam ilmu-Ku dengan membawa risalah, yaitu dalam usia empat puluh tahun (hai Musa!).
t_id.iwq: (yaitu) ketika saudaramu yang perempuan berjalan, lalu ia berkata kepada (keluarga Fir'aun): "Bolehkah saya menunjukkan kepadamu orang yang akan memeliharanya?" Maka Kami mengembalikanmu kepada ibumu, agar senang hatinya dan tidak berduka cita. Dan kamu pernah membunuh seorang manusia, lalu Kami selamatkan kamu dari kesusahan dan Kami telah mencobamu dengan beberapa cobaan; maka kamu tinggal beberapa tahun diantara penduduk Madyan, kemudian kamu datang menurut waktu yang ditetapkan hai Musa,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إذ» للتعليل «تمشي أختك» مريم لتتعرف من خبرك وقد أحضروا مراضع وأنت لا تقبل ثدي واحدة منهن «فتقول هل أدلكم على من يكفله» فأجيبت فجاءت بأمه فقبل ثديها «فرجعناك إلى أمك كي تقر عينها» بلقائك «ولا تحزن» حينئذ «وقتلت نفسا» هو القبطي بمصر، فاغتممت لقتله من جهة فرعون «فنجيناك من الغم وفتناك فتونا» اختبرناك بالإيقاع في غير ذلك وخلصناك منه «فلبثت سنين» عشرا «في أهل مدين» بعد مجيئك إليها من مصر عند شعيب النبي وتزوجك بابنته «ثم جئت على قدر» في علمي بالرسالة وهو أربعون سنة من عمرك «يا موسى»
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِذۡ تَمۡشِيٓ أُخۡتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَن يَكۡفُلُهُۥۖ فَرَجَعۡنَٰكَ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ كَيۡ تَقَرَّ عَيۡنُهَا وَلَا تَحۡزَنَۚ وَقَتَلۡتَ نَفۡسٗا فَنَجَّيۡنَٰكَ مِنَ ٱلۡغَمِّ وَفَتَنَّـٰكَ فُتُونٗاۚ فَلَبِثۡتَ سِنِينَ فِيٓ أَهۡلِ مَدۡيَنَ ثُمَّ جِئۡتَ عَلَىٰ قَدَرٖ يَٰمُوسَىٰ (يَكْفُلُهُ: يُرَبِّيهِ، وَيُرْضِعُهُ, تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا: تَطِيبُ نَفْسُهَا, وَفَتَنَّاكَ فُتُونًا: ابْتَلَيْنَاكَ ابْتِلَاءً, عَلَى قَدَرٍ: عَلَى وَفْقِ الوَقْتِ المُقَدَّرِ لِإرْسَالِكَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika) lafal Idz di sini bermakna Ta'lil (saudaramu yang perempuan berjalan) namanya Maryam untuk menyelidiki beritamu. Karena sesungguhnya Firaun dan keluarganya telah mendatangkan orang-orang perempuan yang menyusui, sedangkan kamu tidak mau menerima air susu seorang pun di antara mereka (lalu ia berkata, 'Bolehkah saya menunjukkan kepada kalian orang yang akan memeliharanya?') kemudian usulnya itu ternyata diperkenankan oleh keluarga Firaun, maka segera Maryam mendatangkan ibunya, lalu Nabi Musa mau menerima air susunya. (Maka Kami mengembalikanmu kepada ibumu, agar senang hatinya) karena bertemu kembali denganmu (dan tidak berduka cita) sejak saat itu. (Dan kamu pernah membunuh seorang manusia) yaitu seorang bangsa Qibti di Mesir. Maka kamu merasa susah dan khawatir setelah membunuh orang itu terhadap pembalasan raja Firaun (lalu Kami selamatkan kamu dari kesusahan dan Kami telah mencobamu dengan beberapa cobaan) Kami telah mengujimu dengan beberapa cobaan selain dari peristiwa itu, kemudian Kami selamatkan pula kamu daripadanya (maka kamu tinggal beberapa tahun) yakni selama sepuluh tahun (di antara penduduk Madyan) sesudah kamu datang ke tempat itu dari negeri Mesir, yaitu kamu tinggal di tempat Nabi Syuaib yang kemudian ia mengawinkanmu dengan putrinya (kemudian kamu datang menurut waktu yang ditetapkan) di dalam ilmu-Ku dengan membawa risalah, yaitu dalam usia empat puluh tahun (hai Musa)

ar: وَٱصۡطَنَعۡتُكَ لِنَفۡسِی
ar_simple: وٱصطنعتك لنفسی

t.sahih-int: And I produced you for Myself
t.asad: for I have chosen thee for Mine Own service
t.yusuf-ali: And I have prepared thee for Myself (for service)
t.pickthall: And I have attached thee to Myself
t.arberry: I have chosen thee for My service
t.hilali-khan: And I have Istana'tuka, for Myself
t.itani: And I made you for Myself.
t.mubarakpuri: And I have chosen you for Myself.
t.qarib: And I have chosen you for Myself
t.wahiduddin: I have chosen you for Myself
t.shakir: And I have chosen you for Myself
t.haleem: I have chosen you for Myself
t.maariful: I have fashioned you for Myself
t.khattab: And I have selected you for My service
t.ghali: And I had you worked out for Myself
t.irving: I have produced you for Myself
t.iwq: And I produced you for Myself

t_id.kemenag: dan Aku telah memilihmu untuk diri-Ku.
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah memilihmu untuk diberi wahyu dan menyampaikan risalah-Ku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Aku telah memilihmu) telah menjadikanmu sebagai orang yang terpilih (untuk diri-Ku) untuk mengemban risalah.
t_id.iwq: dan Aku telah memilihmu untuk diri-Ku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واصطنعتك» اخترتك «لنفسي» بالرسالة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱصۡطَنَعۡتُكَ لِنَفۡسِي (وَاصْطَنَعْتُكَ لِنَفْسِي: اصْطَفَيْتُكَ لِرِسَالَتِي)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Aku telah memilihmu) telah menjadikanmu sebagai orang yang terpilih (untuk diri-Ku) untuk mengemban risalah

ar: ٱذۡهَبۡ أَنتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِءَایَـٰتِی وَلَا تَنِیَا فِی ذِكۡرِی
ar_simple: ٱذهب أنت وأخوك بءایتی ولا تنیا فی ذكری

t.sahih-int: Go, you and your brother, with My signs and do not slacken in My remembrance
t.asad: Go forth, [then,] thou and thy brother, with My messages, and never tire of remembering Me
t.yusuf-ali: Go, thou and thy brother, with My Signs, and slacken not, either of you, in keeping Me in remembrance
t.pickthall: Go, thou and thy brother, with My tokens, and be not faint in remembrance of Me
t.arberry: go therefore, thou and thy brother; with My signs, and neglect not to remember Me
t.hilali-khan: Go you and your brother with My Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and do not, you both, slacken and become weak in My Remembrance
t.itani: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not neglect My remembrance.
t.mubarakpuri: Go you and your brother with My Ayat, and do not, you both, slacken and become weak in My remembrance.
t.qarib: Go, you and your brother, with My signs and do not falter in remembering Me
t.wahiduddin: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not be remiss in remembering Me
t.shakir: Go you and your brother with My communications and be not remiss in remembering Me
t.haleem: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and make sure that you remember Me
t.maariful: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not be slack in My remembrance
t.khattab: Go forth, you and your brother, with My signs and never falter in remembering Me
t.ghali: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not slacken in Remembrance of Me
t.irving: Take My signs away, both you and your brother, and do not neglect to mention Me
t.iwq: Go, you and your brother, with My signs and do not slacken in My remembrance

t_id.kemenag: Pergilah kamu beserta saudaramu dengan membawa ayat-ayat-Ku, dan janganlah kamu berdua lalai dalam mengingat-Ku;
t_id.sabiq: Pergilah kamu bersama saudaramu, dengan bekal beberapa mukjizat-Ku sebagai bukti atas kenabian dan kerasulanmu. Janganlah kalian berdua lemah dalam menyampaikan risalah-Ku, dan jangan pula lalai untuk mengingat dan memohon pertolongan kepada-Ku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pergilah kamu beserta saudaramu) kepada manusia (dengan membawa ayat-ayat-Ku) yang berjumlah sembilan ayat (dan janganlah kamu berdua lalai) melalaikan (dalam mengingat-Ku) yaitu dengan cara bertasbih dan cara-cara lainnya.
t_id.iwq: Pergilah kamu beserta saudaramu dengan membawa ayat-ayat-Ku, dan janganlah kamu berdua lalai dalam mengingat-Ku;

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «اذهب أنت وأخوك» إلى الناس «بآياتي» التسع «ولا تَنِيا» تفترا «في ذكري» بتسبيح وغيره
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱذۡهَبۡ أَنتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِـَٔايَٰتِي وَلَا تَنِيَا فِي ذِكۡرِي (وَلَا تَنِيَا: لَا تَفْتُرَا وَلَا تَضْعُفَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pergilah kamu beserta saudaramu) kepada manusia (dengan membawa ayat-ayat-Ku) yang berjumlah sembilan ayat (dan janganlah kamu berdua lalai) melalaikan (dalam mengingat-Ku) yaitu dengan cara bertasbih dan cara-cara lainnya

ar: ٱذۡهَبَاۤ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
ar_simple: ٱذهبا إلى فرعون إنه طغى

t.sahih-int: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed
t.asad: go forth, both of you, unto Pharaoh: for, verily, he has transgressed all bounds of equity
t.yusuf-ali: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh, for he has indeed transgressed all bounds
t.pickthall: Go, both of you, unto Pharaoh. Lo! he hath transgressed (the bounds)
t.arberry: Go to Pharaoh, for he has waxed insolent
t.hilali-khan: Go, both of you, to Fir'aun (Pharaoh), verily, he has transgressed (all bounds in disbelief and disobedience and behaved as an arrogant and as a tyrant)
t.itani: Go to Pharaoh. He has tyrannized.
t.mubarakpuri: Go both of you to Fir`awn, verily, he has transgressed.
t.qarib: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. He has overstepped all My limits
t.wahiduddin: Go, both of you to Pharaoh, for he has transgressed all bounds
t.shakir: Go both to Firon, surely he has become inordinate
t.haleem: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh, for he has exceeded all bounds
t.maariful: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh; he has indeed transgressed all limits
t.khattab: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ˹all bounds˺
t.ghali: Go, (both of you), to Firaawn; (Pharaoh) surely he has tyrannized
t.irving: Go to Pharaoh; he has been so arrogant
t.iwq: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed

t_id.kemenag: Pergilah kamu berdua kepada Fir'aun, sesungguhnya dia telah melampaui batas;
t_id.sabiq: Pergilah kamu bersama saudaramu, Hârûn, kepada Fir'aun. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang kafir yang telah melampaui batas dalam kekufuran dan kezalimannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pergilah kamu berdua kepada Firaun, sesungguhnya dia telah melampaui batas) karena ia mengaku menjadi tuhan.
t_id.iwq: Pergilah kamu berdua kepada Fir'aun, sesungguhnya dia telah melampaui batas;

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «اذهبا إلى فرعون إنه طغى» بادعائه الربوبية
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱذۡهَبَآ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pergilah kamu berdua kepada Firaun, sesungguhnya dia telah melampaui batas) karena ia mengaku menjadi tuhan

ar: فَقُولَا لَهُۥ قَوۡلࣰا لَّیِّنࣰا لَّعَلَّهُۥ یَتَذَكَّرُ أَوۡ یَخۡشَىٰ
ar_simple: فقولا له قولا لینا لعله یتذكر أو یخشى

t.sahih-int: And speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear [Allah]
t.asad: But speak unto him in a mild manner, so that he might bethink himself or [at least] be filled with apprehension
t.yusuf-ali: But speak to him mildly; perchance he may take warning or fear (Allah)
t.pickthall: And speak unto him a gentle word, that peradventure he may heed or fear
t.arberry: yet speak gently to him, that haply he may be mindful, or perchance fear
t.hilali-khan: And speak to him mildly, perhaps he may accept admonition or fear Allah
t.itani: But speak to him nicely. Perhaps he will remember, or have some fear.”
t.mubarakpuri: And speak to him mildly, perhaps he may accept admonition or fear (Allah).
t.qarib: And speak to him gently that perhaps he may remember or fear [God]
t.wahiduddin: But speak gently to him; perhaps he may yet take heed or even feel afraid
t.shakir: Then speak to him a gentle word haply he may mind or fear
t.haleem: Speak to him gently so that he may take heed, or show respect.’
t.maariful: So speak to him in soft words. May be, he accepts the advice or fears (Allah)”
t.khattab: Speak to him gently, so perhaps he may be mindful ˹of Me˺ or fearful ˹of My punishment˺.”
t.ghali: So speak (you both) leniently (Literally: say a lenient) to him, that possibly he would remind himself or would be apprehensive
t.irving: Speak a soft word to him so that he may be reminded or even feel afraid
t.iwq: And speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear Allah

t_id.kemenag: maka berbicaralah kamu berdua kepadanya dengan kata-kata yang lemah lembut, mudah-mudahan ia ingat atau takut".
t_id.sabiq: Ajaklah ia untuk beriman kepada-Ku dengan lemah lembut. Mudah-mudahan ia ingat akan keimanan yang telah dilalaikannya, dan takut akan akibat sikapnya yang kafir dan zalim."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka berbicaralah kamu berdua kepadanya dengan kata-kata yang lemah lembut) untuk menyadarkannya supaya jangan mengaku menjadi tuhan (mudah-mudahan ia ingat) yakni sadar dan mau menerimanya (atau takut") kepada Allah lalu karenanya ia mau sadar. Ungkapan 'mudah-mudahan' berkaitan dengan pengetahuan Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun. Adapun menurut pengetahuan Allah, maka Dia telah mengetahui bahwa Firaun tidak akan mau sadar dari perbuatannya.
t_id.iwq: maka berbicaralah kamu berdua kepadanya dengan kata-kata yang lemah lembut, mudah-mudahan ia ingat atau takut".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فقولا له قولا لينا» في رجوعه عن ذلك «لعله يتذكر» يتعظ «أو يخشى» الله فيرجع والترجي بالنسبة إليهما لعلمه تعالى بأنه لا يرجع
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَقُولَا لَهُۥ قَوۡلٗا لَّيِّنٗا لَّعَلَّهُۥ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوۡ يَخۡشَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka berbicaralah kamu berdua kepadanya dengan kata-kata yang lemah lembut) untuk menyadarkannya supaya jangan mengaku menjadi tuhan (mudah-mudahan ia ingat) yakni sadar dan mau menerimanya (atau takut") kepada Allah lalu karenanya ia mau sadar. Ungkapan 'mudah-mudahan' berkaitan dengan pengetahuan Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun. Adapun menurut pengetahuan Allah, maka Dia telah mengetahui bahwa Firaun tidak akan mau sadar dari perbuatannya

ar: قَالَا رَبَّنَاۤ إِنَّنَا نَخَافُ أَن یَفۡرُطَ عَلَیۡنَاۤ أَوۡ أَن یَطۡغَىٰ
ar_simple: قالا ربنا إننا نخاف أن یفرط علینا أو أن یطغى

t.sahih-int: They said, "Our Lord, indeed we are afraid that he will hasten [punishment] against us or that he will transgress
t.asad: The two [brothers] said: "O our Sustainer! Verily, we fear lest he act hastily with regard to us, or lest he [continue to] transgress all bounds of equity
t.yusuf-ali: They (Moses and Aaron) said: "Our Lord! We fear lest he hasten with insolence against us, or lest he transgress all bounds
t.pickthall: They said: Our Lord! Lo! we fear that he may be beforehand with us or that he may play the tyrant
t.arberry: O our Lord,' said Moses and Aaron, 'truly we fear he may exceed against us, or wax insolent
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Our Lord! Verily! We fear lest he should hasten to punish us or lest he should transgress (all bounds against us)
t.itani: They said, “Lord, we fear he may persecute us, or become violent.”
t.mubarakpuri: Both of them said: "Our Lord! Verily, we fear lest he should hasten to punish us or lest he should transgress."
t.qarib: They said, "Our Lord, we are afraid that He will quickly do us harm or that he will overstep all boundaries
t.wahiduddin: They both said, "Our Lord, We fear that he may commit some excess against us, or exceed all bounds in transgression
t.shakir: Both said: O our Lord! Surely we fear that he may hasten to do evil to us or that he may become inordinate
t.haleem: They said, ‘Lord, we fear he will do us great harm or exceed all bounds.’
t.maariful: They said, “Our Lord, we fear that he will hasten against us, or will become (more) rebellious (against You).”
t.khattab: They both pleaded, “Our Lord! We fear that he may be quick to harm us or act tyrannically.”
t.ghali: They (both) said, "Our Lord, surely we (ourselves) fear he may exceed against us or tyrannize (us)
t.irving: They both said: "Our Lord, we fear lest he crack down on us or that he should act arrogant
t.iwq: They said, "Our Lord, indeed we are afraid that he will hasten punishment against us or that he will transgress

t_id.kemenag: Berkatalah mereka berdua: "Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya kami khawatir bahwa ia segera menyiksa kami atau akan bertambah melampaui batas".
t_id.sabiq: Lalu Mûsâ dan Hârûn memohon kepada Allah seraya berkata, "Ya Tuhan, sesungguhnya kami takut bila Fir'aun akan menyiksa kami, atau bahkan semakin melampaui batas."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah mereka berdua, "Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya kami khawatir bahwa ia segera menyiksa kami) mengambil keputusan yang cepat untuk menyiksa kami (atau akan bertambah melampaui batas") terhadap kami, yakni bertambah takabbur.
t_id.iwq: Berkatalah mereka berdua: "Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya kami khawatir bahwa ia segera menyiksa kami atau akan bertambah melampaui batas".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالا ربنا إننا نخاف أن يفرط علينا» أي يعجل بالعقوبة «أو أن يطغى» علينا أي يتكبر
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَا رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا نَخَافُ أَن يَفۡرُطَ عَلَيۡنَآ أَوۡ أَن يَطۡغَىٰ (يَفْرُطَ عَلَيْنَا: يُعَاجِلَنَا بِالعُقُوبَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah mereka berdua, "Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya kami khawatir bahwa ia segera menyiksa kami) mengambil keputusan yang cepat untuk menyiksa kami (atau akan bertambah melampaui batas") terhadap kami, yakni bertambah takabbur

ar: قَالَ لَا تَخَافَاۤۖ إِنَّنِی مَعَكُمَاۤ أَسۡمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ
ar_simple: قال لا تخافا إننی معكما أسمع وأرى

t.sahih-int: [Allah] said, "Fear not. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see
t.asad: Answered He: "Fear not! Verily, I shall be with you two, hearing and seeing [all]
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "Fear not: for I am with you: I hear and see (everything)
t.pickthall: He said: Fear not. Lo! I am with you twain, Hearing and Seeing
t.arberry: Fear not,' said He. 'Surely I shall be with you, hearing and seeing
t.hilali-khan: He (Allah) said: "Fear not, verily! I am with you both, hearing and seeing
t.itani: He said, “Do not fear, I am with you, I hear and I see.
t.mubarakpuri: He (Allah) said: "Fear not, verily, I am with you both, hearing and seeing."
t.qarib: God said, "Fear not. I am with you both; I hear and I see
t.wahiduddin: God said, "Do not fear; I am with you both. I hear and I see
t.shakir: He said: Fear not, surely I am with you both: I do hear and see
t.haleem: He said, ‘Do not be afraid, I am with you both, hearing and seeing everything
t.maariful: He said, “Do not be afraid. I AM surely with you both. I hear and I see
t.khattab: Allah reassured ˹them˺, “Have no fear! I am with you, hearing and seeing
t.ghali: Said He, "Do not fear (him). Surely I (Myself) will be with you (both); I hear and I see
t.irving: He said: "Do not fear; I am with you both. I both hear and see
t.iwq: Allah said, "Fear not. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see

t_id.kemenag: Allah berfirman: "Janganlah kamu berdua khawatir, sesungguhnya Aku beserta kamu berdua, Aku mendengar dan melihat".
t_id.sabiq: Allah menenangkan mereka berdua dengan berfirman, "Janganlah kalian berdua takut kepada Fir'aun. Aku selalu menyertai dan menjaga kalian. Aku Maha Mendengar apa yang ia katakan dan Maha Melihat apa yang ia perbuat. Dan Aku tidak akan memberinya kekuasaan untuk menyiksa kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Allah berfirman, "Janganlah kamu berdua takut sesungguhnya Aku beserta kamu berdua) Aku akan membantu kamu berdua (Aku mendengar) apa yang dikatakannya (dan melihat) apa yang dikerjakannya.
t_id.iwq: Allah berfirman: "Janganlah kamu berdua khawatir, sesungguhnya Aku beserta kamu berdua, Aku mendengar dan melihat".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال لا تخافا إنني معكما» بعوني «أسمع» ما يقول «وأرى» ما يفعل
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ لَا تَخَافَآۖ إِنَّنِي مَعَكُمَآ أَسۡمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Allah berfirman, "Janganlah kamu berdua takut sesungguhnya Aku beserta kamu berdua) Aku akan membantu kamu berdua (Aku mendengar) apa yang dikatakannya (dan melihat) apa yang dikerjakannya

ar: فَأۡتِیَاهُ فَقُولَاۤ إِنَّا رَسُولَا رَبِّكَ فَأَرۡسِلۡ مَعَنَا بَنِیۤ إِسۡرَ ٰۤءِیلَ وَلَا تُعَذِّبۡهُمۡۖ قَدۡ جِئۡنَـٰكَ بِءَایَةࣲ مِّن رَّبِّكَۖ وَٱلسَّلَـٰمُ عَلَىٰ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلۡهُدَىٰۤ
ar_simple: فأتیاه فقولا إنا رسولا ربك فأرسل معنا بنی إسر ءیل ولا تعذبهم قد جئنك بءایة من ربك وٱلسلم على من ٱتبع ٱلهدى

t.sahih-int: So go to him and say, 'Indeed, we are messengers of your Lord, so send with us the Children of Israel and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace will be upon he who follows the guidance
t.asad: Go, then; you two unto him and say, `Behold, we are apostles sent by thy Sustainer: let, then, the children of Israel go with us, and cause them not to suffer [any longer]. We have now come unto thee with a message from thy Sustainer; and [know that His] peace shall be [only] on those who follow [His] guidance
t.yusuf-ali: So go ye both to him, and say, 'Verily we are messengers sent by thy Lord: Send forth, therefore, the Children of Israel with us, and afflict them not: with a Sign, indeed, have we come from thy Lord! and peace to all who follow guidance
t.pickthall: So go ye unto him and say: Lo! we are two messengers of thy Lord. So let the children of Israel go with us, and torment them not. We bring thee a token from thy Lord. And peace will be for him who followeth right guidance
t.arberry: So go you both to Pharaoh, and say, "We are the Messengers of thy Lord, so send forth with us the Children of Israel and chastise them not; we have brought thee a sign from thy Lord; and peace be upon him who follows the guidance
t.hilali-khan: So go you both to him, and say: 'Verily, we are Messengers of your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with us, and torment them not; indeed, we have come with a sign from your Lord! And peace will be upon him who follows the guidance
t.itani: Approach him and say, `We are the messengers of your Lord; so let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not torment them. We bring you a sign from your Lord, and peace be upon him who follows guidance.
t.mubarakpuri: "So go you both to him, and say: 'Verily, we are both Messengers of your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with us, and torment them not; indeed, we have come with a sign from your Lord! And peace will be upon him who follows the guidance!"
t.qarib: Go to him and tell him: "We are messengers of your Lord, so send the Children of Israel with us, and do not punish them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace will be upon him who follows His guidance
t.wahiduddin: Go to him and say, 'We are both messengers from your Lord. Let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not oppress them. We have brought you a Sign from your Lord, and may peace be upon whoever follows the right guidance
t.shakir: So go you both to him and say: Surely we are two apostles of your Lord; therefore send the children of Israel with us and do not torment them! Indeed we have brought to you a communication from your Lord, and peace is on him who follows the guidance
t.haleem: Go and tell him, “We are your Lord’s mes-sengers, so send the Children of Israel with us and do not oppress them. We have brought you a sign from your Lord. Peace be upon whoever follows the right guidance
t.maariful: So, come to him and say, ‘We are the messengers of your Lord. So, let the children of Isrā’īl go with us, and do not persecute them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord; and peace be upon the one who follows the guidance
t.khattab: So go to him and say, ‘Indeed we are both messengers from your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not oppress them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And salvation will be for whoever follows the ˹right˺ guidance
t.ghali: So come you up (both) to him, then say, ‘Surely we are (both) the Messengers of your Lord; so send (forth) with us the Seeds (Or: sons) of Israel) and do not torment them; we have already come to you with a sign from your Lord; and peace be upon him who closely follows the guidance!’
t.irving: Go to him and say: "We are both messengers from your Lord. Send the Children of Israel away with us and do not torment them. We have brought you a sign from your Lord, and may "Peace be upon whoever follows guidance
t.iwq: Thus go to him and say, 'Indeed, we are messengers of your Lord, so send with us the Children of Israel and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace will be upon he who follows the guidance

t_id.kemenag: Maka datanglah kamu berdua kepadanya (Fir'aun) dan katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya kami berdua adalah utusan Tuhanmu, maka lepaskanlah Bani Israil bersama kami dan janganlah kamu menyiksa mereka. Sesungguhnya kami telah datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti (atas kerasulan kami) dari Tuhanmu. Dan keselamatan itu dilimpahkan kepada orang yang mengikuti petunjuk.
t_id.sabiq: Pergilah kalian berdua kepada Fir'aun dan katakan kepadanya, 'Sesunguhnya kami berdua adalah utusan Tuhanmu. Kami datang untuk mengajakmu beriman kepada-Nya, dan melepaskan Banû Isrâ'îl serta tidak menyiksa mereka. Kami membawa mukjizat dari Allah sebagai bukti kebenaran apa yang kami serukan kepadamu. Barangsiapa mengikuti petunjuk Allah, maka ia akan terjaga dari siksa dan murka-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka datanglah kamu berdua kepadanya dan katakanlah, " Sesungguhnya kami berdua adalah utusan Rabbmu, maka lepaskanlah Bani Israel bersama kami) untuk berangkat ke negeri Syam (dan janganlah kamu menyiksa mereka) lepaskanlah mereka dari perbudakanmu yang telah kamu pekerjakan dengan kerja-kerja yang berat seperti menggali, membangun bangunan dan mengangkat barang-barang yang berat. (Sesungguhnya kami telah datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti) yakni hujah (dari Rabbmu) yang membenarkan kerasulan kami. (Dan keselamatan itu dilimpahkan kepada orang yang mengikuti petunjuk) keselamatan dari azab bagi orang yang mengikutinya.
t_id.iwq: Maka datanglah kamu berdua kepadanya (Fir'aun) dan katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya kami berdua adalah utusan Tuhanmu, maka lepaskanlah Bani Israil bersama kami dan janganlah kamu menyiksa mereka. Sesungguhnya kami telah datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti (atas kerasulan kami) dari Tuhanmu. Dan keselamatan itu dilimpahkan kepada orang yang mengikuti petunjuk.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأتياه فقولا إنَّا رسولا ربك فأرسل معنا بني إسرائيل» إلى الشام «ولا تعذبهم» أي خل عنهم من استعمالك إياهم في أشغالك الشاقة كالحفر والبناء وحمل الثقيل «قد جئناك بآية» بحجة «من ربك» على صدقنا بالرسالة «والسلام على من اتبع الهدى» أي السلامة له من العذاب
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأۡتِيَاهُ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولَا رَبِّكَ فَأَرۡسِلۡ مَعَنَا بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ وَلَا تُعَذِّبۡهُمۡۖ قَدۡ جِئۡنَٰكَ بِـَٔايَةٖ مِّن رَّبِّكَۖ وَٱلسَّلَٰمُ عَلَىٰ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلۡهُدَىٰٓ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka datanglah kamu berdua kepadanya dan katakanlah, " Sesungguhnya kami berdua adalah utusan Rabbmu, maka lepaskanlah Bani Israel bersama kami) untuk berangkat ke negeri Syam (dan janganlah kamu menyiksa mereka) lepaskanlah mereka dari perbudakanmu yang telah kamu pekerjakan dengan kerja-kerja yang berat seperti menggali, membangun bangunan dan mengangkat barang-barang yang berat. (Sesungguhnya kami telah datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti) yakni hujah (dari Rabbmu) yang membenarkan kerasulan kami. (Dan keselamatan itu dilimpahkan kepada orang yang mengikuti petunjuk) keselamatan dari azab bagi orang yang mengikutinya

ar: إِنَّا قَدۡ أُوحِیَ إِلَیۡنَاۤ أَنَّ ٱلۡعَذَابَ عَلَىٰ مَن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ
ar_simple: إنا قد أوحی إلینا أن ٱلعذاب على من كذب وتولى

t.sahih-int: Indeed, it has been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies and turns away
t.asad: for, behold, it has been revealed to us that [in the life to come] suffering shall befall all who give the lie to the truth and turn away [from it]
t.yusuf-ali: Verily it has been revealed to us that the Penalty (awaits) those who reject and turn away
t.pickthall: Lo! it hath been revealed unto us that the doom will be for him who denieth and turneth away
t.arberry: It has been revealed to us that chastisement shall light upon him who cries lies and turns his back
t.hilali-khan: Truly, it has been revealed to us that the torment will be for him who denies [believes not in the Oneness of Allah, and in His Messengers, etc.], and turns away.'(from the truth and obedience of Allah)
t.itani: It was revealed to us that the punishment falls upon him who disbelieves and turns away.'“
t.mubarakpuri: `Truly, it has been revealed to us that the torment will be for him who denies, and turns away.`"
t.qarib: It has been revealed to us that punishment will come upon whoever rejects the truth and turns away
t.wahiduddin: it has been revealed to us that punishment shall overtake him who rejects it and turns away
t.shakir: Surely it has been revealed to us that the chastisement will surely come upon him who rejects and turns back
t.haleem: it has been revealed to us that punishment falls on whoever rejects the truth and turns his back on it.”’
t.maariful: Verily, it has been revealed to us that the punishment is for the one who denies and turns away.’”
t.khattab: It has indeed been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies ˹the truth˺ and turns away.’”
t.ghali: Surely it has already been revealed to us that the torment shall be for him who cries lies and turns away
t.irving: As for us, it has been revealed to us that torment is due anyone who says it is a lie and turns away!´
t.iwq: Indeed, it has been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies and turns away

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya telah diwahyukan kepada kami bahwa siksa itu (ditimpakan) atas orang-orang yang mendustakan dan berpaling.
t_id.sabiq: Allah telah mewahyukan kepada kami, bahwa azab-Nya yang amat pedih akan menimpa siapa yang mendustakan kami dan menentang seruan kami'."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya telah diwahyukan kepada kami, bahwa siksa itu ditimpakan kepada orang-orang yang mendustakan) apa yang kami datangkan ini (dan berpaling") daripadanya. Kemudian Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun mendatangi Firaun dan keduanya mengatakan semuanya itu kepadanya.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya telah diwahyukan kepada kami bahwa siksa itu (ditimpakan) atas orang-orang yang mendustakan dan berpaling.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا قد أوحي إلينا أن العذاب على مَن كذب» ما جئنا له «وتولى» أعرض عنه، فأتياه وقالا جميع ما ذكر
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّا قَدۡ أُوحِيَ إِلَيۡنَآ أَنَّ ٱلۡعَذَابَ عَلَىٰ مَن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya telah diwahyukan kepada kami, bahwa siksa itu ditimpakan kepada orang-orang yang mendustakan) apa yang kami datangkan ini (dan berpaling") daripadanya. Kemudian Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun mendatangi Firaun dan keduanya mengatakan semuanya itu kepadanya

ar: قَالَ فَمَن رَّبُّكُمَا یَـٰمُوسَىٰ
ar_simple: قال فمن ربكما یموسى

t.sahih-int: [Pharaoh] said, "So who is the Lord of you two, O Moses
t.asad: [But when God's message was conveyed unto Pharaoh,] he said: "Who, now, is this Sustainer of you two, O Moses
t.yusuf-ali: (When this message was delivered), (Pharaoh) said: "Who, then, O Moses, is the Lord of you two
t.pickthall: (Pharaoh) said: Who then is the Lord of you twain, O Moses
t.arberry: Pharaoh said, 'Who is your Lord, Moses
t.hilali-khan: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "Who then, O Musa (Moses), is the Lord of you two
t.itani: He said, “Who is your Lord, O Moses.”
t.mubarakpuri: Fir`awn said: "Who then, O Musa, is the Lord of you two"
t.qarib: [Pharaoh] said, "So who is the Lord of you two, Moses
t.wahiduddin: Pharaoh said, "Who then is the Lord of you both, Moses
t.shakir: (Firon) said: And who is your Lord, O Musa
t.haleem: [Pharaoh] said, ‘Moses, who is this Lord of yours?’
t.maariful: He (Pharaoh) said, “Who then is the Lord of you two, O Mūsā?”
t.khattab: Pharaoh asked, “Who then is the Lord of you two, O Moses?”
t.ghali: He (Firaawn) (Pharaoh) said, "Who then is the Lord of both of you, O Musa?" (Moses)
t.irving: He said; "Who is Lord for both of you, Moses
t.iwq: Pharaoh said, "So who is the Lord of you two, O Moses

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Fir'aun: "Maka siapakah Tuhanmu berdua, hai Musa?.
t_id.sabiq: Dengan zalim dan sombong, Fir'aun berkata, "Siapakah Tuhan kalian itu, hai Mûsâ?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Firaun, "Maka siapakah Rabbmu berdua, hai Musa?") ungkapan ini ditujukan kepada Nabi Musa, karena dialah asal pembawa risalah Allah dan yang mendapatkan pemeliharaan dari-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Fir'aun: "Maka siapakah Tuhanmu berdua, hai Musa?.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال فمن ربكما يا موسى» اقتصر عليه لأنه الأصل ولإدلاله عليه بالتربية
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ فَمَن رَّبُّكُمَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Firaun, "Maka siapakah Rabbmu berdua, hai Musa?") ungkapan ini ditujukan kepada Nabi Musa, karena dialah asal pembawa risalah Allah dan yang mendapatkan pemeliharaan dari-Nya

ar: قَالَ رَبُّنَا ٱلَّذِیۤ أَعۡطَىٰ كُلَّ شَیۡءٍ خَلۡقَهُۥ ثُمَّ هَدَىٰ
ar_simple: قال ربنا ٱلذی أعطى كل شیء خلقه ثم هدى

t.sahih-int: He said, "Our Lord is He who gave each thing its form and then guided [it]
t.asad: He replied: "Our Sustainer is He who gives unto every thing [that exists] its true nature and form. and thereupon guides it [towards its fulfilment]
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "Our Lord is He Who gave to each (created) thing its form and nature, and further, gave (it) guidance
t.pickthall: He said: Our Lord is He Who gave unto everything its nature, then guided it aright
t.arberry: He said, 'Our Lord is He who gave everything its creation, then guided it
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses)] said: "Our Lord is He Who gave to each thing its form and nature, then guided it aright
t.itani: He said, “Our Lord is He who gave everything its existence, then guided it.”
t.mubarakpuri: [Musa] said: "Our Lord is He Who gave to each thing its form and nature, then guided it aright."
t.qarib: He said, "Our Lord is He who gave each thing its form and then guided [it]
t.wahiduddin: Moses replied, "Our Lord is He who has given everything its form, then guided it
t.shakir: He said: Our Lord is He Who gave to everything its creation, then guided it (to its goal)
t.haleem: Moses said, ‘Our Lord is He who gave everything its form, then gave it guidance.’
t.maariful: He (Mūsā) said, “Our Lord is the One who gave everything its due shape, then guided it.”
t.khattab: He answered, “Our Lord is the One Who has given everything its ˹distinctive˺ form, then guided ˹it˺.”
t.ghali: He said, "Our Lord is He Who gave everything its creation (and) thereafter guided (it)
t.irving: He said: "Our Lord is the One Who has given everything its own constitution; then guided it
t.iwq: He said, "Our Lord is He who gave each thing its form and then guided it

t_id.kemenag: Musa berkata: "Tuhan kami ialah (Tuhan) yang telah memberikan kepada tiap-tiap sesuatu bentuk kejadiannya, kemudian memberinya petunjuk.
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ menjawab, "Tuhan kami adalah Tuhan yang memberi nikmat kejadian bagi setiap sesuatu yang ada, dan menciptakannya dalam bentuk yang dikehendaki-Nya, lalu memberi petunjuk apa yang telah diciptakan-Nya(1). (1) Setiap makhluk memiliki ciri-ciri dan sifatsifat khusus yang membedakannya dari makhluk lain, yang memungkinkannya menjalankan fungsinya masing-masing di dalam kehidupan ini, di samping merupakan salah satu jalan untuk memperoleh hidayah bagi manusia.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Musa berkata, "Rabb kami ialah yang telah memberikan kepada tiap-tiap sesuatu) yakni tiap-tiap makhluk (bentuk kejadiannya) yang membedakannya daripada makhluk yang lain (kemudian memberinya petunjuk") sehingga mengetahui makanan, minuman dan cara mengembangkan keturunannya serta hal-hal lain yang menyangkut kehidupannya.
t_id.iwq: Musa berkata: "Tuhan kami ialah (Tuhan) yang telah memberikan kepada tiap-tiap sesuatu bentuk kejadiannya, kemudian memberinya petunjuk.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال ربنا الذي أعطى كل شيء» من الخلق «خلقه» الذي هو عليه متميز به عن غيره «ثم هدى» الحيوان منه إلى مطعمه ومشربه ومنكحه وغير ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ رَبُّنَا ٱلَّذِيٓ أَعۡطَىٰ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ خَلۡقَهُۥ ثُمَّ هَدَىٰ (خَلْقَهُ: صُورَتَهُ اللَّائِقَةَ بِخَاصَّتِهِ وَمَنْفَعَتِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Musa berkata, "Rabb kami ialah yang telah memberikan kepada tiap-tiap sesuatu) yakni tiap-tiap makhluk (bentuk kejadiannya) yang membedakannya daripada makhluk yang lain (kemudian memberinya petunjuk") sehingga mengetahui makanan, minuman dan cara mengembangkan keturunannya serta hal-hal lain yang menyangkut kehidupannya

ar: قَالَ فَمَا بَالُ ٱلۡقُرُونِ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
ar_simple: قال فما بال ٱلقرون ٱلأولى

t.sahih-int: [Pharaoh] said, "Then what is the case of the former generations
t.asad: Said [Pharaoh]: "And what of all the past generations
t.yusuf-ali: (Pharaoh) said: "What then is the condition of previous generations
t.pickthall: He said: What then is the state of the generations of old
t.arberry: Pharaoh said, 'And what of the former generations
t.hilali-khan: Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said: "What about the generations of old
t.itani: He said, “What about the first generations?”
t.mubarakpuri: [Fir`awn] said: "What about the generations of old"
t.qarib: [Pharaoh] said, "Then what about the former generations
t.wahiduddin: Pharaoh asked, "What about the previous generations
t.shakir: He said: Then what is the state of the former generations
t.haleem: He said, ‘What about former generations?’
t.maariful: He said, “Then, what is the status of the earlier generations?”
t.khattab: Pharaoh asked, “And what about previous peoples?”
t.ghali: He said, "Then what about the earliest generations
t.irving: He said: "So what was the attitude during earlier centuries
t.iwq: Pharaoh said, "Then what is the case of the former generations

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Fir'aun: "Maka bagaimanakah keadaan umat-umat yang dahulu?"
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun berkata, "Lalu bagaimana dengan orang-orang yang telah lalu?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah ia) yakni Firaun, ("Maka bagaimanakah) keadaan (umat-umat) yakni bangsa-bangsa (yang dahulu?") seperti kaum Nabi Nuh, kaum Nabi Hud, kaum Nabi Luth dan kaum Nabi Saleh, tentang penyembahan mereka kepada berhala-berhala.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Fir'aun: "Maka bagaimanakah keadaan umat-umat yang dahulu?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» فرعون «فما بال» حال «القرون» الأمم «الأولى» كقوم نوح وهود ولوط وصالح في عبادتهم الأوثان
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ فَمَا بَالُ ٱلۡقُرُونِ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ (الْقُرُونِ الْأُولَى: الأُمَمِ المَاضِيَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah ia) yakni Firaun, ("Maka bagaimanakah) keadaan (umat-umat) yakni bangsa-bangsa (yang dahulu?") seperti kaum Nabi Nuh, kaum Nabi Hud, kaum Nabi Luth dan kaum Nabi Saleh, tentang penyembahan mereka kepada berhala-berhala

ar: قَالَ عِلۡمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّی فِی كِتَـٰبࣲۖ لَّا یَضِلُّ رَبِّی وَلَا یَنسَى
ar_simple: قال علمها عند ربی فی كتب لا یضل ربی ولا ینسى

t.sahih-int: [Moses] said, "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets
t.asad: [Moses] answered: "Knowledge thereof rests with my Sustainer [alone, and is laid down] in His decree; my Sustainer does not err, and neither does He forget
t.yusuf-ali: He replied: "The knowledge of that is with my Lord, duly recorded: my Lord never errs, nor forgets
t.pickthall: He said: The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a Record. My Lord neither erreth nor forgetteth
t.arberry: Said Moses, 'The knowledge of them is with my Lord, in a Book; my Lord goes not astray, nor forgets
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses)] said: "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord, in a Record. My Lord is neither unaware nor He forgets
t.itani: He said, “Knowledge thereof is with my Lord, in a Book. My Lord never errs, nor does He forget.”
t.mubarakpuri: [Musa] said: "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord, in a Record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets."
t.qarib: He said, "Their knowledge is with my Lord in a record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets
t.wahiduddin: Moses said, "My Lord alone has knowledge of that, recorded in a Book. My Lord neither errs nor forgets
t.shakir: He said: The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a book, my Lord errs not, nor does He forget
t.haleem: Moses said, ‘My Lord alone has knowledge of them, all in a record; my Lord does not err or forget.’
t.maariful: He replied, “The knowledge about those is with my Lord in a Book. My Lord does neither err nor forget.”
t.khattab: He replied, “That knowledge is with my Lord in a Record. My Lord neither falters nor forgets ˹anything˺.”
t.ghali: He said, "The knowledge of them is in the Providence of my Lord, in a Book; my Lord does not err, nor does He forget
t.irving: He said: "Knowledge concerning them rests in a Book with my Lord. My Lord neither misses anything, nor does He forget
t.iwq: Moses said, "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets

t_id.kemenag: Musa menjawab: "Pengetahuan tentang itu ada di sisi Tuhanku, di dalam sebuah kitab, Tuhan kami tidak akan salah dan tidak (pula) lupa;
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ menjawab, "Hanya Tuhan yang tahu tentang mereka. Semuanya tercatat dalam buku catatan amal mereka. Tak ada yang tersembunyi dan terlupakan sedikit pun.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ia berkata) yakni Nabi Musa, ("Pengetahuan tentang itu) pengetahuan mengenai keadaan mereka berada (di sisi Rabbku, di dalam sebuah kitab) yaitu Lohmahfuz; Dia akan membalas mereka kelak di hari kiamat. (Tidak akan salah) tidak akan lenyap (dari Rabbku) segala sesuatu (dan tidak pula lupa) akan sesuatu.
t_id.iwq: Musa menjawab: "Pengetahuan tentang itu ada di sisi Tuhanku, di dalam sebuah kitab, Tuhan kami tidak akan salat dan tidak (pula) lupa;

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» موسى «علمها» أي علم حالهم محفوظ «عند ربي في كتاب» هو اللوح المحفوظ يجازيهم عليها يوم القيامة «لا يضل» يغيب «ربي» عن شيء «ولا ينسى» ربي شيئا
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ عِلۡمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّي فِي كِتَٰبٖۖ لَّا يَضِلُّ رَبِّي وَلَا يَنسَى
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ia berkata) yakni Nabi Musa, ("Pengetahuan tentang itu) pengetahuan mengenai keadaan mereka berada (di sisi Rabbku, di dalam sebuah kitab) yaitu Lohmahfuz; Dia akan membalas mereka kelak di hari kiamat. (Tidak akan salah) tidak akan lenyap (dari Rabbku) segala sesuatu (dan tidak pula lupa) akan sesuatu

ar: ٱلَّذِی جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَهۡدࣰا وَسَلَكَ لَكُمۡ فِیهَا سُبُلࣰا وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَاۤءِ مَاۤءࣰ فَأَخۡرَجۡنَا بِهِۦۤ أَزۡوَ ٰجࣰا مِّن نَّبَاتࣲ شَتَّىٰ
ar_simple: ٱلذی جعل لكم ٱلأرض مهدا وسلك لكم فیها سبلا وأنزل من ٱلسماء ماء فأخرجنا به أزو جا من نبات شتى

t.sahih-int: [It is He] who has made for you the earth as a bed [spread out] and inserted therein for you roadways and sent down from the sky, rain and produced thereby categories of various plants
t.asad: HE IT IS who has made the earth a cradle for you, and has traced out for you ways [of livelihood] thereon, and [who] sends down waters from the sky: and by this means We bring forth various kinds of plants
t.yusuf-ali: He Who has, made for you the earth like a carpet spread out; has enabled you to go about therein by roads (and channels); and has sent down water from the sky." With it have We produced diverse pairs of plants each separate from the others
t.pickthall: Who hath appointed the earth as a bed and hath threaded roads for you therein and hath sent down water from the sky and thereby We have brought forth divers kinds of vegetation
t.arberry: He who appointed the earth to be a cradle for you, and therein threaded roads for you, and sent down water out of heaven, and therewith We have brought forth divers kinds of plants
t.hilali-khan: Who has made earth for you like a bed (spread out); and has opened roads (ways and paths etc.) for you therein; and has sent down water (rain) from the sky. And We have brought forth with it various kinds of vegetation
t.itani: He who made the earth a habitat for you; and traced in it routes for you; and sent down water from the sky, with which We produce pairs of diverse plants.
t.mubarakpuri: Who has made earth for you like a bed; and has opened ways for you therein, and has sent down water (rain) from the sky. And We have brought forth with it various kinds of vegetation.
t.qarib: It is He who has made the earth as a cradle for you and traced pathways for you through it and sent down rain from the sky, with which We bring forth various kinds of plants
t.wahiduddin: It is He who has laid out the earth for you and traced routes in it and sent down water from the sky. We have brought forth every sort of plant with it
t.shakir: Who made the earth for you an expanse and made for you therein paths and sent down water from the cloud; then thereby We have brought forth many species of various herbs
t.haleem: It was He who spread out the earth for you and traced routes in it. He sent down water from the sky. With that water We bring forth every kind of plant
t.maariful: (He is) the One who made the earth a cradle for you, and made in it pathways for you to move, and sent down water from the heavens; and We brought forth with it pairs of different vegetations
t.khattab: ˹He is the One˺ Who has laid out the earth for ˹all of˺ you, and set in it pathways for you, and sends down rain from the sky, causing various types of plants to grow
t.ghali: He who has made the earth (as) a cradle for you, and inserted ways therein for you, and sent down water from the heaven; so We have brought out therewith pairs of divers (kinds of) growth
t.irving: [since he is the One] Who has laid out the earth as a carpet for you and has traced highways on it for you, and sent down water from the sky, We have brought forth every sort of plant with it, of various types
t.iwq: It is He who has made for you the earth as a bed spread out and inserted therein for you roadways and sent down from the sky, rain and produced thereby categories of various plants

t_id.kemenag: Yang telah menjadikan bagimu bumi sebagai hamparan dan Yang telah menjadikan bagimu di bumi itu jalan-ja]an, dan menurunkan dari langit air hujan. Maka Kami tumbuhkan dengan air hujan itu berjenis-jenis dari tumbuh-tumbuhan yang bermacam-macam.
t_id.sabiq: Dialah Tuhan yang menganugerahkan nikmat kehidupan dan pemeliharaan kepada hamba-hamba-Nya. Dengan kekuasaan-Nya, Dia telah menjadikan bumi sebagai hamparan untukmu, membuka jalan-jalan untuk kamu lalui dan menurunkan hujan di atas bumi sehingga terciptalah sungai-sungai. Dengan air itu Allah menumbuhkan tumbuh-tumbuhan yang berbeda-beda warna, rasa dan manfaatnya. Ada yang berwarna putih dan hitam, ada pula yang rasanya manis dan pahit.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Dia (yang telah menjadikan bagi kalian) di antara sekian banyak makhluk-Nya (bumi sebagai hamparan) tempat berpijak (dan Dia memudahkan) mempermudah (bagi kalian di bumi itu jalan-jalan) tempat-tempat untuk berjalan (dan Dia menurunkan dari langit air hujan) yakni merupakan hujan. Allah berfirman menggambarkan apa yang telah disebutkan-Nya itu sebagai nikmat dari-Nya, kepada Nabi Musa dan dianggap sebagai khithab untuk penduduk Mekah. (Maka Kami tumbuhkan dengan air hujan itu berjenis-jenis) bermacam-macam (tumbuh-tumbuhan yang beraneka ragam). Lafal Syattaa ini menjadi kata sifat daripada lafal Azwaajan, maksudnya, yang berbeda-beda warna dan rasa serta lain-lainnya. Lafal syattaa ini adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Syatiitun, wazannya sama dengan lafal Mardhaa sebagai jamak dari lafal Mariidhun. Ia berasal dari kata kerja Syatta artinya Tafarraqa atau berbeda-beda.
t_id.iwq: Yang telah menjadikan bagimu bumi sebagai hamparan dan Yang telah menjadikan bagimu di bumi itu jalan-ja]an, dan menurunkan dari langit air hujan. Maka Kami tumbuhkan dengan air hujan itu berjenis-jenis dari tumbuh-tumbuhan yang bermacam-macam.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: هو «الذي جعل لكم» في جملة الخلق «الأرض مهادا» فراشا «وسلك» سهل «لكم فيها سبلا» طرقا «وأنزل من السماء ماءً» مطرا قال تعالى تتميما لما وصفه به موسى وخطابا لأهل مكة «فأخرجنا به أزواجا» أصنافا «من نبات شتى» صفة أزواجا أي مختلفة الألوان والطعوم وغيرهما، وشتى جمع شتيت كمريض ومرضى، من شت الأمر تفرق
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَهۡدٗا وَسَلَكَ لَكُمۡ فِيهَا سُبُلٗا وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ فَأَخۡرَجۡنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٗا مِّن نَّبَاتٖ شَتَّىٰ (مَهْدًا: مُيَسَّرَةً لِلِانْتِفَاعِ بِهَا, سُبُلًا: طُرُقًا, أَزْوَاجًا: أَنْوَاعًا مُخْتَلِفَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dia (yang telah menjadikan bagi kalian) di antara sekian banyak makhluk-Nya (bumi sebagai hamparan) tempat berpijak (dan Dia memudahkan) mempermudah (bagi kalian di bumi itu jalan-jalan) tempat-tempat untuk berjalan (dan Dia menurunkan dari langit air hujan) yakni merupakan hujan. Allah berfirman menggambarkan apa yang telah disebutkan-Nya itu sebagai nikmat dari-Nya, kepada Nabi Musa dan dianggap sebagai khithab untuk penduduk Mekah. (Maka Kami tumbuhkan dengan air hujan itu berjenis-jenis) bermacam-macam (tumbuh-tumbuhan yang beraneka ragam). Lafal Syattaa ini menjadi kata sifat daripada lafal Azwaajan, maksudnya, yang berbeda-beda warna dan rasa serta lain-lainnya. Lafal syattaa ini adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Syatiitun, wazannya sama dengan lafal Mardhaa sebagai jamak dari lafal Mariidhun. Ia berasal dari kata kerja Syatta artinya Tafarraqa atau berbeda-beda

ar: كُلُوا۟ وَٱرۡعَوۡا۟ أَنۡعَـٰمَكُمۡۚ إِنَّ فِی ذَ ٰلِكَ لَءَایَـٰتࣲ لِّأُو۟لِی ٱلنُّهَىٰ
ar_simple: كلوا وٱرعوا أنعمكم إن فی ذ لك لءایت لأولی ٱلنهى

t.sahih-int: Eat [therefrom] and pasture your livestock. Indeed, in that are signs for those of intelligence
t.asad: Eat, [then, of this produce of the soil,] and pasture your cattle [thereon]. In all this, behold, there are messages indeed for those who are endowed with reason
t.yusuf-ali: Eat (for yourselves) and pasture your cattle: verily, in this are Signs for men endued with understanding
t.pickthall: (Saying): Eat ye and feed your cattle. Lo! herein verily are portents for men of thought
t.arberry: Do you eat, and pasture your cattle! Surely in that are signs for men possessing reason
t.hilali-khan: Eat and pasture your cattle, (therein); verily, in this are proofs and signs for men of understanding
t.itani: Eat and pasture your livestock. In that are signs for those with understanding.
t.mubarakpuri: Eat and pasture your cattle; verily, in this are Ayat for men of understanding.
t.qarib: to eat as well as pasture for your cattle. In this there are signs for intelligent beings
t.wahiduddin: so eat and graze your cattle. In this there are signs for men of understanding
t.shakir: Eat and pasture your cattle; most surely there are signs in this for those endowed with understanding
t.haleem: so eat, and graze your cattle. There are truly signs in all this for people of understand-ing
t.maariful: Eat, and graze your cattle. Surely, in that there are signs for the people of understanding
t.khattab: ˹so˺ eat and graze your cattle. Surely in this are signs for people of sound judgment
t.ghali: Eat (thereof), and pasture your cattle! Surely in that are indeed signs for ones endowed with intelligence
t.irving: Eat and pasture your livestock [on it]; in that are signs for men of accomplishment
t.iwq: Eat therefrom and pasture your livestock. Indeed, in that are signs for those of intelligence

t_id.kemenag: Makanlah dan gembalakanlah binatang-binatangmu. Sesungguhnya pada yang demikian itu, terdapat tanda-tanda kekuasaan Allah bagi orang-orang yang berakal.
t_id.sabiq: Allah memberi petunjuk kepada para hamba-Nya cara memanfaatkan tumbuh-tumbuhan yang telah ditumbuhkan-Nya. Yaitu dengan memakan, menggembalakan binatang dan lain-lain. Allah menjelaskan bahwa di dalam penciptaan makhluk, pengaturan dan cara memanfaatkannya terdapat bukti-bukti nyata yang dapat dijadikan petunjuk bagi orang-orang yang berakal untuk beriman kepada Allah dan risalah- risalah-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Makanlah) daripadanya (dan gembalakanlah ternak kalian) di dalamnya. Lafal An'am adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Ni'amun, yang artinya mencakup unta, sapi dan kambing. Dikatakan, Ru'tul An'aama atau aku menggembalakan ternak dan Ra'aituhaa atau aku telah menggembalakannya. Pengertian yang terkandung di dalam perintah ini menunjukkan makna ibahah atau boleh dan sekaligus sebagai pengingat akan nikmat-nikmat-Nya. Jumlah keseluruhan ayat ini menjadi kata keterangan keadaan daripada Dhamir yang terkandung di dalam lafal Akhrajnaa. Maksudnya, Kami memperbolehkan bagi kalian untuk memakannya dan mengembalakan ternak padanya. (sesungguhnya pada yang demikian itu) yakni pada hal-hal yang telah disebutkan dalam ayat ini (terdapat tanda-tanda) pelajaran-pelajaran (bagi orang-orang yang berakal) lafal Nuhaa adalah bentuk jamak dan lafal Nuhyah, wazannya sama dengan lafal Ghurfah yang jamaknya Ghuraf. Akal dinamakan dengan istilah ini, karena dapat mencegah pemiliknya dari melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan yang buruk.
t_id.iwq: Makanlah dan gembalakanlah binatang-binatangmu. Sesungguhnya pada yang demikian itu, terdapat tanda-tanda kekuasaan Allah bagi orang-orang yang berakal.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كلوا» منها «وارعوا أنعامكم» فيها جمع نعم، وهي الإبل والبقر والغنم، يقال رعت الأنعام ورعيتها والأمر للإباحة وتذكير النعمة والجملة حال من ضمير أخرجنا، أي مبيحين لكم الأكل ورعي الأنعام «إن في ذلك» المذكور «لآيات» لعبرا «لأولي النهى» لأصحاب العقول ينهى صاحبه عن ارتكاب القبائح
tafsir.siraj-ar: كُلُواْ وَٱرۡعَوۡاْ أَنۡعَٰمَكُمۡۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ لِّأُوْلِي ٱلنُّهَىٰ (لِّأُوْلِي النُّهَى: لِذَوِي العُقُولِ السَّلِيمَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Makanlah) daripadanya (dan gembalakanlah ternak kalian) di dalamnya. Lafal An'am adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Ni'amun, yang artinya mencakup unta, sapi dan kambing. Dikatakan, Ru'tul An'aama atau aku menggembalakan ternak dan Ra'aituhaa atau aku telah menggembalakannya. Pengertian yang terkandung di dalam perintah ini menunjukkan makna ibahah atau boleh dan sekaligus sebagai pengingat akan nikmat-nikmat-Nya. Jumlah keseluruhan ayat ini menjadi kata keterangan keadaan daripada Dhamir yang terkandung di dalam lafal Akhrajnaa. Maksudnya, Kami memperbolehkan bagi kalian untuk memakannya dan mengembalakan ternak padanya.
