(sesungguhnya pada yang demikian itu) yakni pada hal-hal yang telah disebutkan dalam ayat ini (terdapat tanda-tanda) pelajaran-pelajaran (bagi orang-orang yang berakal) lafal Nuhaa adalah bentuk jamak dan lafal Nuhyah, wazannya sama dengan lafal Ghurfah yang jamaknya Ghuraf. Akal dinamakan dengan istilah ini, karena dapat mencegah pemiliknya dari melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan yang buruk

ar: ۞ مِنۡهَا خَلَقۡنَـٰكُمۡ وَفِیهَا نُعِیدُكُمۡ وَمِنۡهَا نُخۡرِجُكُمۡ تَارَةً أُخۡرَىٰ
ar_simple: منها خلقنكم وفیها نعیدكم ومنها نخرجكم تارة أخرى

t.sahih-int: From the earth We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will extract you another time
t.asad: out of this [earth] have We created you, and into it shall We return you, and out of it shall We bring you forth once again
t.yusuf-ali: From the (earth) did We create you, and into it shall We return you, and from it shall We bring you out once again
t.pickthall: Thereof We created you, and thereunto We return you, and thence We bring you forth a second time
t.arberry: Out of the earth We created you, and We shall restore you into it, and bring you forth from it a second time
t.hilali-khan: Thereof (the earth) We created you, and into it We shall return you, and from it We shall bring you out once again
t.itani: From it We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will bring you out another time.
t.mubarakpuri: Thereof We created you, and into it We shall return you, and from it We shall bring you out once again.
t.qarib: From earth We created you, and to it We will return you, and from it We will bring you forth once again
t.wahiduddin: From the earth We have created you and We will return you to it, and from it We shall bring you forth a second time
t.shakir: From it We created you and into it We shall send you back and from it will We raise you a second time
t.haleem: From the earth We created you, into it We shall return you, and from it We shall raise you a second time
t.maariful: From this (dust) We created you, and in this we shall put you back, and from this We shall raise you up once again
t.khattab: From the earth We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will bring you back again
t.ghali: Thereof We created you, and We will bring you back into it, and We bring you out of it on another occasion (i.e., a second time)
t.irving: From it We have created you and We will return you to it, and from it shall We bring you forth for another chance
t.iwq: From the earth We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will extract you another time

t_id.kemenag: Dari bumi (tanah) itulah Kami menjadikan kamu dan kepadanya Kami akan mengembalikan kamu dan daripadanya Kami akan mengeluarkan kamu pada kali yang lain,
t_id.sabiq: Dari tanah bumi ini, Allah menciptakan Adam dan anak-cucunya. Ke dalam tanah itu pula Allah mengembalikan mereka setelah mati untuk mengubur jasad mereka. Dan dari tanah itu juga Allah mengeluarkan mereka hidup kembali untuk dibangkitkan dan diberi balasan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dari bumi itulah) dari tanah (Kami menjadikan kalian) dengan menciptakan nenek moyang kalian Adam daripadanya (dan kepadanya Kami akan mengembalikan kalian) kalian akan dikuburkan di dalamnya sesudah mati (dan daripadanya Kami akan mengeluarkan kalian) pada hari berbangkit (pada kali) untuk kali (yang lain) sebagaimana Kami mengeluarkan kalian pada permulaan penciptaan kalian.
t_id.iwq: Dari bumi (tanah) itulah Kami menjadikan kamu dan kepadanya Kami akan mengembalikan kamu dan daripadanya Kami akan mengeluarkan kamu pada kali yang lain,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «منها» أي من الأرض «خلقناكم» بخلق أبيكم آدم منها «وفيها نعيدكم» مقبورين بعد الموت «ومنها نخرجكم» عند البعث «تارة» مرة «أخرى» كما أخرجناكم عند ابتداء خلقكم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞مِنۡهَا خَلَقۡنَٰكُمۡ وَفِيهَا نُعِيدُكُمۡ وَمِنۡهَا نُخۡرِجُكُمۡ تَارَةً أُخۡرَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dari bumi itulah) dari tanah (Kami menjadikan kalian) dengan menciptakan nenek moyang kalian Adam daripadanya (dan kepadanya Kami akan mengembalikan kalian) kalian akan dikuburkan di dalamnya sesudah mati (dan daripadanya Kami akan mengeluarkan kalian) pada hari berbangkit (pada kali) untuk kali (yang lain) sebagaimana Kami mengeluarkan kalian pada permulaan penciptaan kalian

ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَرَیۡنَـٰهُ ءَایَـٰتِنَا كُلَّهَا فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبَىٰ
ar_simple: ولقد أرینه ءایتنا كلها فكذب وأبى

t.sahih-int: And We certainly showed Pharaoh Our signs - all of them - but he denied and refused
t.asad: AND, INDEED, We made Pharaoh aware of all Our messages-but he gave them the lie and refused [to heed them]
t.yusuf-ali: And We showed Pharaoh all Our Signs, but he did reject and refuse
t.pickthall: And We verily did show him all Our tokens, but he denied them and refused
t.arberry: So We showed Pharaoh all Our signs, but he cried lies, and refused
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We showed him [Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] all Our Signs and Evidences, but he denied and refused
t.itani: We showed him Our signs, all of them, but he denied and refused.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed We showed him (Fir`awn) all Our Ayat, but he denied and refused.
t.qarib: We certainly showed Pharaoh all of Our signs, but he denied [them] and refused [to believe]
t.wahiduddin: We showed Pharaoh all Our signs but he rejected them and refused to believe in them
t.shakir: And truly We showed him Our signs, all of them, but he rejected and refused
t.haleem: We showed Pharaoh all Our signs, but he denied them and refused [to change]
t.maariful: And We showed him (Pharaoh) all Our signs, yet he rejected and refused
t.khattab: And We certainly showed Pharaoh all of Our signs, but he denied them and refused ˹to believe˺
t.ghali: And indeed We already showed him (Firaawn) all Our signs, yet he cried lies and refused
t.irving: Yet We showed him all Our signs, and he said they were ties and rejected them
t.iwq: And We certainly showed Pharaoh Our signs - all of them - but he denied and refused

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah perlihatkan kepadanya (Fir'aun) tanda-tanda kekuasaan Kami semuanya maka ia mendustakan dan enggan (menerima kebenaran).
t_id.sabiq: Melalui tangan Mûsâ, Kami memperlihatkan kepada Fir'aun mukjizat nyata yang menguatkan kerasulan dan kebenaran tentang Allah dan kekuasaan-Nya yang dikabarkan oleh Mûsâ. Namun demikian, Fir'aun tetap kufur dan mendustakan semua itu. Ia enggan untuk mempercayainya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami perlihatkan kepadanya) kepada Firaun (ayat-ayat Kami semuanya) yang berjumlah sembilan ayat itu (maka ia mendustakan) nya dan menuduh bahwa ayat-ayat itu adalah sihir (dan ia enggan) untuk mengesakan Allah swt.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah perlihatkan kepadanya (Fir'aun) tanda-tanda kekuasaan Kami semuanya maka ia mendustakan dan enggan (menerima kebenaran).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد أريناه» أي أبصرنا فرعون «آياتنا كلها» التسع «فكذب» بها وزعم أنها سحر «وأبى» أن يوحد الله تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَرَيۡنَٰهُ ءَايَٰتِنَا كُلَّهَا فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami perlihatkan kepadanya) kepada Firaun (ayat-ayat Kami semuanya) yang berjumlah sembilan ayat itu (maka ia mendustakan) nya dan menuduh bahwa ayat-ayat itu adalah sihir (dan ia enggan) untuk mengesakan Allah swt

ar: قَالَ أَجِئۡتَنَا لِتُخۡرِجَنَا مِنۡ أَرۡضِنَا بِسِحۡرِكَ یَـٰمُوسَىٰ
ar_simple: قال أجئتنا لتخرجنا من أرضنا بسحرك یموسى

t.sahih-int: He said, "Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses
t.asad: He said: "Hash thou come to drive us out of our land by thy sorcery, O Moses
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "Hast thou come to drive us out of our land with thy magic, O Moses
t.pickthall: He said: Hast come to drive us out from our land by thy magic, O Moses
t.arberry: Hast thou come, Moses,' he said, 'to expel us out of our land
t.hilali-khan: He [Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said: "Have you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Musa (Moses)
t.itani: He said, “Did you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?
t.mubarakpuri: He (Fir`awn) said: "Have you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Musa"
t.qarib: [Pharaoh] said, "Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, Moses
t.wahiduddin: He said, "Have you come to us to turn us out of our land by means of your magic, Moses
t.shakir: Said he: Have you come to us that you should turn us out of our land by your magic, O Musa
t.haleem: He said, ‘Have you come to drive us from our land with your sorcery, Moses
t.maariful: Said he, “Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your sorcery, O Mūsā
t.khattab: He said, “Have you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses
t.ghali: He said, "Have you come to us to drive us out of our land by your sorcery, O Musa
t.irving: He said: "Have you come to us to turn us out of our land through your magic, Moses
t.iwq: And he said, "Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Fir'aun: "Adakah kamu datang kepada kami untuk mengusir kami dari negeri kami (ini) dengan sihirmu, hai Musa?
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun berkata kepada Mûsâ, "Apakah kamu datang untuk mengusir kami dari negeri ini, dan kamu menjadikan negeri ini berada dalam kekuasaan kaummu dengan sihir yang telah kamu gunakan untuk membunuh manusia?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Firaun, "Adakah kamu datang kepada kami untuk mengusir kami dari negeri kami) yakni dari negeri Mesir, kemudian kamu menjadi raja padanya (dengan sihirmu, hai Musa?")
t_id.iwq: Berkata Fir'aun: "Adakah kamu datang kepada kami untuk mengusir kami dari negeri kami (ini) dengan sihirmu, hai Musa?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال أجئتنا لتخرجنا من أرضنا» مصر ويكون لك فيها «بسحرك يا موسى»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ أَجِئۡتَنَا لِتُخۡرِجَنَا مِنۡ أَرۡضِنَا بِسِحۡرِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Firaun, "Adakah kamu datang kepada kami untuk mengusir kami dari negeri kami) yakni dari negeri Mesir, kemudian kamu menjadi raja padanya (dengan sihirmu, hai Musa)

ar: فَلَنَأۡتِیَنَّكَ بِسِحۡرࣲ مِّثۡلِهِۦ فَٱجۡعَلۡ بَیۡنَنَا وَبَیۡنَكَ مَوۡعِدࣰا لَّا نُخۡلِفُهُۥ نَحۡنُ وَلَاۤ أَنتَ مَكَانࣰا سُوࣰى
ar_simple: فلنأتینك بسحر مثله فٱجعل بیننا وبینك موعدا لا نخلفه نحن ولا أنت مكانا سوى

t.sahih-int: Then we will surely bring you magic like it, so make between us and you an appointment, which we will not fail to keep and neither will you, in a place assigned
t.asad: In that case, we shall most certainly produce before thee the like thereof! Appoint, then, a tryst between us and thee - which we shall not fail to keep, nor [mayest] thou -at a suitable place
t.yusuf-ali: But we can surely produce magic to match thine! So make a tryst between us and thee, which we shall not fail to keep - neither we nor thou - in a place where both shall have even chances
t.pickthall: But we surely can produce for thee magic the like thereof; so appoint a tryst between us and you, which neither we nor thou shall fail to keep, at a place convenient (to us both)
t.arberry: by thy sorcery? We shall assuredly bring thee sorcery the like of it; therefore appoint a tryst between us and thee, a place mutually agreeable, and we shall not fail it, neither thou
t.hilali-khan: Then verily, we can produce magic the like thereof; so appoint a meeting between us and you, which neither we, nor you shall fail to keep, in an open wide place where both shall have a just and equal chance (and beholders could witness the competition)
t.itani: We will produce for you magic like it; so make an appointment between us and you, which we will not miss—neither us, nor you—in a central place.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Then verily, we can produce magic the like thereof; so appoint a meeting between us and you, which neither we nor you shall fail to keep, in an open place where both shall have a just and equal chance."
t.qarib: Then we will surely bring you magic like it. So let's set a time that neither [of us] will fail to keep in a mutually agreed-upon place
t.wahiduddin: We will certainly bring you magic to match it. So appoint a time between us and you, in an open space, which neither we nor you will fail to keep
t.shakir: So we too will produce before you magic like it, therefore make between us and you an appointment, which we should not break, (neither) we nor you, (in) a central place
t.haleem: We will confront you with sorcery to match your own: make an appointment between us which neither of us will fail to keep, in a mutually agreeable place.’
t.maariful: We will, then, bring to you a similar sorcery. So, make an appointment between you and us for an even place, not to be backed out, neither by us nor by you.”
t.khattab: We can surely meet you with similar magic. So set for us an appointment that neither of us will fail to keep, in a central place.”
t.ghali: Then, indeed we will definitely come up to you with sorcery the like of it; so set (Literally: make) an appointment between us and you, and we will not fail it, neither we nor you, a place (equally) convenient (to us both)
t.irving: We will bring magic to match it, so make an appointment between us and you in some convenient place which neither we nor you will break
t.iwq: Then we will surely bring you magic like it, so make between us and you an appointment, which we will not fail to keep and neither will you, in a place assigned

t_id.kemenag: Dan kamipun pasti akan mendatangkan (pula) kepadamu sihir semacam itu, maka buatlah suatu waktu untuk pertemuan antara kami dan kamu, yang kami tidak akan menyalahinya dan tidak (pula) kamu di suatu tempat yang pertengahan (letaknya).
t_id.sabiq: Kami akan menghentikan sihirmu dengan sihir kami. Tentukanlah waktu yang kita sepakati bersama untuk pertemuan kita, dan jangan ada seorang pun dari kita yang menyalahinya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Dan kami pun pasti akan mendatangkan pula kepadamu sihir semacam itu) yang akan melawannya (maka buatlah suatu waktu antara kami dan kamu) untuk pertemuan itu (yang kami tidak akan menyalahinya dan tidak pula kamu, di suatu tempat) lafal Makaanan ini dinashabkan dengan mencabut huruf Jarnya, maksudnya, di tempat (yang pertengahan") lafal Suwan dapat pula dibaca Siwan, artinya, tempat yang letaknya pertengahan, dari arah mana saja didatangi oleh kedua pihak jaraknya sama.
t_id.iwq: Dan kamipun pasti akan mendatangkan (pula) kepadamu sihir semacam itu, maka buatlah suatu waktu untuk pertemuan antara kami dan kamu, yang kami tidak akan menyalahinya dan tidak (pula) kamu di suatu tempat yang pertengahan (letaknya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلنأتينك بسحر مثله» يعارضه «فاجعل بيننا وبينك موعدا» لذلك «لا نخلفه نحن ولا أنت مكانا» منصوب بنزع الخافض في «سِوى» بكسر أوله وضمه أي وسطا تستوي مسافة الجائي إليه من الطرفين
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَنَأۡتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحۡرٖ مِّثۡلِهِۦ فَٱجۡعَلۡ بَيۡنَنَا وَبَيۡنَكَ مَوۡعِدٗا لَّا نُخۡلِفُهُۥ نَحۡنُ وَلَآ أَنتَ مَكَانٗا سُوٗى (سُوًى: مُسْتَوِيًا مُعْتَدِلًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan kami pun pasti akan mendatangkan pula kepadamu sihir semacam itu) yang akan melawannya (maka buatlah suatu waktu antara kami dan kamu) untuk pertemuan itu (yang kami tidak akan menyalahinya dan tidak pula kamu, di suatu tempat) lafal Makaanan ini dinashabkan dengan mencabut huruf Jarnya, maksudnya, di tempat (yang pertengahan") lafal Suwan dapat pula dibaca Siwan, artinya, tempat yang letaknya pertengahan, dari arah mana saja didatangi oleh kedua pihak jaraknya sama

ar: قَالَ مَوۡعِدُكُمۡ یَوۡمُ ٱلزِّینَةِ وَأَن یُحۡشَرَ ٱلنَّاسُ ضُحࣰى
ar_simple: قال موعدكم یوم ٱلزینة وأن یحشر ٱلناس ضحى

t.sahih-int: [Moses] said, "Your appointment is on the day of the festival when the people assemble at mid-morning
t.asad: Answered [Moses]: "Your tryst shall be the day of the Festival; and let the people assemble when the sun is risen high
t.yusuf-ali: Moses said: "Your tryst is the Day of the Festival, and let the people be assembled when the sun is well up
t.pickthall: (Moses) said: Your tryst shall be the day of the feast, and let the people assemble when the sun hath risen high
t.arberry: Your tryst shall be upon the Feast Day.' said Moses. 'Let the people be mustered at the high noon
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses)] said: "Your appointed meeting is the day of the festival, and let the people assemble when the sun has risen (forenoon)
t.itani: He said, “Your appointment is the day of the festival, so let the people be gathered together at mid-morning.”
t.mubarakpuri: (Musa) said: "Your appointed meeting is the day of the festival, and let the people assemble when the sun has risen (forenoon)."
t.qarib: [Moses] said, "Your appointment is on the day of the festival, when the people assemble at mid-morning
t.wahiduddin: Moses said, "The day of the encounter will be the day of the festival, and let the people assemble when the sun has risen high
t.shakir: (Musa) said: Your appointment is the day of the Festival and let the people be gathered together in the early forenoon
t.haleem: He said, ‘Your meeting will be on the day of the feast, so let the people be assembled when the sun has risen high.’
t.maariful: He (Mūsā) said, “Your appointment is the festival day, and the people should be assembled at forenoon.”
t.khattab: Moses said, “Your appointment is on the Day of the Festival, and let the people be gathered mid-morning.”
t.ghali: He (Musa) said, "Your appointment shall be upon the adornment day, (i.e., the feast day) and let the multitudes be mustered at the forenoon
t.irving: He said: "Your appointment will be on Decoration Day so that people may be summoned early in the morning
t.iwq: Moses said, "Your appointment is on the day of the festival when the people assemble at mid-morning

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Musa: "Waktu untuk pertemuan (kami dengan) kamu itu ialah di hari raya dan hendaklah dikumpulkan manusia pada waktu matahari sepenggalahan naik".
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ menjawab, "Waktu kita adalah pada hari raya kalian, ketika kalian berhias diri dan bersuka ria, sehingga pada hari itu orang-orang berkumpul di waktu duha, untuk menyaksikan apa yang akan terjadi di antara kita."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah ia) Nabi Musa, ("Waktu untuk pertemuan kami dengan kalian itu adalah hari raya) yakni hari raya Firaun dan kaumnya, yang pada hari itu mereka berhias diri dan berkumpul-kumpul (dan hendaklah dikumpulkan manusia) semua penduduk negeri Mesir dikumpulkan (pada waktu matahari naik sepenggalah") untuk menyaksikan apa yang akan terjadi.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Musa: "Waktu untuk pertemuan (kami dengan) kamu itu ialah di hari raya dan hendaklah dikumpulkan manusia pada waktu matahari sepenggalahan naik".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» موسى «موعدكم يوم الزينة» يوم عيد لهم يتزينون فيه ويجتمعون «وأن يُحشر الناس» يجمع أهل مصر «ضحى» وقته للنظر فيما يقع
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ مَوۡعِدُكُمۡ يَوۡمُ ٱلزِّينَةِ وَأَن يُحۡشَرَ ٱلنَّاسُ ضُحٗى (يَوْمُ الزِّينَةِ: يَوْمُ العِيدِ, يُحْشَرَ: يُجْمَعَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah ia) Nabi Musa, ("Waktu untuk pertemuan kami dengan kalian itu adalah hari raya) yakni hari raya Firaun dan kaumnya, yang pada hari itu mereka berhias diri dan berkumpul-kumpul (dan hendaklah dikumpulkan manusia) semua penduduk negeri Mesir dikumpulkan (pada waktu matahari naik sepenggalah") untuk menyaksikan apa yang akan terjadi

ar: فَتَوَلَّىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فَجَمَعَ كَیۡدَهُۥ ثُمَّ أَتَىٰ
ar_simple: فتولى فرعون فجمع كیده ثم أتى

t.sahih-int: So Pharaoh went away, put together his plan, and then came [to Moses]
t.asad: Thereupon Pharaoh withdrew [with his counsellors] and decided upon the scheme which he would pursue; and then he came [to the tryst]
t.yusuf-ali: So Pharaoh withdrew: He concerted his plan, and then came (back)
t.pickthall: Then Pharaoh went and gathered his strength, then came (to the appointed tryst)
t.arberry: Pharaoh then withdrew, and gathered his guile. Thereafter he came again
t.hilali-khan: So Fir'aun (Pharaoh) withdrew, devised his plot and then came back
t.itani: Pharaoh turned away, put together his plan, and then came back.
t.mubarakpuri: So Fir`awn withdrew, devised his plot and then came back.
t.qarib: So Pharaoh turned away, decided on his scheme, and then he returned
t.wahiduddin: So Pharaoh withdrew, devised his stratagem and returned
t.shakir: So Firon turned his back and settled his plan, then came
t.haleem: Pharaoh withdrew and gathered his resources, then he returned
t.maariful: So, Pharaoh turned back, and put his plot together, then came
t.khattab: Pharaoh then withdrew, orchestrated his scheme, then returned
t.ghali: Then Firaawn turned away and so gathered his plotting; thereafter (he) came up
t.irving: So Pharaoh turned away and put his plan together; then he came [back]
t.iwq: So Pharaoh went away, put together his plan, and then came to Moses

t_id.kemenag: Maka Fir'aun meninggalkan (tempat itu), lalu mengatur tipu dayanya, kemudian dia datang.
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun pun meninggalkan tempat itu untuk kemudian menangani sendiri urusan itu. Dilengkapinya pula segala sarana demi menyukseskan urusan itu, dengan mengumpulkan penyihir-penyihir kampiun, kemudian dengan itu semua ia datang pada waktu yang telah disepakati bersama.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Firaun meninggalkan) pergi meninggalkan tempat itu (lalu ia mengatur tipu muslihatnya) ia mulai mengumpulkan para ahli sihirnya (kemudian ia datang) bersama mereka pada waktu yang telah ditentukan itu.
t_id.iwq: Maka Fir'aun meninggalkan (tempat itu), lalu mengatur tipu dayanya, kemudian dia datang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فتولى فرعون» أدير «فجمع كيده» أي ذوي كيده من السحرة «ثم أتى» بهم الموعد
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَتَوَلَّىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فَجَمَعَ كَيۡدَهُۥ ثُمَّ أَتَىٰ (كَيْدَهُ: المُرَادُ: سَحَرَتُهُ الَّذِينَ يَكِيدُ بِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Firaun meninggalkan) pergi meninggalkan tempat itu (lalu ia mengatur tipu muslihatnya) ia mulai mengumpulkan para ahli sihirnya (kemudian ia datang) bersama mereka pada waktu yang telah ditentukan itu

ar: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ وَیۡلَكُمۡ لَا تَفۡتَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبࣰا فَیُسۡحِتَكُم بِعَذَابࣲۖ وَقَدۡ خَابَ مَنِ ٱفۡتَرَىٰ
ar_simple: قال لهم موسى ویلكم لا تفتروا على ٱلله كذبا فیسحتكم بعذاب وقد خاب من ٱفترى

t.sahih-int: Moses said to the magicians summoned by Pharaoh, "Woe to you! Do not invent a lie against Allah or He will exterminate you with a punishment; and he has failed who invents [such falsehood]
t.asad: Said Moses to them: "Woe unto you! Do not invent lies against God, lest He afflict you with most grievous suffering: for He who contrives [such] a lie is already undone
t.yusuf-ali: Moses said to him: Woe to you! Forge not ye a lie against Allah, lest He destroy you (at once) utterly by chastisement: the forger must suffer frustration
t.pickthall: Moses said unto them: Woe unto you! Invent not a lie against Allah, lest He extirpate you by some punishment. He who lieth faileth miserably
t.arberry: and Moses said to them, 'O beware! Forge not a lie against God, lest He destroy you with a chastisement. Whoso forges has ever failed
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses) said to them: "Woe unto you! Invent not a lie against Allah, lest He should destroy you completely by a torment. And surely, he who invents a lie (against Allah) will fail miserably
t.itani: Moses said to them, “Woe to you. Do not fabricate lies against God, or He will destroy you with a punishment. He who invents lies will fail.”
t.mubarakpuri: Musa said to them: "Woe unto you! Invent not a lie against Allah, lest He should destroy you completely by a torment. And surely, he who invents a lie (against Allah) will fail miserably."
t.qarib: Moses said to the magicians summoned by Pharaoh, "Beware! Do not invent lies against God or He will afflict you with a grievous punishment. He who lies shall fail
t.wahiduddin: Moses said to them, "Woe to you! Do not invent lies against God, lest He destroy you by some calamity: whoever invents lies is bound to fail
t.shakir: Musa said to them: Woe to you! do not forge a lie against Allah, lest He destroy you by a punishment, and he who forges (a lie) indeed fails to attain (his desire)
t.haleem: Moses said to them, ‘Beware, do not invent lies against God or He will destroy you with His punishment. Whoever invents lies will fail.’
t.maariful: Mūsā said to them, “Pity on you, do not fabricate a lie against Allah, lest He should destroy you with a punishment. And loser is he who fabricates a lie.”
t.khattab: Moses warned the magicians, “Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, or He will wipe you out with a torment. Whoever fabricates ˹lies˺ is bound to fail.”
t.ghali: Musa said to them, "Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah (or) then He will eradicate you with a torment; and whoever fabricates (a lie) is readily disappointed
t.irving: Moses told them: "Watch out for yourselves! Don´t invent any lie about God lest He blot you out through torment. Anyone who invents things will be disappointed
t.iwq: Moses said to the magicians summoned by Pharaoh, "Woe to you! Do not invent a lie against Allah or He will exterminate you with a punishment; and he has failed who invents such falsehood

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Musa kepada mereka: "Celakalah kamu, janganlah kamu mengada-adakan kedustaan terhadap Allah, maka Dia membinasakan kamu dengan siksa". Dan sesungguhnya telah merugi orang yang mengada-adakan kedustaan.
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ mengingatkan mereka akan ancaman dan siksaan Allah, melarang mereka agar tidak mengada- adakan kebohongan dengan menganggap Fir'aun sebagai tuhan, mendustakan rasul-rasul Allah dan mengingkari mukjizat. Mûsâ juga memberikan ancaman bahwa Allah akan menbinasakan mereka dengan siksa-Nya manakala mereka terus menerus melakukan hal itu, dengan menegaskan kerugian orang yang membuat-buat kebohongan tentang Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa kepada mereka,) jumlah para ahli sihir Firaun ada tujuh puluh dua orang; setiap orang dari mereka memegang tali dan tongkat ("Celakalah kalian) maksudnya semoga Allah menimpakan kecelakaan kepada kalian (janganlah kalian mengada-adakan kedustaan terhadap Allah) dengan menyekutukan seseorang bersama-Nya (maka Dia membinasakan kalian) ia dapat dibaca Fayushitakum dan Fayashitakum, artinya Dia akan membinasakan kalian, karena perbuatan musyrik itu (dengan siksa") dari sisi-Nya. (Dan sesungguhnya telah kecewa) merugi (orang yang mengada-adakan kedustaan) terhadap Allah.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Musa kepada mereka: "Celakalah kamu, janganlah kamu mengada-adakan kedustaan terhadap Allah, maka Dia membinasakan kamu dengan siksa". Dan sesungguhnya telah merugi orang yang mengada-adakan kedustaan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال لهم موسى» وهم اثنان وسبعون مع كل واحد حبل وعصا «ويلكم» أي ألزمكم الله الويل «لا تفتروا على الله كذبا» بإشراك أحد معه «فيُسحتكم» بضم الياء وكسر الحاء وبفتحهما أي يهلككم «بعذاب» من عنده «وقد خاب» خسر «من افترى» كذب على الله
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ وَيۡلَكُمۡ لَا تَفۡتَرُواْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبٗا فَيُسۡحِتَكُم بِعَذَابٖۖ وَقَدۡ خَابَ مَنِ ٱفۡتَرَىٰ (لَا تَفْتَرُوا: لَا تَخْتَلِقُوا, فَيُسْحِتَكُمْ: فَيَسْتَاصِلَكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa kepada mereka,) jumlah para ahli sihir Firaun ada tujuh puluh dua orang; setiap orang dari mereka memegang tali dan tongkat ("Celakalah kalian) maksudnya semoga Allah menimpakan kecelakaan kepada kalian (janganlah kalian mengada-adakan kedustaan terhadap Allah) dengan menyekutukan seseorang bersama-Nya (maka Dia membinasakan kalian) ia dapat dibaca Fayushitakum dan Fayashitakum, artinya Dia akan membinasakan kalian, karena perbuatan musyrik itu (dengan siksa") dari sisi-Nya. (Dan sesungguhnya telah kecewa) merugi (orang yang mengada-adakan kedustaan) terhadap Allah

ar: فَتَنَـٰزَعُوۤا۟ أَمۡرَهُم بَیۡنَهُمۡ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّجۡوَىٰ
ar_simple: فتنزعوا أمرهم بینهم وأسروا ٱلنجوى

t.sahih-int: So they disputed over their affair among themselves and concealed their private conversation
t.asad: So they debated among themselves as to what to do; but they kept their counsel secret
t.yusuf-ali: So they disputed, one with another, over their affair, but they kept their talk secret
t.pickthall: Then they debated one with another what they must do, and they kept their counsel secret
t.arberry: And they disputed upon their plan between them, and communed secretly
t.hilali-khan: Then they debated with one another what they must do, and they kept their talk secret
t.itani: They disagreed among themselves over their affair, and conferred secretly.
t.mubarakpuri: Then they debated one with another what they must do, and they kept their talk secret.
t.qarib: So [the magicians] argued among themselves over their plan but kept their conversation secret
t.wahiduddin: Then they [the magicians] conferred among themselves, whispering to one another
t.shakir: So they disputed with one another about their affair and kept the discourse secret
t.haleem: So they discussed their plan among themselves, talking secretly
t.maariful: Then, they disputed among themselves in their matter, and kept their talk secret
t.khattab: So the magicians disputed the matter among themselves, conversing privately
t.ghali: Then they contended among themselves their command, (Or: their affair) and they conferred in secret
t.irving: They debated their case among themselves and kept the discussion secret
t.iwq: Thus they disputed over their affair among themselves and concealed their private conversation

t_id.kemenag: Maka mereka berbantah-bantahan tentang urusan mereka di antara mereka dan mereka merahasiakan percakapan (mereka).
t_id.sabiq: Mereka terperanjat ketakutan dengan peringatan Mûsâ itu, lalu berembuk secara rahasia di antara mereka dengan saling bertukar pendapat. Masing-masing mengemukakan pendapat tentang apa yang akan ditimpakan kepada Mûsâ.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka berbantah-bantahan di antara mereka tentang urusannya) yakni mengenai Nabi Musa dan saudaranya itu (dan mereka merahasiakan percakapan) mereka yang menyangkut Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun.
t_id.iwq: Maka mereka berbantah-bantahan tentang urusan mereka di antara mereka dan mereka merahasiakan percakapan (mereka).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فتنازعوا أمرهم بينهم» في موسى وأخيه «وأسرُّوا النجوى» أي الكلام بينهم فيهما
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَتَنَٰزَعُوٓاْ أَمۡرَهُم بَيۡنَهُمۡ وَأَسَرُّواْ ٱلنَّجۡوَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka berbantah-bantahan di antara mereka tentang urusannya) yakni mengenai Nabi Musa dan saudaranya itu (dan mereka merahasiakan percakapan) mereka yang menyangkut Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ إِنۡ هَـٰذَ ٰنِ لَسَـٰحِرَ ٰنِ یُرِیدَانِ أَن یُخۡرِجَاكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُم بِسِحۡرِهِمَا وَیَذۡهَبَا بِطَرِیقَتِكُمُ ٱلۡمُثۡلَىٰ
ar_simple: قالوا إن هذ ن لسحر ن یریدان أن یخرجاكم من أرضكم بسحرهما ویذهبا بطریقتكم ٱلمثلى

t.sahih-int: They said, "Indeed, these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and do away with your most exemplary way
t.asad: saying [to one another]: "These two are surely sorcerers intent on driving you from your land by their sorcery, and on doing away with your time-honoured way of life
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "These two are certainly (expert) magicians: their object is to drive you out from your land with their magic, and to do away with your most cherished institutions
t.pickthall: They said: Lo! these are two wizards who would drive you out from your country by their magic, and destroy your best traditions
t.arberry: saying, 'These two men are sorcerers and their purpose is to expel you out of your land by their sorcery, and to extirpate your justest way
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Verily! These are two magicians. Their object is to drive you out from your land with magic, and overcome your chiefs and nobles
t.itani: They said, “These two are magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic, and to abolish your exemplary way of life.
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Verily, these are two magicians. Their object is to drive you out from your land with magic, and to take you away from your exemplary way."
t.qarib: They said, "these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and to abolish your most exemplary way of life
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Certainly they are both magicians who want to drive you out of your land by their magic, and destroy your best traditions
t.shakir: They said: These are most surely two magicians who wish to turn you out from your land by their magic and to take away your best traditions
t.haleem: saying, ‘These two men are sorcerers. Their purpose is to drive you out of your land with their sorcery and put an end to your time-honoured way of life
t.maariful: Said they, “Certainly, these two are sorcerers who wish to drive you out from your land, and do away with your excellent way of life
t.khattab: They concluded, “These two are only magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic, and do away with your most cherished traditions
t.ghali: They said, "Decidedly these two (men) are indeed (both) sorcerers; they would (like) to drive you out of your land by their sorcery, and go away with your exemplary mode (of life)
t.irving: They said: "These are both magicians who want to expel you from your land by means of their magic, and do away with your exemplary ways
t.iwq: And they said, "Indeed, these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and do away with your most exemplary way

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Sesungguhnya dua orang ini adalah benar-benar ahli sihir yang hendak mengusir kamu dari negeri kamu dengan sihirnya dan hendak melenyapkan kedudukan kamu yang utama.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka berkesimpulan bahwa Mûsâ dan Hârûn hanyalah penyihir biasa yang saling bekerja sama untuk mengusir mereka dari negeri mereka dan merebut kekuasaan dari tangan mereka dengan jalan sihir. Dengan begitu, Banû Isrâ'îl akan terjebak dengan sihirnya yang menunjukkan kepalsuan kepercayaan yang mereka mereka anut dan mereka anggap baik.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) kepada diri mereka sendiri, ("Sesungguhnya dua orang ini) ungkapan Haadzaani dijadikan hujah atau alasan bagi sebagian ahli Nahwu yang menetapkan huruf Alif pada isim Tatsniyah dalam tiga keadaan. Akan tetapi menurut qiraat Abu 'Amr, lafal Haadzaani ini dibaca Haadzaini (adalah benar-benar ahli sihir yang hendak mengusir kalian, dari negeri kalian dengan sihirnya dan hendak melenyapkan kedudukan kalian yang utama") lafal Mutslaa adalah bentuk Muannats dari lafal Amtsal; maksudnya yang mulia. Artinya, keduanya akan melenyapkan kemuliaan kalian, bila mereka berdua dibiarkan menang, kemudian kalian cenderung kepada keduanya.
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Sesungguhnya doa orang ini adalah benar-benar ahli sihir yang hendak mengusir kamu dari negeri kamu dengan sihirnya dan hendak melenyapkan kedudukan kamu yang utama.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا» لأنفسهم «إن هذان» وهو موافق للغة من يأتي في المثنى بالألف في أحواله الثلاث ولأبي عمرو: هذين «لساحران يريدان أن يخرجاكم من أرضكم بسحرهما ويذهبا بطريقتكم المثلى» مؤنث أمثل بمعنى أشرف أي بأشرافكم بميلهم إليهما لغلبتهما
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُوٓاْ إِنۡ هَٰذَٰنِ لَسَٰحِرَٰنِ يُرِيدَانِ أَن يُخۡرِجَاكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُم بِسِحۡرِهِمَا وَيَذۡهَبَا بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ ٱلۡمُثۡلَىٰ (بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ الْمُثْلَى: طَرِيقَةِ السِّحْرِ العَظِيمَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) kepada diri mereka sendiri, ("Sesungguhnya dua orang ini) ungkapan Haadzaani dijadikan hujah atau alasan bagi sebagian ahli Nahwu yang menetapkan huruf Alif pada isim Tatsniyah dalam tiga keadaan. Akan tetapi menurut qiraat Abu 'Amr, lafal Haadzaani ini dibaca Haadzaini (adalah benar-benar ahli sihir yang hendak mengusir kalian, dari negeri kalian dengan sihirnya dan hendak melenyapkan kedudukan kalian yang utama") lafal Mutslaa adalah bentuk Muannats dari lafal Amtsal; maksudnya yang mulia. Artinya, keduanya akan melenyapkan kemuliaan kalian, bila mereka berdua dibiarkan menang, kemudian kalian cenderung kepada keduanya

ar: فَأَجۡمِعُوا۟ كَیۡدَكُمۡ ثُمَّ ٱئۡتُوا۟ صَفࣰّاۚ وَقَدۡ أَفۡلَحَ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ مَنِ ٱسۡتَعۡلَىٰ
ar_simple: فأجمعوا كیدكم ثم ٱئتوا صفا وقد أفلح ٱلیوم من ٱستعلى

t.sahih-int: So resolve upon your plan and then come [forward] in line. And he has succeeded today who overcomes
t.asad: Hence, [O sorcerers of Egypt,] decide upon the scheme which you will pursue, and then come forward in one single body: for, indeed, he who prevails today shall prosper indeed
t.yusuf-ali: Therefore concert your plan, and then assemble in (serried) ranks: He wins (all along) today who gains the upper hand
t.pickthall: So arrange your plan, and come in battle line. Whoso is uppermost this day will be indeed successful
t.arberry: So gather your guile; then come in battle-line. Whoever today gains the upper hand shall surely prosper
t.hilali-khan: So devise your plot, and then assemble in line. And whoever overcomes this day will be indeed successful
t.itani: So settle your plan, and come as one front. Today, whoever gains the upper hand will succeed.”
t.mubarakpuri: "So devise your plot, and then assemble in line. And whoever overcomes this day will be indeed successful."
t.qarib: So gather your resources and line up for the contest. And whoever succeeds today will prosper
t.wahiduddin: Therefore, decide upon your plan and then come forward in ranks. Whoever gains the upper hand today shall surely triumph
t.shakir: Therefore settle your plan, then come standing in ranks and he will prosper indeed this day who overcomes
t.haleem: So gather your resources and line up for the contest. Whoever wins today is sure to prosper.’
t.maariful: So, make your plot firm and come forth in one row. Successful today is the one who prevails.”
t.khattab: So orchestrate your plan, then come forward in ˹perfect˺ ranks. And whoever prevails today will certainly be successful.”
t.ghali: So gather your plotting, (and) there- after come up in ranks; (i.e., in battle, ranks) and whoever seeks exaltedness today will readily prosper
t.irving: Get together for your scheme; then come all lined up. Whoever comes out on top today will prosper
t.iwq: So resolve upon your plan and then come forward in line. And he has succeeded today who overcomes

t_id.kemenag: Maka himpunkanlah segala daya (sihir) kamu sekalian, kemudian datanglah dengan berbaris. dan sesungguhnya beruntunglah oran yang menang pada hari ini.
t_id.sabiq: Seorang di antara mereka berkata, "Bersatu dan bersepepakatlah kalian mengenai apa yang akan kalian perdayakan kepada Mûsâ. Datanglah dalam barisan teratur agar kalian tampak berwibawa dan perkasa di mata orang-orang yang melihat kalian. Sungguh beruntung orang yang menang pada hari ini."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Maka himpunkanlah segala daya upaya kalian) semua kekuatan dan keahlian sihir kalian. Kalau dibaca Fajma'uu berasal dari lafal Jama'a dan kalau dibaca Fa-ajmi'uu berasal dari lafal Ajma'a, artinya kerahkanlah (kemudian datanglah dengan berbaris). Lafal Shaffan adalah Hal atau kata keterangan keadaan, maksudnya dalam keadaan berbaris (dan sesungguhnya beruntunglah) yakni akan memperoleh keuntungan (orang yang menang pada hari ini") yakni yang dapat mengalahkan musuhnya.
t_id.iwq: Maka himpunkanlah segala daya (sihir) kamu sekalian, kemudian datanglah dengan berbaris. dan sesungguhnya beruntunglah oran yang menang pada hari ini.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأجمعوا كيدكم» من السحر بهمزة وصل وفتح الميم من جمع أي لمَّ وبهمزة قطع وكسر الميم من أجمع أحكم «ثم أئتوا صفا» حال أي مصطفين «وقد أفلح» فاز «اليوم من استعلى» غلب
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَجۡمِعُواْ كَيۡدَكُمۡ ثُمَّ ٱئۡتُواْ صَفّٗاۚ وَقَدۡ أَفۡلَحَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ مَنِ ٱسۡتَعۡلَىٰ (فَأَجْمِعُوا كَيْدَكُمْ: فأَحْكِمُوا كَيْدَكُمْ، وَاعْزِمُوا عَلَيْهِ، وَلَا تَخْتَلِفُوا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka himpunkanlah segala daya upaya kalian) semua kekuatan dan keahlian sihir kalian. Kalau dibaca Fajma'uu berasal dari lafal Jama'a dan kalau dibaca Fa-ajmi'uu berasal dari lafal Ajma'a, artinya kerahkanlah (kemudian datanglah dengan berbaris). Lafal Shaffan adalah Hal atau kata keterangan keadaan, maksudnya dalam keadaan berbaris (dan sesungguhnya beruntunglah) yakni akan memperoleh keuntungan (orang yang menang pada hari ini") yakni yang dapat mengalahkan musuhnya

ar: قَالُوا۟ یَـٰمُوسَىٰۤ إِمَّاۤ أَن تُلۡقِیَ وَإِمَّاۤ أَن نَّكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنۡ أَلۡقَىٰ
ar_simple: قالوا یموسى إما أن تلقی وإما أن نكون أول من ألقى

t.sahih-int: They said, "O Moses, either you throw or we will be the first to throw
t.asad: Said [the sorcerers]: "O Moses! Either thou throw (thy staff first], or we shall be the first to throw
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "O Moses! whether wilt thou that thou throw (first) or that we be the first to throw
t.pickthall: They said: O Moses! Either throw first, or let us be the first to throw
t.arberry: They said, 'Moses, either thou wilt cast, or we shall be the first to cast
t.hilali-khan: They said:"O Musa (Moses)! Either you throw first or we be the first to throw
t.itani: They said, “O Moses, either you throw, or we will be the first to throw.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "O Musa! Either you throw first or we be the first to throw"
t.qarib: They said, "Moses, either you throw, or we will be the first to throw
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Moses, will you throw down first, or shall we be the first to throw down
t.shakir: They said: O Musa! will you cast, or shall we be the first who cast down
t.haleem: They said, ‘Moses, will you throw first or shall we?’
t.maariful: They said, “O Mūsā, either you throw (first), or shall we be first to throw?”
t.khattab: They said, “O Moses! Either you cast, or let us be the first to cast.”
t.ghali: They said, "O Musa, (Moses) either you will cast, or we will be the first to cast
t.irving: They said: "Moses, either you will cast [something] or shall we be the first to cast [a spell]
t.iwq: And they said, "O Moses, either you throw or we will be the first to throw

t_id.kemenag: (Setelah mereka berkumpul) mereka berkata: "Hai Musa (pilihlah), apakah kamu yang melemparkan (dahulu) atau kamikah orang yang mula-mula melemparkan?"
t_id.sabiq: Para penyihir itu menghadapi Mûsâ dengan satu kata dan langkah. Mereka memberikan pilihan kepadanya dengan penuh rasa sombong dan congkak, apakah ia yang memulai memperlihatkan sihirnya dengan melempar tongkatnya atau mereka yang harus memulai dahulu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Hai Musa!) Pilihlah (apakah kamu yang melemparkan dahulu) tongkatmu (atau kamikah orang yang mula-mula melemparkan?") tongkat.
t_id.iwq: (Setelah mereka berkumpul) mereka berkata: "Hai Musa (pilihlah), apakah kamu yang melemparkan (dahulu) atau kamikah orang yang mula-mula melemparkan?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا يا موسى» اختر «إما أن تُلقيَ» عصاك أولا «وإما أن نكون أول من ألقى» عصاه
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلۡقِيَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنۡ أَلۡقَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Hai Musa!) Pilihlah (apakah kamu yang melemparkan dahulu) tongkatmu (atau kamikah orang yang mula-mula melemparkan?") tongkat

ar: قَالَ بَلۡ أَلۡقُوا۟ۖ فَإِذَا حِبَالُهُمۡ وَعِصِیُّهُمۡ یُخَیَّلُ إِلَیۡهِ مِن سِحۡرِهِمۡ أَنَّهَا تَسۡعَىٰ
ar_simple: قال بل ألقوا فإذا حبالهم وعصیهم یخیل إلیه من سحرهم أنها تسعى

t.sahih-int: He said, "Rather, you throw." And suddenly their ropes and staffs seemed to him from their magic that they were moving [like snakes]
t.asad: He answered: "Nay, you throw [first]." And lo! by virtue of their sorcery, their [magic] ropes and staffs seemed to him to be moving rapidly
t.yusuf-ali: He said, "Nay, throw ye first!" Then behold their ropes and their rods-so it seemed to him on account of their magic - began to be in lively motion
t.pickthall: He said: Nay, do ye throw! Then lo! their cords and their staves, by their magic, appeared to him as though they ran
t.arberry: No,' said Moses. 'Do you cast!' And lo, it seemed to him, by their sorcery, their ropes and their staffs were sliding
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses)] said: "Nay, throw you (first)!" Then behold, their ropes and their sticks, by their magic, appeared to him as though they moved fast
t.itani: He said, “You throw.” And suddenly, their ropes and sticks appeared to him, because of their magic, to be crawling swiftly.
t.mubarakpuri: [Musa] said: "Nay, throw you (first)!" Then behold! their ropes and their sticks, by their magic, appeared to him as though they moved fast.
t.qarib: He said, "No, you throw." And, behold! Their ropes and staffs seemed to him from their sorcery to move [like snakes]
t.wahiduddin: Moses said, "You throw down first." Suddenly their ropes and staffs appeared to him, by their magic, to be moving about rapidly
t.shakir: He said: Nay! cast down. then lo! their cords and their rods-- it was imaged to him on account of their magic as if they were running
t.haleem: ‘You throw,’ said Moses, and––lo and behold!––through their sorcery, their ropes and staffs seemed to him to be moving
t.maariful: He said, “Rather, you throw.” Then, suddenly their ropes and sticks seemed to him, due to their magic, as if they were running
t.khattab: Moses responded, “No, you go first.” And suddenly their ropes and staffs appeared to him—by their magic—to be slithering
t.ghali: He said, "No indeed, you cast!" Then only then, their ropes and their staffs were made to appear to him, by their sorcery, to be gliding along
t.irving: He said: "Rather you throw [first]." You should have seen their ropes and rods! It appeared to him as if they were moving around because of their magic
t.iwq: He said, "Rather, you throw." And suddenly their ropes and staffs seemed to him from their magic that they were moving like snakes

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Musa: "Silahkan kamu sekalian melemparkan". Maka tiba-tiba tali-tali dan tongkat-tongkat mereka, terbayang kepada Musa seakan-akan ia merayap cepat, lantaran sihir mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ berkata, "Mulailah kalian!" Lalu mereka melemparkan tali dan tongkat mereka. Lantaran sihir mereka, terlihat oleh Mûsâ bahwa tali dan tongkat itu berubah menjadi ular-ular yang bergerak dan berjalan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa, "Silakan kamu sekalian melemparkan") maka mereka melemparkannya. (Maka tiba-tiba tali-tali dan tongkat-tongkat mereka) asal kata 'Ishiyyun adalah 'Ushuwwun, kemudian kedua huruf Wawu ditukar menjadi Ya, selanjutnya harakat huruf 'Ain dan Shad dikasrahkan, maka menjadi 'Ishiyyun (terbayang kepada Musa seakan-akan karena pengaruh sihir mereka ia) merupakan ular-ular (yang berjalan) atau merayap pada perutnya.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Musa: "Silahkan kamu sekalian melemparkan". Maka tiba-tiba tali-tali dan tongkat-tongkat mereka, terbayang kepada Musa seakan-akan ia merayap cepat, lantaran sihir mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال بل ألقوا» فألقوا «فإذا حبالهم وعصيهم» أصله عصوو قبلت الواوان ياءين وكسرت العين والصاد «يخيل إليه من سحرهم أنها» حيات «تسعى» على بطونها
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ بَلۡ أَلۡقُواْۖ فَإِذَا حِبَالُهُمۡ وَعِصِيُّهُمۡ يُخَيَّلُ إِلَيۡهِ مِن سِحۡرِهِمۡ أَنَّهَا تَسۡعَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa, "Silakan kamu sekalian melemparkan") maka mereka melemparkannya. (Maka tiba-tiba tali-tali dan tongkat-tongkat mereka) asal kata 'Ishiyyun adalah 'Ushuwwun, kemudian kedua huruf Wawu ditukar menjadi Ya, selanjutnya harakat huruf 'Ain dan Shad dikasrahkan, maka menjadi 'Ishiyyun (terbayang kepada Musa seakan-akan karena pengaruh sihir mereka ia) merupakan ular-ular (yang berjalan) atau merayap pada perutnya

ar: فَأَوۡجَسَ فِی نَفۡسِهِۦ خِیفَةࣰ مُّوسَىٰ
ar_simple: فأوجس فی نفسه خیفة موسى

t.sahih-int: And he sensed within himself apprehension, did Moses
t.asad: and in his heart Moses became apprehensive
t.yusuf-ali: So Moses conceived in his mind a (sort of) fear
t.pickthall: And Moses conceived a fear in his mind
t.arberry: and Moses conceived a fear within him
t.hilali-khan: So Musa (Moses) conceived a fear in himself
t.itani: So Moses felt apprehensive within himself.
t.mubarakpuri: So Musa conceived fear in himself.
t.qarib: Moses felt fear within himself
t.wahiduddin: and in his heart Moses became apprehensive
t.shakir: So Musa conceived in his mind a fear
t.haleem: Moses was inwardly alarmed
t.maariful: So, Mūsā concealed some fear in his heart
t.khattab: So Moses concealed fear within himself
t.ghali: So Musa entertained a fright within himself
t.irving: so Moses conceived a fear within himself
t.iwq: And he sensed within himself apprehension, did Moses

t_id.kemenag: Maka Musa merasa takut dalam hatinya.
t_id.sabiq: Ia pun merasa takut karena pengaruh sihir yang dilihatnya itu, dan khawatir kalau-kalau orang-orang yang melihatnya tidak dapat membedakan antara sihir dan mukjizat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka timbullah perasaan) muncul perasaan (takut dalam hati Musa) dia merasa takut karena ternyata sihir mereka sejenis dengan mukjizatnya, sehingga akibatnya akan mengaburkan mana yang hak dan mana yang batil di mata orang banyak, yang nantinya mereka tidak mau beriman, disebabkan kejadian itu.
t_id.iwq: Maka Musa merasa takut dalam hatinya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأوجس» أحس «في نفسه خيفة موسى» أي خاف من جهة أن سحرهم من جنس معجزته أن يتلبس أمره على الناس فلا يؤمنوا به
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَوۡجَسَ فِي نَفۡسِهِۦ خِيفَةٗ مُّوسَىٰ (فَأَوْجَسَ: فَشَعَرَ، وَأَحَسَّ فِي نَفْسِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka timbullah perasaan) muncul perasaan (takut dalam hati Musa) dia merasa takut karena ternyata sihir mereka sejenis dengan mukjizatnya, sehingga akibatnya akan mengaburkan mana yang hak dan mana yang batil di mata orang banyak, yang nantinya mereka tidak mau beriman, disebabkan kejadian itu

ar: قُلۡنَا لَا تَخَفۡ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰ
ar_simple: قلنا لا تخف إنك أنت ٱلأعلى

t.sahih-int: Allah said, "Fear not. Indeed, it is you who are superior
t.asad: [But] We said: "Fear not! Verily, it is thou who shalt prevail
t.yusuf-ali: We said: "Fear not! for thou hast indeed the upper hand
t.pickthall: We said: Fear not! Lo! thou art the higher
t.arberry: We said unto him, 'Fear not; surely thou art the uppermost
t.hilali-khan: We (Allah) said: "Fear not! Surely, you will have the upper hand
t.itani: We said, “Do not be afraid, you are the uppermost.
t.mubarakpuri: We (Allah) said: "Fear not! Surely, you will have the upper hand."
t.qarib: but, God said, "Have no fear. You will have the upper hand
t.wahiduddin: but We said, "Do not be afraid. It is you who shall prevail
t.shakir: We said: Fear not, surely you shall be the uppermost
t.haleem: but We said, ‘Do not be afraid, you have the upper hand
t.maariful: We said, “Do not be scared. Certainly, you are to be the upper most
t.khattab: We reassured ˹him˺, “Do not fear! It is certainly you who will prevail
t.ghali: We said (to Musa), "Do not fear (anything); surely you (yourself) are the most exalted
t.irving: We said: "Do not act afraid: you will come out on top
t.iwq: Allah said, "Fear not. Indeed, it is you who are superior

t_id.kemenag: Kami berkata: "janganlah kamu takut, sesungguhnya kamulah yang paling unggul (menang).
t_id.sabiq: Lalu Allah memberitahukan Mûsâ kemahalembutan-Nya seraya berfirman, "Kamu tidak perlu merasa takut, sebab kamulah sebenarnya yang akan mengalahkan kebatilan mereka."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kami berkata) kepada Musa, ("Janganlah kamu takut, sesungguhnya kamulah yang paling unggul) kamulah yang akan mengungguli mereka.
t_id.iwq: Kami berkata: "janganlah kamu takut, sesungguhnya kamulah yang paling unggul (menang).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قلنا» له «لا تخف إنك أنت الأعلى» عليهم بالغلبة
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡنَا لَا تَخَفۡ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kami berkata) kepada Musa, ("Janganlah kamu takut, sesungguhnya kamulah yang paling unggul) kamulah yang akan mengungguli mereka

ar: وَأَلۡقِ مَا فِی یَمِینِكَ تَلۡقَفۡ مَا صَنَعُوۤا۟ۖ إِنَّمَا صَنَعُوا۟ كَیۡدُ سَـٰحِرࣲۖ وَلَا یُفۡلِحُ ٱلسَّاحِرُ حَیۡثُ أَتَىٰ
ar_simple: وألق ما فی یمینك تلقف ما صنعوا إنما صنعوا كید سحر ولا یفلح ٱلساحر حیث أتى

t.sahih-int: And throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted. What they have crafted is but the trick of a magician, and the magician will not succeed wherever he is
t.asad: And [now] throw that [staff] which is in thy right hand -it shall swallow up all that they have wrought: [for] they have wrought only a sorcerer's artifice, and the sorcerer can never come to any good, whatever he may aim at
t.yusuf-ali: Throw that which is in thy right hand: Quickly will it swallow up that which they have faked what they have faked is but a magician's trick: and the magician thrives not, (no matter) where he goes
t.pickthall: Throw that which is in thy right hand! It will eat up that which they have made. Lo! that which they have made is but a wizard's artifice, and a wizard shall not be successful to whatever point (of skill) he may attain
t.arberry: Cast down what is in thy right hand, and it shall swallow what they have fashioned; for they have fashioned only the guile of a sorcerer, and the sorcerer prospers not; wherever he goes
t.hilali-khan: And throw that which is in your right hand! It will swallow up that which they have made. That which they have made is only a magician's trick, and the magician will never be successful, no matter whatever amount (of skill) he may attain
t.itani: Now throw down what is in your right hand—it will swallow what they have crafted. What they have crafted is only a magician’s trickery. But the magician will not succeed, no matter what he does.”
t.mubarakpuri: "And throw that which is in your right hand! It will swallow up that which they have made. That which they have made is only a magician's trick, and the magician will never be successful, to whatever amount (of skill) he may attain."
t.qarib: And throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted, for what they have crafted is a magician's trick, and the magician will not succeed in whatever he tries
t.wahiduddin: Throw down that [staff] which is in your right hand -- it shall swallow up what they have wrought, for what they have wrought is only a magician's trick. A magician shall never thrive, come whence he may
t.shakir: And cast down what is in your right hand; it shall devour what they have wrought; they have wrought only the plan of a magician, and the magician shall not be successful wheresoever he may come from
t.haleem: Throw down what is in your right hand: it will swallow up what they have produced. They have only produced the tricks of a sorcerer, and a sorcerer will not prosper, wherever he goes.’
t.maariful: And throw what is in your right hand, and it will devour what they have concocted. What they have concocted is but a sleight of a magician. And the magician does not succeed wherever he comes from.”
t.khattab: Cast what is in your right hand, and it will swallow up what they have made, for what they have made is no more than a magic trick. And magicians can never succeed wherever they go.”
t.ghali: And cast down what is in your right (hand). It will gulp what they have worked out; surely what they have worked out is only the plotting of a sorcerer; and the sorcerer will not prosper where he comes up
t.irving: Throw down what is in your right hand so it will swallow up anything they have produced. What they have produced is only some magician´s trick, and no magician succeeds no matter where he goes
t.iwq: And throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted. What they have crafted is but the trick of a magician, and the magician will not succeed wherever he is

t_id.kemenag: Dan lemparkanlah apa yang ada ditangan kananmu, niscaya ia akan menelan apa yang mereka perbuat. "Sesungguhnya apa yang mereka perbuat itu adalah tipu daya tukang sihir (belaka). Dan tidak akan menang tukang sihir itu, dari mana saja ia datang".
t_id.sabiq: "Lemparkanlah tongkat yang ada di tangan kananmu itu," kata Allah kemudian, "niscaya ia akan menelan kepalsuan sihir mereka. Sesungguhnya apa yang mereka perbuat itu tidak lebih dari sekadar tipuan para penyihir belaka. Dan seorang penyihir tidak akan pernah menang di mana pun ia berada."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan lemparkanlah apa yang ada di tangan kananmu) yakni tongkat (niscaya ia akan menelan) yakni akan melahap (apa yang mereka perbuat. Sesungguhnya apa yang mereka perbuat itu adalah tipu daya tukang sihir) ulah tukang sihir belaka. (Dan tidak akan menang tukang sihir itu, dari mana saja ia datang") dengan sihirnya itu. Lalu Nabi Musa melemparkan tongkatnya, maka tongkat Nabi Musa itu menjadi ular yang besar dan menelan semua apa yang diperbuat oleh para ahli sihir.
t_id.iwq: Dan lemparkanlah apa yang ada ditangan kananmu, niscaya ia akan menelan apa yang mereka perbuat. "Sesungguhnya apa yang mereka perbuat itu adalah tipu daya tukang sihir (belaka). Dan tidak akan menang tukang sihir itu, dari mana saja ia datang".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وألقِ ما في يمينك» وهي عصاه «تَلْقَف» تبتلع «ما صنعوا إنما صنعوا كيد ساحر» أي جنسه «ولا يفلح الساحر حيث أتى» بسحره فألقى موسى عصاه فتلقَّفت كل ما صنعوه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَلۡقِ مَا فِي يَمِينِكَ تَلۡقَفۡ مَا صَنَعُوٓاْۖ إِنَّمَا صَنَعُواْ كَيۡدُ سَٰحِرٖۖ وَلَا يُفۡلِحُ ٱلسَّاحِرُ حَيۡثُ أَتَىٰ (تَلْقَفْ: تَبْتَلِعْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan lemparkanlah apa yang ada di tangan kananmu) yakni tongkat (niscaya ia akan menelan) yakni akan melahap (apa yang mereka perbuat. Sesungguhnya apa yang mereka perbuat itu adalah tipu daya tukang sihir) ulah tukang sihir belaka. (Dan tidak akan menang tukang sihir itu, dari mana saja ia datang") dengan sihirnya itu. Lalu Nabi Musa melemparkan tongkatnya, maka tongkat Nabi Musa itu menjadi ular yang besar dan menelan semua apa yang diperbuat oleh para ahli sihir

ar: فَأُلۡقِیَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدࣰا قَالُوۤا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَـٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ
ar_simple: فألقی ٱلسحرة سجدا قالوا ءامنا برب هرون وموسى

t.sahih-int: So the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, "We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.asad: And so it happened and down fell the sorcerers, prostrating themselves in adoration, [and] exclaimed: "We have come to believe in the Sustainer of Moses and Aaron
t.yusuf-ali: So the magicians were thrown down to prostration: they said, "We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.pickthall: Then the wizards were (all) flung down prostrate, crying: We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.arberry: And the sorcerers cast themselves down prostrating. 'We believe,' they said, 'in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.hilali-khan: So the magicians fell down prostrate. They said: "We believe in the Lord of Harun (Aaron) and Musa (Moses)
t.itani: And the magicians fell down prostrate. They said, “We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.”
t.mubarakpuri: So the magicians fell down prostrate. They said: "We believe in the Lord of Harun and Musa."
t.qarib: [So it was, and] the magicians, fell down in prostration. They said, "We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.wahiduddin: The magicians then prostrated themselves. They said, "We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.shakir: And the magicians were cast down making obeisance; they said: We believe in the Lord of Haroun and Musa
t.haleem: [So it was, and] the sorcerers threw themselves down in submission. ‘We believe,’ they said, ‘in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.’
t.maariful: So, the magicians were led (by the truth) to fall in prostration. They said, “We have (now) believed in the Lord of Hārūn and Mūsā.”
t.khattab: So the magicians fell down in prostration, declaring, “We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.”
t.ghali: Then the sorcerers were (all) cast down constantly prostrating. They said, "We believe in The Lord of Harun (Aaron) and Musa
t.irving: So the magicians threw themselves down on their knees. They said: "We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses
t.iwq: Thus the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, "We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses

t_id.kemenag: Lalu tukang-tukang sihir itu tersungkur dengan bersujud, seraya berkata: "Kami telah percaya kepada Tuhan Harun dan Musa".
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ pun segera melempar tongkatnya. Tiba-tiba, dengan kekuasaan Allah, tongkat itu berubah menjadi ular besar dan menakutkan, yang menelan semua yang dihasilkan oleh sihir mereka. Ketika melihat mukjizat Mûsâ itu, penyihir-penyihir itu segera bersujud meyakini kebenaran Mûsâ seraya berkata, "Kami beriman kepada Allah semata, Tuhan Mûsâ dan Hârûn, serta Tuhan segala sesuatu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lalu tukang-tukang sihir itu tersungkur dengan bersujud) yakni mereka bersujud kepada Allah swt. (seraya berkata, "Kami telah percaya kepada Rabb Harun dan Musa").
t_id.iwq: Lalu tukang-tukang sihir itu tersungkur dengan bersujud, seraya berkata: "Kami telah percaya kepada Tuhan Harun dan Musa".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فألقي السحرة سجدا» خرّوا ساجدين لله تعالى «قالوا آمنا برب هارون وموسى»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأُلۡقِيَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدٗا قَالُوٓاْ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Lalu tukang-tukang sihir itu tersungkur dengan bersujud) yakni mereka bersujud kepada Allah swt. (seraya berkata, "Kami telah percaya kepada Rabb Harun dan Musa)

ar: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِیرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِی عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحۡرَۖ فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَیۡدِیَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَـٰفࣲ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ فِی جُذُوعِ ٱلنَّخۡلِ وَلَتَعۡلَمُنَّ أَیُّنَاۤ أَشَدُّ عَذَابࣰا وَأَبۡقَىٰ
ar_simple: قال ءامنتم له قبل أن ءاذن لكم إنه لكبیركم ٱلذی علمكم ٱلسحر فلأقطعن أیدیكم وأرجلكم من خلف ولأصلبنكم فی جذوع ٱلنخل ولتعلمن أینا أشد عذابا وأبقى

t.sahih-int: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed him before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will surely know which of us is more severe in [giving] punishment and more enduring
t.asad: Said [Pharaoh]: "Have you come to believe in him ere I have given you permission? Verily, he must be your master who has taught you magic! But I shall most certainly cut off your hands and feet in great numbers, because of [your] perverseness, and I shall most certainly crucify you in great numbers on trunks of palm-trees: and [I shall do this] so that you might come to know for certain as to which of us [two] can inflict a more severe chastisement, and [which] is the more abiding
t.yusuf-ali: (Pharaoh) said: "Believe ye in Him before I give you permission? Surely this must be your leader, who has taught you magic! be sure I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will have you crucified on trunks of palm-trees: so shall ye know for certain, which of us can give the more severe and the more lasting punishment
t.pickthall: (Pharaoh) said: Ye put faith in him before I give you leave. Lo! he is your chief who taught you magic. Now surely I shall cut off your hands and your feet alternately, and I shall crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and ye shall know for certain which of us hath sterner and more lasting punishment
t.arberry: Pharaoh said, 'Have you believed him before I gave you leave? Why, he is the chief of you, the same that taught you sorcery; I shall assuredly cut off alternately your hands and feet, then I shall crucify you upon the trunks of palm-trees; you shall know of a certainty which of us is more terrible in chastisement, and more abiding
t.hilali-khan: Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said: "Believe you in him [Musa (Moses)] before I give you permission? Verily! He is your chief who taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you on the trunks of date-palms, and you shall surely know which of us [I (Fir'aun - Pharaoh) or the Lord of Musa (Moses) (Allah)] can give the severe and more lasting torment
t.itani: He said, “Did you believe in him before I have given you permission? He must be your chief, who has taught you magic. I will cut off your hands and your feet on alternate sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of the palm-trees. Then you will know which of us is more severe in punishment, and more lasting.”
t.mubarakpuri: (Fir`awn) said: "Believe you in him before I give you permission Verily, he is your chief who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you on the trunks of date palms, and you shall surely know which of us can give the severe and more lasting torment. "
t.qarib: [Pharaoh] said, "How dare you believe him before I gave you permission? He must be your leader who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will surely know which of us is more severe in punishment and more enduring
t.wahiduddin: Pharaoh said, "Have you believed in him before I permit you? He must be your master who has taught you magic. I will cut your hands and feet off on opposite sides, and have you crucified on the trunks of palm-trees. You shall know whose punishment is more severe and more lasting
t.shakir: (Firon) said: You believe in him before I give you leave; most surely he is the chief of you who taught you enchantment, therefore I will certainly cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will certainly crucify you on the trunks of the palm trees, and certainly you will come to know which of us is the more severe and the more abiding in chastising
t.haleem: Pharaoh said, ‘How dare you believe in him before I have given you permission? This must be your master, the man who taught you witchcraft. I shall certainly cut off your alternate hands and feet, then crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. You will know for certain which of us has the fiercer and more lasting punishment.’
t.maariful: He (Pharaoh) said, “You have believed in him before I permit you. This man is in fact your master who has taught you the magic. So I will certainly cut off your hands and your legs from opposite sides, and will crucify you on the trunks of palm-trees, and you shall know whose punishment, out of us, is more severe and more lasting.”
t.khattab: Pharaoh threatened, “How dare you believe in him before I give you permission? He must be your master who taught you magic. I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. You will really see whose punishment is more severe and more lasting.”
t.ghali: He (Firaawn) (pharaoh) said, "Have you believed him before I gave permission (to do so)? Surely he is indeed your great (master) who has taught you sorcery; so indeed I will definitely cut off (The from of the verb implies an action done repeatedly or to great extent or high degree. Of Surat Yusuf 31) your hands and legs, alternately; and indeed I will definitely crucify you upon the trunks of palm-trees; and indeed you will definitely know whichever of us is more strict in torment and more enduring
t.irving: He said: "Have you believed in Him before I permit you to? He must be your chief who has taught you magic! I´ll cut your hands and feet off on opposite sides, and have you crucified on the trunks of datepalms, so you may know which of us is harsher with torment and going to last longer
t.iwq: Pharaoh said, "You believed him before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will surely know which of us is more severe in giving punishment and more enduring

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Fir'aun: "Apakah kamu telah beriman kepadanya (Musa) sebelum aku memberi izin kepadamu sekalian. Sesungguhnya ia adalah pemimpinmu yang mengajarkan sihir kepadamu sekalian. Maka sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kaki kamu sekalian dengan bersilang secara bertimbal balik, dan sesungguhnya aku akan menyalib kamu sekalian pada pangkal pohon kurma dan sesungguhnya kamu akan mengetahui siapa di antara kita yang lebih pedih dan lebih kekal siksanya".
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun berkata, "Mengapa kalian beriman kepadanya tanpa seizinku? Mûsâ hanyalah seorang ahli sihir yang unggul dan mengajarkan sihirnya kepada kalian. Apa yang dilakukannya itu bukanlah mukjizat seperti kalian kira!" Fir'aun lalu mengancam mereka dengan berkata, "Akan aku potong tangan dan kaki kalian dengan cara bersilang: tangan sebelah kanan dan kaki sebelah kiri. Akan aku salib kalian pada pangkal pohon kurma, sehingga kalian akan mengetahui tuhan manakah yang paling pedih dan kekal siksaannya: aku atau Tuhan Mûsâ!"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah) Firaun, ("Apakah kalian telah beriman) dapat dibaca A-amantum dan Aamantum (kepada Musa sebelum aku memberi izin) sebelum aku mengizinkan (kepadamu sekalian. Sesungguhnya ia adalah pemimpin kalian) guru kalian (yang mengajarkan sihir kepada kalian. Maka sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kaki kamu sekalian dengan bersilang secara bertimbal balik) lafal Min Khilaafin berkedudukan menjadi Hal, artinya secara bersilang, yaitu tangan kanan dengan kaki kiri (dan sesungguhnya aku akan menyalib kalian pada pangkal pohon kurma) yakni pada pohon kurma (dan sesungguhnya kalian akan mengetahui siapa di antara kita) yakni diri Firaun sendiri dan Rabb Nabi Musa (yang lebih pedih dan lebih kekal siksaannya") bila ada orang yang melanggar kepadanya.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Fir'aun: "Apakah kamu telah beriman kepadanya (Musa) sebelum aku memberi izin kepadamu sekalian. Sesungguhnya ia adalah pemimpinmu yang mengajarkan sihir kepadamu sekalian. Maka sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kaki kamu sekalian dengan bersilang secara bertimbal balik, dan sesungguhnya aku akan menyalib kamu sekalian pada pangkal pohon kurma dan sesungguhnya kamu akan mengetahui siapa di antara kita yang lebih pedih dan lebih kekal siksanya".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» فرعون «آمنتم» بتحقيق الهمزتين وإبدال الثانية ألفا «له قبل أن آذن» أنا «لكم إنه لكبيركم» معلمكم «الذي علمكم السحر فلأقطعن أيديكم وأرجلكم من خلاف» حال بمعنى مختلعة أي الأيدي اليمنى والأرجل اليسرى «ولأصلبنكم في جذوع النخل» أي عليها «ولتعلمن أينا» يعني نفسه ورب موسى «أشد عذابا وأبقى» أدوم على مخالفته
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِي عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحۡرَۖ فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيۡدِيَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَٰفٖ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ فِي جُذُوعِ ٱلنَّخۡلِ وَلَتَعۡلَمُنَّ أَيُّنَآ أَشَدُّ عَذَابٗا وَأَبۡقَىٰ (مِّنْ خِلَافٍ: مُخَالِفًا بِيْنَ الأَيْدِي وَالأَرْجُلِ، فَيَقْطَعُ يَدًا مِنْ جِهَةٍ، وَرِجْلًا مِنْ جِهَةٍ أُخْرَى)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah) Firaun, ("Apakah kalian telah beriman) dapat dibaca A-amantum dan Aamantum (kepada Musa sebelum aku memberi izin) sebelum aku mengizinkan (kepadamu sekalian. Sesungguhnya ia adalah pemimpin kalian) guru kalian (yang mengajarkan sihir kepada kalian. Maka sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kaki kamu sekalian dengan bersilang secara bertimbal balik) lafal Min Khilaafin berkedudukan menjadi Hal, artinya secara bersilang, yaitu tangan kanan dengan kaki kiri (dan sesungguhnya aku akan menyalib kalian pada pangkal pohon kurma) yakni pada pohon kurma (dan sesungguhnya kalian akan mengetahui siapa di antara kita) yakni diri Firaun sendiri dan Rabb Nabi Musa (yang lebih pedih dan lebih kekal siksaannya") bila ada orang yang melanggar kepadanya

ar: قَالُوا۟ لَن نُّؤۡثِرَكَ عَلَىٰ مَا جَاۤءَنَا مِنَ ٱلۡبَیِّنَـٰتِ وَٱلَّذِی فَطَرَنَاۖ فَٱقۡضِ مَاۤ أَنتَ قَاضٍۖ إِنَّمَا تَقۡضِی هَـٰذِهِ ٱلۡحَیَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنۡیَاۤ
ar_simple: قالوا لن نؤثرك على ما جاءنا من ٱلبینت وٱلذی فطرنا فٱقض ما أنت قاض إنما تقضی هذه ٱلحیوة ٱلدنیا

t.sahih-int: They said, "Never will we prefer you over what has come to us of clear proofs and [over] He who created us. So decree whatever you are to decree. You can only decree for this worldly life
t.asad: They answered: "Never shall we prefer thee to all the evidence of the truth that has come unto us, nor to Him who has brought us into being! Decree, then, whatever thou art going to decree: thou canst decree only [something that pertains to] this worldly life
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "Never shall we regard thee as more than the Clear Signs that have come to us, or than Him Who created us! so decree whatever thou desirest to decree: for thou canst only decree (touching) the life of this world
t.pickthall: They said: We choose thee not above the clear proofs that have come unto us, and above Him Who created us. So decree what thou wilt decree. Thou wilt end for us only this life of the world
t.arberry: They said, 'We will not prefer thee over the clear signs that have come to us, nor over Him who originated us. Decide then what thou wilt decide; thou canst only decide touching this present life
t.hilali-khan: They said: "We prefer you not over the clear signs that have come to us, and to Him (Allah) Who created us. So decree whatever you desire to decree, for you can only decree (regarding) this life of the world
t.itani: They said, “We will not prefer you to the proofs that have come to us, and Him who created us. So issue whatever judgment you wish to issue. You can only rule in this lowly life.
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "We prefer you not over what have come to us of the clear signs and to Him (Allah) Who created us. So, decree whatever you desire to decree, for you can only' decree (regarding) this life of the world."
t.qarib: They said, "Never will we prefer you over the clear proof that has come to us as nor [over] Him who created us. So, decree whatever you will. You can decree only for this worldly life
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Never shall we prefer you to all the evidence of the truth that has come to us. Nor to Him who has brought us into being. So decide whatever you will. Your jurisdiction only covers the life of this world
t.shakir: They said: We do not prefer you to what has come to us of clear arguments and to He Who made us, therefore decide what you are going to decide; you can only decide about this world's life
t.haleem: They said, ‘We shall never prefer you to the clear sign that has come to us, nor to Him who created us. So decide whatever you will: you can only decide matters of this present life
t.maariful: They said, “We will never prefer you over the clear signs that have come to us, and over Him who has created us. So, decide whatever you have to decide. You will decide only for this worldly life
t.khattab: They responded, “By the One Who created us! We will never prefer you over the clear proofs that have come to us. So do whatever you want! Your authority only covers the ˹fleeting˺ life of this world
t.ghali: They said, "We will never prefer you over the supreme evidences that have come to us, nor over Him Who originated us; so decree whatever you will decree; surely you will only decree touching this present life (Literally: this lowly life, i.e., the life of this world)
t.irving: They said: "We will never choose you before explanations have come to us nor ahead of the One Who originated us. Decide anything you may decide; you are no judge: You will only decide during this worldly life
t.iwq: They said, "Never will we prefer you over what has come to us of clear proofs and over He who created us. So decree whatever you are to decree. You can only decree for this worldly life

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Kami sekali-kali tidak akan mengutamakan kamu daripada bukti-bukti yang nyata (mukjizat), yang telah datang kepada kami dan daripada Tuhan yang telah menciptakan kami; maka putuskanlah apa yang hendak kamu putuskan. Sesungguhnya kamu hanya akan dapat memutuskan pada kehidupan di dunia ini saja.
t_id.sabiq: Namun, para penyihir itu tetap teguh pada keimanannya. Mereka membalas ancaman Fir'aun dengan berkata, "Kami tidak akan tetap ingkar bersamamu setelah kami membuktikan sendiri dengan jelas kebenaran mukjizat Mûsâ. Dan kami tidak akan memilihmu dengan meninggalkan Tuhan Mûsâ yang telah menciptakan kami. Lakukanlah apa yang ingin kau lakukan! Sesungguhnya kekuasaanmu tidak akan lebih panjang dari kehidupan yang singkat ini.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Kami sekali-kali tidak akan mengutamakan kamu) kami tidak akan memilih kamu (daripada bukti-bukti yang nyata yang telah datang kepada kami) bukti-bukti yang menunjukkan kebenaran Nabi Musa (dan daripada Tuhan yang telah menciptakan kami) yakni Allah yang telah menjadikan kami. Kalimat ini dapat diartikan sebagai Qasam atau sumpah atau di'athafkan kepada lafal Ma (maka putuskanlah apa yang hendak kamu putuskan) artinya lakukanlah apa yang kamu ucapkan itu. (Sesungguhnya kamu hanya akan dapat memutuskan pada kehidupan di dunia saja) dinashabkannya lafal Al-Hayaatad Dun-yaa menunjukkan makna Ittisa', maksudnya peradilan di dunia dan kelak kamu akan mendapat balasan yang setimpal di akhirat akibat dari perbuatanmu itu.
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Kami sekali-kali tidak akan mengutamakan kamu daripada bukti-bukti yang nyata (mukjizat), yang telah datang kepada kami dan daripada Tuhan yang telah menciptakan kami; maka putuskanlah apa yang hendak kamu putuskan. Sesungguhnya kamu hanya akan dapat memutuskan pada kehidupan di dunia ini saja.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا لن نؤثرك» نختارك «على ما جاءنا من البيانات» الدالة على صدق موسى «والذي فطرنا» خلفنا قسم أو عطف على ما «فاقض ما أنت قاض» أي اصنع ما قتله «إنما تقضي هذه الحياة الدنيا» النصب على الاتساع أي فيها وتجزى عليه في الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ لَن نُّؤۡثِرَكَ عَلَىٰ مَا جَآءَنَا مِنَ ٱلۡبَيِّنَٰتِ وَٱلَّذِي فَطَرَنَاۖ فَٱقۡضِ مَآ أَنتَ قَاضٍۖ إِنَّمَا تَقۡضِي هَٰذِهِ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنۡيَآ (نُّؤْثِرَكَ: نُفَضِّلَكَ, فَطَرَنَا: خَلَقَنَا وَأَبْدَعَنَا, فَاقْضِ: فَافْعَلْ وَاحْكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Kami sekali-kali tidak akan mengutamakan kamu) kami tidak akan memilih kamu (daripada bukti-bukti yang nyata yang telah datang kepada kami) bukti-bukti yang menunjukkan kebenaran Nabi Musa (dan daripada Tuhan yang telah menciptakan kami) yakni Allah yang telah menjadikan kami. Kalimat ini dapat diartikan sebagai Qasam atau sumpah atau di'athafkan kepada lafal Ma (maka putuskanlah apa yang hendak kamu putuskan) artinya lakukanlah apa yang kamu ucapkan itu. (Sesungguhnya kamu hanya akan dapat memutuskan pada kehidupan di dunia saja) dinashabkannya lafal Al-Hayaatad Dun-yaa menunjukkan makna Ittisa', maksudnya peradilan di dunia dan kelak kamu akan mendapat balasan yang setimpal di akhirat akibat dari perbuatanmu itu

ar: إِنَّاۤ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّنَا لِیَغۡفِرَ لَنَا خَطَـٰیَـٰنَا وَمَاۤ أَكۡرَهۡتَنَا عَلَیۡهِ مِنَ ٱلسِّحۡرِۗ وَٱللَّهُ خَیۡرࣱ وَأَبۡقَىٰۤ
ar_simple: إنا ءامنا بربنا لیغفر لنا خطینا وما أكرهتنا علیه من ٱلسحر وٱلله خیر وأبقى

t.sahih-int: Indeed, we have believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and what you compelled us [to do] of magic. And Allah is better and more enduring
t.asad: As for us, behold, we have come to believe in our Sustainer, [hoping] that He may forgive us our faults and all that magic unto which thou hast forced us: for God is the best [to look forward to], and the One who is truly abiding
t.yusuf-ali: For us, we have believed in our Lord: may He forgive us our faults, and the magic to which thou didst compel us: for Allah is Best and Most Abiding
t.pickthall: Lo! we believe in our Lord, that He may forgive us our sins and the magic unto which thou didst force us. Allah is better and more lasting
t.arberry: We believe in our Lord, that He may pardon us our offences, and the sorcery thou hast constrained us to practise; God is better, and more abiding
t.hilali-khan: Verily! We have believed in our Lord, that He may forgive us our faults, and the magic to which you did compel us. And Allah is better as regards reward in comparison to your [Fir'aun's (Pharaoh)] reward, and more lasting (as regards punishment in comparison to your punishment)
t.itani: We have believed in our Lord, so that He may forgive us our sins, and the magic you have compelled us to practice. God is Better, and more Lasting.”
