tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka apakah mereka tidak memperhatikan bahwa patung anak lembu itu) lafal Allaa merupakan gabungan daripada Ann yang ditakhfif dari Anna dan Laa, sedangkan isim Ann tidak disebutkan, asalnya Annahu Laa, artinya, bahwasanya anak lembu itu (tidak dapat memberi) tidak dapat memberikan (jawaban kepada mereka) yakni tidak dapat menyahuti perkataan mereka (dan tidak dapat memberi kemudaratan kepada mereka) maksudnya tidak dapat menolak kemudaratan yang menimpa mereka (dan tidak pula kemanfaatan?) tidak dapat mendatangkan kemanfaatan buat mereka. Maksudnya, mengapa mereka menjadikannya sebagai Tuhan

ar: وَلَقَدۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ هَـٰرُونُ مِن قَبۡلُ یَـٰقَوۡمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِی وَأَطِیعُوۤا۟ أَمۡرِی
ar_simple: ولقد قال لهم هرون من قبل یقوم إنما فتنتم به وإن ربكم ٱلرحمن فٱتبعونی وأطیعوا أمری

t.sahih-int: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order
t.asad: And, indeed, even before [the return of Moses] had Aaron said unto them: "O my people! You are but being tempted to evil by this [idol] -for, behold, your [only] Sustainer is the Most Gracious! Follow me, then, and obey my bidding
t.yusuf-ali: Aaron had already, before this said to them: "O my people! ye are being tested in this: for verily your Lord is (Allah) Most Gracious; so follow me and obey my command
t.pickthall: And Aaron indeed had told them beforehand: O my people! Ye are but being seduced therewith, for lo! your Lord is the Beneficent, so follow me and obey my order
t.arberry: Yet Aaron had aforetime said to them, 'My people, you have been tempted by this thing, no more; surety your Lord is the All-merciful; therefore follow me, and obey my commandment
t.hilali-khan: And Harun (Aaron) indeed had said to them beforehand: "O my people! You are being tried in this, and verily, your Lord is (Allah) the Most Beneficent, so follow me and obey my order
t.itani: Aaron had said to them before, “O my people, you are being tested by this. And your Lord is the Merciful, so follow me, and obey my command.”
t.mubarakpuri: And Harun indeed had said to them beforehand: "O my people! You are being tried in this, and verily, your Lord is (Allah) the Most Gracious, so follow me and obey my order."
t.qarib: Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "My people, you are only being tested by it, and your Lord is the Merciful-to-all, so follow me and obey my order
t.wahiduddin: Aaron had already told them, "O my people! You are only being tested by this. Your Lord is the All Merciful, so follow me and obey my command
t.shakir: And certainly Haroun had said to them before: O my people! you are only tried by it, and surely your Lord is the Beneficent Allah, therefore follow me and obey my order
t.haleem: Aaron did say to them, ‘My people, this calf is a test for you. Your true Lord is the Lord of Mercy, so follow me and obey my orders,’
t.maariful: Hārūn had already said to them, “O my people, you have only been led astray with it, and your Lord is the RaHmān (All-Merciful). So follow me and obey my command.”
t.khattab: Aaron had already warned them beforehand, “O my people! You are only being tested by this, for indeed your ˹one true˺ Lord is the Most Compassionate. So follow me and obey my orders.”
t.ghali: And Harun (Aaron) had indeed earlier said to them, "O my people, surely you have only been tempted by (this calf); and surely your Lord is The All-Merciful; so closely follow me, and obey my command
t.irving: Now Aaron had already told them: "My people, you are only being tested by means of it. Your Lord is the Mercy-giving, so follow me and obey my command
t.iwq: And Aaron had already told them before the return of Moses, "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Ever-Merciful, so follow me and obey my order

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Harun telah berkata kepada mereka sebelumnya: "Hai kaumku, sesungguhnya kamu hanya diberi cobaan dengan anak lembu. itu dan sesungguhnya Tuhanmu ialah (Tuhan) Yang Maha Pemurah, maka ikutilah aku dan taatilah perintahku".
t_id.sabiq: Ketika terjadi fitnah ini, Nabi Hârûn tinggal bersama mereka. Ia telah mengingatkan kepada mereka sebelum kembalinya Nabi Mûsâ dengan mengatakan, "Wahai kaumku, kalian telah terjerumus dalam fitnah Sâmiriy dengan kebatilan ini! Tuhan kalian yang sebenarnya adalah Allah Yang Maha Pengasih, tidak lain. Ikutilah apa yang telah aku nasihatkan kepada kalian dan laksanakanlah pendapatku dengan meninggalkan kesesatan ini!"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Harun telah berkata kepada mereka sebelumnya) sebelum Nabi Musa kembali kepada mereka ("Hai kaumku! Sesungguhnya kalian hanya diberi cobaan dengan anak lembu itu dan sesungguhnya Rabb kalian ialah Yang Maha Pemurah, maka ikutilah aku) yakni menyembah kepada-Nya (dan taatilah perintahku") di dalam menyembah kepada-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Harun telah berkata kepada mereka sebelumnya: "Hai kaumku, sesungguhnya kamu hanya diberi cobaan dengan anak lembu. itu dan sesungguhnya Tuhanmu ialah (Tuhan) Yang Maha Pemurah, maka ikutilah aku dan taatilah perintahku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد قال لهم هارون من قبل» أي قبل أن يرجع موسى «يا قوم إنما فُتنْتم به وإنَّ ربكم الرحمن فاتَّبعوني» في عبادته «وأطيعوا أمري» فيها
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ هَٰرُونُ مِن قَبۡلُ يَٰقَوۡمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِي وَأَطِيعُوٓاْ أَمۡرِي (فُتِنْتُمْ بِهِ: اُخْتُبِرْتُمْ بِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Harun telah berkata kepada mereka sebelumnya) sebelum Nabi Musa kembali kepada mereka ("Hai kaumku! Sesungguhnya kalian hanya diberi cobaan dengan anak lembu itu dan sesungguhnya Rabb kalian ialah Yang Maha Pemurah, maka ikutilah aku) yakni menyembah kepada-Nya (dan taatilah perintahku") di dalam menyembah kepada-Nya

ar: قَالُوا۟ لَن نَّبۡرَحَ عَلَیۡهِ عَـٰكِفِینَ حَتَّىٰ یَرۡجِعَ إِلَیۡنَا مُوسَىٰ
ar_simple: قالوا لن نبرح علیه عكفین حتى یرجع إلینا موسى

t.sahih-int: They said, "We will never cease being devoted to the calf until Moses returns to us
t.asad: [But] they answered: "By no means shall we cease to worship it until Moses comes back to us
t.yusuf-ali: They had said: "We will not abandon this cult, but we will devote ourselves to it until Moses returns to us
t.pickthall: They said: We shall by no means cease to be its votaries till Moses return unto us
t.arberry: We will not cease,' they said, 'to cleave to it, until Moses returns to us
t.hilali-khan: They said: "We will not stop worshipping it (i.e. the calf), until Musa (Moses) returns to us
t.itani: They said, “We will not give up our devotion to it, until Moses returns to us.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "We will not stop worshipping it, until Musa returns to us."
t.qarib: They said, "We will never cease being devoted to the calf until Moses returns to us
t.wahiduddin: They replied, "We shall not cease to worship it until Moses returns to us
t.shakir: They said: We will by no means cease to keep to its worship until Musa returns to us
t.haleem: but they replied, ‘We shall not give up our devotion to it until Moses returns to us.’
t.maariful: They said, “We will never cease to stay with it in devotion, until Mūsā returns to us.”
t.khattab: They replied, “We will not cease to worship it until Moses returns to us.”
t.ghali: They said, "We will never leave off consecrating ourselves to it, until M?s'a returns to us
t.irving: They said: "We will never quit being devoted to it until Moses returns to us
t.iwq: And they said, "We will never cease being devoted to the calf until Moses returns to us

t_id.kemenag: Mereka menjawab: "Kami akan tetap menyembah patung anak lembu ini, hingga Musa kembali kepada kami".
t_id.sabiq: Tetapi mereka berkata, "Kami akan tetap menyembah anak sapi ini sampai Mûsâ kembali kepada kami."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Kami akan tetap) kami akan terus (menyembah patung anak lembu ini) (hingga Musa kembali kepada kami").
t_id.iwq: Mereka menjawab: "Kami akan tetap menyembah patung anak lembu ini, hingga Musa kembali kepada kami".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا لن نبرح» نزال «عليه عاكفين» على عبادته مقيمين «حتى يرجع إلينا موسى»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ لَن نَّبۡرَحَ عَلَيۡهِ عَٰكِفِينَ حَتَّىٰ يَرۡجِعَ إِلَيۡنَا مُوسَىٰ (لَن نَّبْرَحَ: لَنْ نَزَالَ, عَاكِفِينَ: مُقِيمِينَ عَلَى عِبَادَتِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Kami akan tetap) kami akan terus (menyembah patung anak lembu ini) (hingga Musa kembali kepada kami)

ar: قَالَ یَـٰهَـٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذۡ رَأَیۡتَهُمۡ ضَلُّوۤا۟
ar_simple: قال یهرون ما منعك إذ رأیتهم ضلوا

t.sahih-int: [Moses] said, "O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray
t.asad: [And now that he had come back, Moses] said: "O Aaron! What has prevented thee, when thou didst see that they had gone astray
t.yusuf-ali: (Moses) said: "O Aaron! what kept thee back, when thou sawest them going wrong
t.pickthall: He (Moses) said: O Aaron! What held thee back when thou didst see them gone astray
t.arberry: Moses said, 'What prevented thee, Aaron, when thou sawest them in error
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses)] said: "O Harun (Aaron)! What stopped you when you saw them going astray
t.itani: He said, “O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray.
t.mubarakpuri: (Musa) said: "O Harun! What prevented you when you saw them going astray;"
t.qarib: [Moses] said, "Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray
t.wahiduddin: Moses said to Aaron, "What prevented you, when you saw that they had gone astray
t.shakir: (Musa) said: O Haroun! what prevented you, when you saw them going astray
t.haleem: Moses said, ‘When you realized they had gone astray, what prevented you, Aaron
t.maariful: He (Mūsā) said, “O Hārūn, what did prevent you from following me when you saw them going astray
t.khattab: Moses scolded ˹his brother˺, “O Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray
t.ghali: He (Musa) (Moses) said, "O Harun, what prevented you, when you saw them err away
t.irving: He said: "Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw they had strayed
t.iwq: Moses said, "O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Musa: "Hai Harun, apa yang menghalangi kamu ketika kamu melihat mereka telah sesat,
t_id.sabiq: Karena dipengaruhi oleh apa yang dilihatnya pada diri kaumnya, Mûsâ berkata, "Wahai Hârûn, apa yang menyebabkan kamu tidak menghentikan kesesatan mereka, ketika kamu melihat mereka telah terjerumus di dalamnya?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa) setelah ia kembali kepada mereka, ("Hai Harun! Apa yang menghalangi kamu ketika kamu melihat mereka sesat,) ketika mereka menyembah patung anak lembu itu.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Musa: "Hai Harun, apa yang menghalangi kamu ketika kamu melihat mereka telah sesat,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» موسى بعد رجوعه «يا هارون ما منعك إذ رأيتهم ضلوا» بعبادته
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ يَٰهَٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذۡ رَأَيۡتَهُمۡ ضَلُّوٓاْ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa) setelah ia kembali kepada mereka, ("Hai Harun! Apa yang menghalangi kamu ketika kamu melihat mereka sesat,) ketika mereka menyembah patung anak lembu itu

ar: أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِۖ أَفَعَصَیۡتَ أَمۡرِی
ar_simple: ألا تتبعن أفعصیت أمری

t.sahih-int: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order
t.asad: from [abandoning them and] following me? Hast thou, then, [deliberately] disobeyed my commandment
t.yusuf-ali: From following me? Didst thou then disobey my order
t.pickthall: That thou followedst me not? Hast thou then disobeyed my order
t.arberry: so that thou didst not follow after me? Didst thou then disobey my commandment
t.hilali-khan: That you followed me not (according to my advice to you)? Have you then disobeyed my order
t.itani: From following me? Did you disobey my command?”
t.mubarakpuri: "That you followed me not (according to my advice to you) Have you then disobeyed my order"
t.qarib: from following me? Have you then disobeyed my order
t.wahiduddin: from following me? Why did you disobey my command
t.shakir: So that you did not follow me? Did you then disobey my order
t.haleem: from coming after me? How could you disobey my orders?’
t.maariful: Did you then disobey my command?”
t.khattab: from following after me? How could you disobey my orders?”
t.ghali: So that you did not closely follow me? Did you then disobey my command
t.irving: from having them follow me? Did you disobey my order
t.iwq: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order

t_id.kemenag: (sehingga) kamu tidak mengikuti aku? Maka apakah kamu telah (sengaja) mendurhakai perintahku?"
t_id.sabiq: Dan mengapa pula kamu tidak mengambil posisiku untuk memberikan nasihat sebagaimana yang telah aku katakan kepadamu? Apakah kamu tidak mau mengikutiku pada apa yang telah aku percayakan kepadamu, atau apakah kamu sengaja mendurhakai perintahku?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sehingga kamu tidak mengikuti aku?) huruf Laa di sini Zaidah. (Maka apakah kamu telah sengaja mendurhakai perintahku?") disebabkan kamu tinggal diam di antara orang-orang yang menyembah selain Allah swt.
t_id.iwq: (sehingga) kamu tidak mengikuti aku? Maka apakah kamu telah (sengaja) mendurhakai perintahku?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أ» ن «لا تتبعن» لا زائدة «أفعصيت أمري» بإقامتك بين من يعبد غير الله تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِۖ أَفَعَصَيۡتَ أَمۡرِي
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sehingga kamu tidak mengikuti aku?) huruf Laa di sini Zaidah. (Maka apakah kamu telah sengaja mendurhakai perintahku?") disebabkan kamu tinggal diam di antara orang-orang yang menyembah selain Allah swt

ar: قَالَ یَبۡنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأۡخُذۡ بِلِحۡیَتِی وَلَا بِرَأۡسِیۤۖ إِنِّی خَشِیتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقۡتَ بَیۡنَ بَنِیۤ إِسۡرَ ٰۤءِیلَ وَلَمۡ تَرۡقُبۡ قَوۡلِی
ar_simple: قال یبنؤم لا تأخذ بلحیتی ولا برأسی إنی خشیت أن تقول فرقت بین بنی إسر ءیل ولم ترقب قولی

t.sahih-int: [Aaron] said, "O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe [or await] my word
t.asad: Answered [Aaron]: "O my mother's son! Seize me not by my beard, nor by my head! Behold, I was afraid lest [on thy return] thou say, 'Thou hast caused a split among the children of Israel, and hast paid no heed to my bidding
t.yusuf-ali: (Aaron) replied: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard nor by (the hair of) my head! Truly I feared lest thou shouldst say, 'Thou has caused a division among the children of Israel, and thou didst not respect my word
t.pickthall: He said: O son of my mother! Clutch not my beard nor my head! I feared lest thou shouldst say: Thou hast caused division among the Children of Israel, and hast not waited for my word
t.arberry: Son of my mother,' Aaron said, 'take me not by the beard, or the head! I was fearful that thou wouldst say, "Thou hast divided the Children of Israel, and thou hast not observed my word
t.hilali-khan: He [Harun (Aaron)] said: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard, nor by my head! Verily, I feared lest you should say: 'You have caused a division among the Children of Israel, and you have not respected my word
t.itani: He said, “Son of my mother, do not seize me by my beard or my head. I feared you would say, `You have caused division among the Children of Israel, and did not regard my word.'“
t.mubarakpuri: He (Harun) said: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard, nor by my head! Verily, I feared lest you should say: `You have caused a division among the Children of Israel, and you have not respected my word!"'
t.qarib: [Aaron] said, "Son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. I feared that you would say, You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not remember my words
t.wahiduddin: Aaron said, "Son of my mother! Do not seize me by my beard nor by my head. I was afraid that you would say, 'You have caused dissension among the Children of Israel and did not pay heed to my words
t.shakir: He said: O son of my mother! seize me not by my beard nor by my head; surely I was afraid lest you should say: You have caused a division among the children of Israel and not waited for my word
t.haleem: He said, ‘Son of my mother- let go of my beard and my hair!- I was afraid you would say, “You have caused division among the children of Israel and have not heeded what I said.”’
t.maariful: He said, “O son of my mother, do not hold me by my beard, nor by my head. I feared that you would say, ‘You have caused discord among the children of Isrā’īl and did not observe my advice.’”
t.khattab: Aaron pleaded, “O son of my mother! Do not seize me by my beard or ˹the hair of˺ my head. I really feared that you would say, ‘You have caused division among the Children of Israel, and did not observe my word.’”
t.ghali: He (Harun) said, "O son of my mother, do not take hold of my beard or of my head! I was apprehensive that you would say, "You have caused disunity among the Seeds (Or: sons) of Israel) and you have not respected my saying
t.irving: He said: "My (blood) brother, do not seize me by my beard nor by my head! I dreaded you would say: ´You have brought dissension to the Children of Israel and did not observe what I said."´
t.iwq: Aaron said, "O son of my mother, do not seize me by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe or await my word

t_id.kemenag: Harun menjawab' "Hai putera ibuku, janganlah kamu pegang janggutku dan jangan (pula) kepalaku; sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan berkata (kepadaku): "Kamu telah memecah antara Bani Israil dan kamu tidak memelihara amanatku".
t_id.sabiq: Hârûn berkata kepada Mûsâ, "Wahai putra ibuku, jangan tergesa-gesa memarahiku! Jangan kau pegang jenggot dan kepalaku! Aku benar-benar khawatir kalau aku bersikap keras kepada mereka lalu mereka terpecah menjadi beberapa kelompok, kamu akan berkata kepadaku, "Kau telah memecah Banû Isrâ'îl dan tidak menggantikan aku di antara mereka sebagaimana yang aku percayakan kepadamu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah) Harun ("Hai putra ibuku!) dapat dibaca Ummi dan Umma maksudnya ibuku, Nabi Harun sengaja menyebut nama ini untuk mengharapkan belas kasihan dari Nabi Musa (Janganlah kamu pegang janggutku) Nabi Musa memegang janggut Nabi Harun dengan tangan kirinya (dan jangan pula kepalaku) Nabi Musa memegang rambut kepala Nabi Harun dengan tangan kanannya sebagai pelampiasan kemarahannya (sesungguhnya aku khawatir) seandainya aku mengikutimu, maka pastilah akan mengikutiku pula segolongan orang-orang yang menyembah anak lembu itu (bahwa kamu akan berkata kepadaku, 'Kamu telah memecah belah antara Bani Israel) lalu kamu marah kepadaku (dan kamu tidak menunggu) (perintahku") di dalam mengambil sikap.
t_id.iwq: Harun menjawab' "Hai putera ibuku, janganlah kamu pegang janggutku dan jangan (pula) kepalaku; sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan berkata (kepadaku): "Kamu telah memecah antara Bani Israil dan kamu tidak memelihara amanatku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» هارون «يا ابن أم» بكسر الميم وفتحها أراد أمي وذكرها أَعْطَف لقلبه «لا تأخذ بلحيتي» وكان أخذها بشماله «ولا برأسي» وكان أخذ شعره بيمينه غضبا «إني خشيت» لو اتبعك ولا بد أن يتبعني جمع ممن لم يعبدوا العجل «أن تقول فرقت بين بني إسرائيل» وتغضب عليَّ «ولم ترقب» تنتظر «قولي» فيما رأيته في ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ يَبۡنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأۡخُذۡ بِلِحۡيَتِي وَلَا بِرَأۡسِيٓۖ إِنِّي خَشِيتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقۡتَ بَيۡنَ بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ وَلَمۡ تَرۡقُبۡ قَوۡلِي (وَلَمْ تَرْقُبْ قَوْلِي: لَمْ تَحْفَظْ وَصِيَّتِي بِحُسْنِ رِعَايَتِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah) Harun ("Hai putra ibuku!) dapat dibaca Ummi dan Umma maksudnya ibuku, Nabi Harun sengaja menyebut nama ini untuk mengharapkan belas kasihan dari Nabi Musa (Janganlah kamu pegang janggutku) Nabi Musa memegang janggut Nabi Harun dengan tangan kirinya (dan jangan pula kepalaku) Nabi Musa memegang rambut kepala Nabi Harun dengan tangan kanannya sebagai pelampiasan kemarahannya (sesungguhnya aku khawatir) seandainya aku mengikutimu, maka pastilah akan mengikutiku pula segolongan orang-orang yang menyembah anak lembu itu (bahwa kamu akan berkata kepadaku, 'Kamu telah memecah belah antara Bani Israel) lalu kamu marah kepadaku (dan kamu tidak menunggu) (perintahku") di dalam mengambil sikap

ar: قَالَ فَمَا خَطۡبُكَ یَـٰسَـٰمِرِیُّ
ar_simple: قال فما خطبك یسمری

t.sahih-int: [Moses] said, "And what is your case, O Samiri
t.asad: Said [Moses]: "What, then, didst thou have in view, O Samaritan
t.yusuf-ali: (Moses) said: "What then is thy case, O Samiri
t.pickthall: (Moses) said: And what hast thou to say, O Samiri
t.arberry: Moses said, 'And thou, Samaritan, what was thy business
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses)] said: "And what is the matter with you. O Samiri? (i.e. why did you do so)
t.itani: He said, “What do you have to say, O Samarian?”
t.mubarakpuri: (Musa) said: "And what is the matter with you. O Samiri"
t.qarib: [Moses] said, "And what is the matter with you Samaritan
t.wahiduddin: Moses said, "What was the matter with you, Samiri
t.shakir: He said: What was then your object, O Samiri
t.haleem: Moses said, ‘And what was the matter with you, Samiri?’
t.maariful: He (Mūsā) said, “What then is your case, O Sāmiriyy?”
t.khattab: Moses then asked, “What did you think you were doing, O Sâmiri?”
t.ghali: He (M?s'a) said, "Then what is your concern, O Samiriyy
t.irving: He said: "What have you been trying to do, 0 Samaritan
t.iwq: Moses said, "And what is your case, O Samiri

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Musa: "Apakah yang mendorongmu (berbuat demikian) hai Samiri?"
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ berkata kepada Sâmiriy, "Ada apa denganmu, Sâmiriy?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: Berkata Musa, "Apakah yang mendorongmu) berbuat demikian (hai Samiri?")
t_id.iwq: Berkata Musa: "Apakah yang mendorongmu (berbuat demikian) hai Samiri?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال فما خطبك» شأنك الداعي إلى ما صنعت «يا سامري»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ فَمَا خَطۡبُكَ يَٰسَٰمِرِيُّ (فَمَا خَطْبُكَ: فَمَا شَانُكَ الخَطِيرُ؟)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa, "Apakah yang mendorongmu) berbuat demikian (hai Samiri)

ar: قَالَ بَصُرۡتُ بِمَا لَمۡ یَبۡصُرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضۡتُ قَبۡضَةࣰ مِّنۡ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذۡتُهَا وَكَذَ ٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتۡ لِی نَفۡسِی
ar_simple: قال بصرت بما لم یبصروا به فقبضت قبضة من أثر ٱلرسول فنبذتها وكذ لك سولت لی نفسی

t.sahih-int: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me
t.asad: He answered: "I have gained insight into something which they were unable to see: and so I took hold of a handful of the Apostle's teachings and cast it away: for thus has my mind prompted me [to act]
t.yusuf-ali: He replied: "I saw what they saw not: so I took a handful (of dust) from the footprint of the Messenger, and threw it (into the calf): thus did my soul suggest to me
t.pickthall: He said: I perceived what they perceive not, so I seized a handful from the footsteps of the messenger, and then threw it in. Thus my soul commended to me
t.arberry: I beheld what they beheld not,' he said, 'and I seized a handful of dust from the messenger's track, and cast it into the thing. So my soul prompted me
t.hilali-khan: (Samiri) said: "I saw what they saw not, so I took a handful (of dust) from the hoof print of the messenger [Jibrael's (Gabriel) horse] and threw it [into the fire in which were put the ornaments of the Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) people, or into the calf]. Thus my inner-self suggested to me
t.itani: He said, “I saw what they did not see, so I grasped a handful from the Messenger’s traces, and I flung it away. Thus my soul prompted me.”
t.mubarakpuri: (Samiri) said: "I saw what they saw not, so I took a (Qabdah) handful (of dust) from the (hoof) print of the messenger and threw it. Thus my inner self suggested to me."
t.qarib: He said, "I gained insight into what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the messenger's track and threw it in. This is what my soul tempted me to do
t.wahiduddin: He said, "I perceived what they did not see. So I took a handful [of dust] from the footprint of the Messenger and threw it in [the calf]. That is what my inner self prompted me to do
t.shakir: He said: I saw (Jibreel) what they did not see, so I took a handful (of the dust) from the footsteps of the messenger, then I threw it in the casting; thus did my soul commend to me
t.haleem: He replied, ‘I saw something they did not; I took in some of the teachings of the Messenger but tossed them aside: my soul prompted me to do what I did.’
t.maariful: He said, “I perceived something they did not perceive. So I picked up a handful from under the footstep of the messenger. Then, I cast it. And thus my inner self tempted me.”
t.khattab: He said, “I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful ˹of dust˺ from the hoof-prints of ˹the horse of˺ the messenger-angel ˹Gabriel˺ then cast it ˹on the moulded calf˺. This is what my lower-self tempted me into.”
t.ghali: Said he, "I beheld what they did not behold; so I grasped a handful (Literally: I grasped a grasp) (of dust) from the messenger's track; then flung it off. Thus my self instigated (that) to me
t.irving: He said: "I noticed something they do not notice, so I snatched a handful from the messenger´s footprints and flung it away. Thus my soul has lured me on
t.iwq: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful of dust from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me

t_id.kemenag: Samiri menjawab: "Aku mengetahui sesuatu yang mereka tidak mengetahuinya, maka aku ambil segenggam dari jejak rasul lalu aku melemparkannya, dan demikianlah nafsuku membujukku".
t_id.sabiq: Sâmiriy menjawab, "Aku memiliki keterampilan dan cara-cara pembuatan yang tidak diketahui Banû Isrâ'îl. Aku telah membuat patung dalam bentuk anak sapi yang mengeluarkan suara seperti ini, dan aku ambil segenggam dari Tawrât, lalu aku lemparkan ke dalam perut patung anak sapi itu, agar manusia ragu. Demikianlah nafsuku membujukku untuk melakukan apa yang aku lakukan."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Samiri menjawab, "Aku mengetahui sesuatu yang mereka tidak mengetahuinya) dapat dibaca Yabshuruu dan Tabshuruu, artinya, aku mengetahui apa yang tidak mereka ketahui (maka aku ambil segenggam dari) tanah (bekas) jejak teracak kuda (rasul) yakni malaikat Jibril (lalu aku melemparkannya) yakni aku menaruhnya pada patung anak lembu yang telah dicetak (dan demikianlah telah menggoda) telah membujuk (aku hawa nafsuku") untuk menaruhnya ke dalam tubuhnya, yaitu setelah terlebih dahulu aku mengambil segenggam tanah bekas jejak teracak kuda malaikat Jibril. Lalu tanah itu aku taruh ke dalam patung yang tidak bernyawa, hingga patung itu menjadi hidup bagaikan ada rohnya. Kemudian aku melihat bahwa kaummu meminta kepadamu untuk menjadikan tuhan buat mereka. Lalu hatiku menggodaku untuk menjadikan patung anak lembu itu sebagai tuhan mereka.
t_id.iwq: Samiri menjawab: "Aku mengetahui sesuatu yang mereka tidak mengetahuinya, maka aku ambil segenggam dari jejak rasul lalu aku melemparkannya, dan demikianlah nafsuku membujukku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال بصرت بما لم يبصروا به» بالياء والتاء أي علمت ما لم يعلموه «فقبضت قبضة من» تراب «أثر» حافر فرس «الرسول» جبريل «فنبذتها» ألقيتها في صورة العجل المصاغ «وكذلك سولت» زينت «لي نفسي» وألقى فيها أن أخذ قبضة من تراب ما ذكر، وألقيها على ما لا روح، به يصير له روح ورأيت قومك طلبوا منك أن تجعل لهم إلها فحدثتني نفسي أن يكون ذلك العجل إلههم
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ بَصُرۡتُ بِمَا لَمۡ يَبۡصُرُواْ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضۡتُ قَبۡضَةٗ مِّنۡ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذۡتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتۡ لِي نَفۡسِي (بَصُرْتُ: رَأَيْتُ أَوْ عَلِمْتُ بِبَصِيرَتي, فَقَبَضْتُ قَبْضَةً: أَخَذْتُ بِكَفِّي تُرَابًا, مِّنْ أَثَرِ الرَّسُولِ: مِنْ أَثَرِ حَافِرِ فَرَسِ جِبْرِيلَ - عليه السلام -, فَنَبَذْتُهَا: أَلْقَيْتُهَا عَلَى الحُلِيِّ, سَوَّلَتْ: زَيَّنَتْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Samiri menjawab, "Aku mengetahui sesuatu yang mereka tidak mengetahuinya) dapat dibaca Yabshuruu dan Tabshuruu, artinya, aku mengetahui apa yang tidak mereka ketahui (maka aku ambil segenggam dari) tanah (bekas) jejak teracak kuda (rasul) yakni malaikat Jibril (lalu aku melemparkannya) yakni aku menaruhnya pada patung anak lembu yang telah dicetak (dan demikianlah telah menggoda) telah membujuk (aku hawa nafsuku") untuk menaruhnya ke dalam tubuhnya, yaitu setelah terlebih dahulu aku mengambil segenggam tanah bekas jejak teracak kuda malaikat Jibril. Lalu tanah itu aku taruh ke dalam patung yang tidak bernyawa, hingga patung itu menjadi hidup bagaikan ada rohnya. Kemudian aku melihat bahwa kaummu meminta kepadamu untuk menjadikan tuhan buat mereka. Lalu hatiku menggodaku untuk menjadikan patung anak lembu itu sebagai tuhan mereka

ar: قَالَ فَٱذۡهَبۡ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِی ٱلۡحَیَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوۡعِدࣰا لَّن تُخۡلَفَهُۥۖ وَٱنظُرۡ إِلَىٰۤ إِلَـٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِی ظَلۡتَ عَلَیۡهِ عَاكِفࣰاۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِی ٱلۡیَمِّ نَسۡفًا
ar_simple: قال فٱذهب فإن لك فی ٱلحیوة أن تقول لا مساس وإن لك موعدا لن تخلفه وٱنظر إلى إلهك ٱلذی ظلت علیه عاكفا لنحرقنه ثم لننسفنه فی ٱلیم نسفا

t.sahih-int: [Moses] said, "Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it into the sea with a blast
t.asad: Said [Moses]; "Begone, then! And, behold, it shall be thy lot to say throughout [thy] life, `Touch me not! But, verily, [in the life to come] thou shalt be faced with a destiny from which there will be no escape! And [now] look at this deity of thine to whose worship thou hast become so devoted: we shall most certainly burn it, and then scatter [whatever remains of] it far and wide over the sea
t.yusuf-ali: (Moses) said: "Get thee gone! but thy (punishment) in this life will be that thou wilt say, 'touch me not'; and moreover (for a future penalty) thou hast a promise that will not fail: Now look at thy god, of whom thou hast become a devoted worshipper: We will certainly (melt) it in a blazing fire and scatter it broadcast in the sea
t.pickthall: (Moses) said: Then go! and lo! in this life it is for thee to say: Touch me not! and lo! there is for thee a tryst thou canst not break. Now look upon thy god of which thou hast remained a votary. Verily we will burn it and will scatter its dust over the sea
t.arberry: Depart!' said Moses. 'It shall be thine all this life to cry "Untouchable!" And thereafter a tryst awaits thee thou canst not fail to keep Behold thy god, to whom all the day thou wast cleaving! We will surely burn it and scatter its ashes into the sea
t.hilali-khan: Musa (Moses) said: "Then go away! And verily, your (punishment) in this life will be that you will say: "Touch me not (i.e. you will live alone exiled away from mankind); and verily (for a future torment), you have a promise that will not fail. And look at your ilah (god), to which you have been devoted. We will certainly burn it, and scatter its particles in the sea
t.itani: He said, “Begone! Your lot in this life is to say, ‘No contact.’ And you have an appointment that you will not miss. Now look at your god that you remained devoted to—we will burn it up, and then blow it away into the sea, as powder.”
t.mubarakpuri: Musa said: "Then go away! And verily, your (punishment) in this life will be that you will say: `Touch me not;' and verily, you have a promise that will not fail. And look at your god to which you have been devoted. We will certainly burn it, and scatter its particles in the sea."
t.qarib: [Moses] said, "Get out of here. All that you can ask for in this life is not to be physically harmed. But you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. Look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will burn it up and we will scatter its dust into the sea
t.wahiduddin: Moses said, "Begone! It shall be your lot to say throughout your life, "Do not touch me," and you will be faced with a fate from which there will be no escape. Now look at your deity to which you have become so devoted: we shall burn it up, and then scatter it into the sea
t.shakir: He said: Begone then, surely for you it will be in this life to say, Touch (me) not; and surely there is a threat for you, which shall not be made to fail to you, and look at your god to whose worship you kept (so long); we will certainly burn it, then we will certainly scatter it a (wide) scattering in the sea
t.haleem: Moses said, ‘Get away from here! Your lot in this life is to say, “Do not touch me,” but you have an appointment from which there is no escape. Look at your god which you have kept on worshipping- we shall grind it down and scatter it into the sea
t.maariful: He (Mūsā) said, “Then go away; it is destined for you that, throughout your life, you will say: ‘Do not touch me’. And, of course, you have another promise that will not be broken for you. And look at your god to which you stayed devoted. We will certainly burn it, then we will scatter it thoroughly in the sea
t.khattab: Moses said, “Go away then! And for ˹the rest of your˺ life you will surely be crying, ‘Do not touch ˹me˺!’ Then you will certainly have a fate that you cannot escape. Now look at your god to which you have been devoted: we will burn it up, then scatter it in the sea completely.”
t.ghali: He (Musa) (Moses) said, "Then go! Yet, surely it shall be for you in (this) life to say, ‘Untouchable!’ Literally: no mutual contact, i.e., As-Samiriyy was made untouchable) And surely there is for you an appointment you will never be allowed to fail; and behold your god, to whom you lingered on consecrating yourself! Indeed we will definitely burn it away; thereafter indeed we will definitely crush it (into powder) (Literally: crush it "an utter" crushing) into the main
t.irving: He said: "Go away! During your lifetime you will say: ´Don´t touch [me]!´ You have an appointment [for your torment] which will not be broken. Look at your god which you have become so devoted to: we shall burn it up, then scatter its remains in the flood
t.iwq: Moses said, "Then go. And indeed, it is decreed for you in this life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment in the Hereafter you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it into the sea with a blast

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Musa: "Pergilah kamu, maka sesungguhnya bagimu di dalam kehidupan di dunia ini (hanya dapat) mengatakan: "Janganlah menyentuh (aku)". Dan sesungguhnya bagimu hukuman (di akhirat) yang kamu sekali-kali tidak dapat menghindarinya, dan lihatlah tuhanmu itu yang kamu tetap menyembahnya. Sesungguhnya kami akan membakarnya, kemudian kami sungguh-sungguh akan menghamburkannya ke dalam laut (berupa abu yang berserakan).
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ berkata lagi, "Keluarlah kamu dari kelompok kami dan jauhilah kami! Kau akan mendapat balasan di dunia sebagai orang terhina dan dijauhi manusia. Dengan begitu, tak ada hubungan lagi antara kamu dengan mereka. Tidak ada seorang pun akan mendekatimu dan kau un tidak dapat mendekati seseorang. Lebih dari itu, siksaanmu di akhirat sudah pula ditentukan. Dan kamu tidak akan dapat menghindarinya." Mûsâ kemudian mencemooh Sâmiriy dan tuhanya seraya berkata, "Lihatlah sekarang apa yang akan kami perbuat terhadap tuhan yang kamu sembah dan kamu jadikan alat untuk menggoda manusia. Kami akan membakar dan menghamburkannya ke laut."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa) kepada Samiri, ("Pergilah kamu) dari kalangan kami ini (maka sesungguhnya bagimu di dalam kehidupan di dunia ini) selama kamu hidup di dalamnya (hanya dapat mengatakan) kepada orang-orang yang kamu bertemu dengannya, ('Janganlah menyentuhku') janganlah kamu mendekat kepadaku. Dan disebutkan bahwa sejak saat itu Samiri mengembara tanpa tujuan dan jika ada seseorang menyentuhnya atau dia menyentuhnya, maka semuanya kena penyakit demam. (Dan sesungguhnya bagimu telah ada ketentuan waktu) bagi hukumanmu (yang kamu sekali-kali tidak dapat menghindarinya) jika dibaca Lan tukhlifahu artinya, kamu tidak dapat selamat dari azab itu. Dan jika dibaca Lan Tukhlafahu artinya, kamu dibangkitkan kelak di hari kiamat untuk diazab (dan lihatlah tuhanmu itu yang kamu tetap) lafal Zhalta asalnya dibaca Zhalilta, kemudian Lam yang pertama dibuang sehingga jadilah Zhalta artinya yang kamu selamanya (menyembah kepadanya) tetap menyembahnya. (Sesungguhnya kami akan membakarnya) dengan api (kemudian kami sungguh-sungguh akan menghambur-hamburkannya ke dalam laut) berupa abu yang berserakan terbawa oleh angin laut. Dan Nabi Musa mengerjakan apa yang telah dikatakannya itu setelah terlebih dahulu menyembelihnya.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Musa: "Pergilah kamu, maka sesungguhnya bagimu di dalam kehidupan di dunia ini (hanya dapat) mengatakan: "Janganlah menyentuh (aku)". Dan sesungguhnya bagimu hukuman (di akhirat) yang kamu sekali-kali tidak dapat menghindarinya, dan lihatlah tuhanmu itu yang kamu tetap menyembahnya. Sesungguhnya kami akan membakarnya, kemudian kami sungguh-sungguh akan menghamburkannya ke dalam laut (berupa abu yang berserakan).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» له موسى «فاذهب» من بيننا «فإن لك في الحياة» أي مدة حياتك «أن تقول» لمن رأيته «لا مساس» أي لا تقربني فكان بهم في البرية وإذا مس أحدا أو مسه أحد حُمَّا جميعا «وإن لك موعدا» لعذابك «لن تخلفه» بكسر اللام: أي لن تغيب عنه، وبفتحها أي بل تبعث إليه «وانظر إلى إلهك الذي ظلْت» أصله ظللت بلامين أولاهما مكسورة حذفت تخفيفا أي دمت «عليه عاكفا» أي مقيما تعبده «لنحرقنه» بالنار «ثم لننسفنه في اليمّ نسفا» نذرينه في هواء البحر، وفعل موسى بعد ذبحه ما ذكره
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ فَٱذۡهَبۡ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِي ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوۡعِدٗا لَّن تُخۡلَفَهُۥۖ وَٱنظُرۡ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِي ظَلۡتَ عَلَيۡهِ عَاكِفٗاۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ نَسۡفًا (لَا مِسَاسَ: أَيْ: تَكُونَ مَنْبُوذًا؛ تَقُولُ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ: لَا أَمَسُّكَ، وَلَا تَمَسُّنِي, ظَلْتَ: أَقَمْتَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkata Musa) kepada Samiri, ("Pergilah kamu) dari kalangan kami ini (maka sesungguhnya bagimu di dalam kehidupan di dunia ini) selama kamu hidup di dalamnya (hanya dapat mengatakan) kepada orang-orang yang kamu bertemu dengannya, ('Janganlah menyentuhku') janganlah kamu mendekat kepadaku. Dan disebutkan bahwa sejak saat itu Samiri mengembara tanpa tujuan dan jika ada seseorang menyentuhnya atau dia menyentuhnya, maka semuanya kena penyakit demam. (Dan sesungguhnya bagimu telah ada ketentuan waktu) bagi hukumanmu (yang kamu sekali-kali tidak dapat menghindarinya) jika dibaca Lan tukhlifahu artinya, kamu tidak dapat selamat dari azab itu. Dan jika dibaca Lan Tukhlafahu artinya, kamu dibangkitkan kelak di hari kiamat untuk diazab (dan lihatlah tuhanmu itu yang kamu tetap) lafal Zhalta asalnya dibaca Zhalilta, kemudian Lam yang pertama dibuang sehingga jadilah Zhalta artinya yang kamu selamanya (menyembah kepadanya) tetap menyembahnya. (Sesungguhnya kami akan membakarnya) dengan api (kemudian kami sungguh-sungguh akan menghambur-hamburkannya ke dalam laut) berupa abu yang berserakan terbawa oleh angin laut. Dan Nabi Musa mengerjakan apa yang telah dikatakannya itu setelah terlebih dahulu menyembelihnya

ar: إِنَّمَاۤ إِلَـٰهُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِی لَاۤ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَۚ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَیۡءٍ عِلۡمࣰا
ar_simple: إنما إلهكم ٱلله ٱلذی لا إله إلا هو وسع كل شیء علما

t.sahih-int: Your god is only Allah, except for whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge
t.asad: Your only deity is God - He save whom there is no deity, [and whho] embraces all things within His knowledge
t.yusuf-ali: But the god of you all is the One Allah: there is no god but He: all things He comprehends in His knowledge
t.pickthall: Your Allah is only Allah, than Whom there is no other Allah. He embraceth all things in His knowledge
t.arberry: Your God is only the One God; there is no god, but He alone who in His knowledge embraces everything
t.hilali-khan: Your Ilah (God) is only Allah, the One (La ilaha illa Huwa) (none has the right to be worshipped but He). He has full knowledge of all things
t.itani: Surely your god is God, the One besides whom there is no other god. He comprehends everything in knowledge.
t.mubarakpuri: Your God is only Allah, there is no God but Him. He has full knowledge of all things.
t.qarib: [People] your true god is God; there is no god but Him. His knowledge encompasses all things
t.wahiduddin: Your only deity is God, there is no deity but Him. His knowledge encompasses all things
t.shakir: Your Allah is only Allah, there is no god but He; He comprehends all things in (His) knowledge
t.haleem: [People], your true god is the One God- there is no god but Him- whose knowledge embraces everything.’
t.maariful: Your God is Allah other than whom there is no god. He encompasses everything with knowledge.”
t.khattab: ˹Then Moses addressed his people,˺ “Your only god is Allah, there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. He encompasses everything in ˹His˺ knowledge.”
t.ghali: Surely your God is only Allah; there is no god except He (alone); He has embraced everything (in) His Knowledge
t.irving: Your God is God [Alone]; there is no other deity than Him! He is Vaster than everything in knowledge
t.iwq: Your god is only Allah, except for whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Tuhanmu hanyalah Allah, yang tidak ada Tuhan selain Dia. Pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu".
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ benar-benar melaksanakan apa yang diucapkannya. Setelah mengambil pelajaran dari peristiwa ini, ia kemudian menemui Banû Isrâ'îl dan berkata, "Sesungguhnya Tuhan kalian Yang Mahaesa, adalah Tuhan yang tidak boleh disembah bersama tuhan-tuhan lain. Pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu, baik yang lampau maupun yang akan datang."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Tuhan kalian hanyalah Allah yang tidak ada Tuhan selain Dia, pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu) lafal 'Ilman adalah Tamyiz yang dipindahkan dari bentuk Fa'ilnya, artinya, pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Tuhanmu hanyalah Allah, yang tidak ada Tuhan selain Dia. Pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنما إلهكم الله الذي لا اله إلا هو وسع كل شيء علما» تمييز محول عن الفاعل وسع علمه كل شيء
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّمَآ إِلَٰهُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِي لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَۚ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ عِلۡمٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Tuhan kalian hanyalah Allah yang tidak ada Tuhan selain Dia, pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu) lafal 'Ilman adalah Tamyiz yang dipindahkan dari bentuk Fa'ilnya, artinya, pengetahuan-Nya meliputi segala sesuatu

ar: كَذَ ٰلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَیۡكَ مِنۡ أَنۢبَاۤءِ مَا قَدۡ سَبَقَۚ وَقَدۡ ءَاتَیۡنَـٰكَ مِن لَّدُنَّا ذِكۡرࣰا
ar_simple: كذ لك نقص علیك من أنباء ما قد سبق وقد ءاتینك من لدنا ذكرا

t.sahih-int: Thus, [O Muhammad], We relate to you from the news of what has preceded. And We have certainly given you from Us the Qur'an
t.asad: THUS DO WE relate unto thee some of the stories of what happened in the past; and [thus] have We vouchsafed unto thee, out of Our grace, a reminder
t.yusuf-ali: Thus do We relate to thee some stories of what happened before: for We have sent thee a Message from Our own Presence
t.pickthall: Thus relate We unto thee (Muhammad) some tidings of that which happened of old, and We have given thee from Our presence a reminder
t.arberry: So We relate to thee stories of what has gone before, and We have given thee a remembrance from Us
t.hilali-khan: Thus We relate to you (O Muhammad SAW) some information of what happened before. And indeed We have given you from Us a Reminder (this Quran)
t.itani: Thus We narrate to you reports of times gone by; and We have given you a message from Our Presence.
t.mubarakpuri: Thus We relate to you some information of what happened before. And indeed We have given you from Us a Reminder.
t.qarib: In this way We tell you [Prophet] the news of what has happened before, and We have given you a Reminder directly from Us
t.wahiduddin: Thus We relate to you the history of past events, and We have given you a reminder [the Quran] from Us
t.shakir: Thus do We relate to you (some) of the news of what has gone before; and indeed We have given to you a Reminder from Ourselves
t.haleem: In this way We relate to you [Prophet] stories of what happened before. We have given you a Quran from Us
t.maariful: This is how We narrate to you certain events of what has passed, and We have given to you, from Ourself, a (book of) advice
t.khattab: This is how We relate to you ˹O Prophet˺ some of the stories of the past. And We have certainly granted you a Reminder from Us
t.ghali: Thus We narrate to you (some) tidings of what has gone before; and We have already brought you a Remembrance from very close to Us
t.irving: Thus We relate some news to you about what has occurred previously; We have even brought you a Reminder from Our very presence
t.iwq: Thus, O Muhammad, We relate to you from the news of what has preceded. And We have certainly given you from Us the Qur'an

t_id.kemenag: Demikianlah kami kisahkan kepadamu (Muhammad) sebagian kisah umat yang telah lalu, dan sesungguhnya telah Kami berikan kepadamu dari sisi Kami suatu peringatan (Al Quran).
t_id.sabiq: Seperti Kami menceritakan kepadamu, Muhammad, kisah Mûsâ, Kami pun menceritakan dengan benar kisah umat-umat terdahulu lainnya. Dan Kami telah menurunkan kepadamu sebuah kitab suci yang mengandung peringatan bagimu dan umatmu, juga mengandung kebaikan agama dan dunia kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Demikianlah) sebagaimana Kami telah mengisahkan cerita ini kepadamu, hai Muhammad (Kami kisahkan kepadamu sebagian kisah) berita (umat yang telah silam) yakni bangsa-bangsa di masa lampau (dan sesungguhnya telah Kami berikan kepadamu) yakni Kami telah memberimu (dari sisi Kami) yakni dari hadirat Kami (suatu peringatan) yakni Alquran.
t_id.iwq: Demikianlah kami kisahkan kepadamu (Muhammad) sebagian kisah umat yang telah lalu, dan sesungguhnya telah Kami berikan kepadamu dari sisi Kami suatu peringatan (Al Al-Quran).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كذلك» أي كما قصصنا عليك يا محمد هذه القصة «نقص عليك من أنباء» أخبار «ما قد سبق» من الأمم «وقد آتيناك» أعطيناك «من لدنا» من عندنا «ذكرا» قرآنا
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَذَٰلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَيۡكَ مِنۡ أَنۢبَآءِ مَا قَدۡ سَبَقَۚ وَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَٰكَ مِن لَّدُنَّا ذِكۡرٗا (مِن لَّدُنَّا: مِنْ عِنْدِنَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Demikianlah) sebagaimana Kami telah mengisahkan cerita ini kepadamu, hai Muhammad (Kami kisahkan kepadamu sebagian kisah) berita (umat yang telah silam) yakni bangsa-bangsa di masa lampau (dan sesungguhnya telah Kami berikan kepadamu) yakni Kami telah memberimu (dari sisi Kami) yakni dari hadirat Kami (suatu peringatan) yakni Alquran

ar: مَّنۡ أَعۡرَضَ عَنۡهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ یَحۡمِلُ یَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِیَـٰمَةِ وِزۡرًا
ar_simple: من أعرض عنه فإنه یحمل یوم ٱلقیمة وزرا

t.sahih-int: Whoever turns away from it - then indeed, he will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden
t.asad: All who shall turn away from it will, verily, bear a [heavy] burden on the Day of Resurrection
t.yusuf-ali: If any do turn away therefrom, verily they will bear a burden on the Day of judgment
t.pickthall: Whoso turneth away from it, he verily will bear a burden on the Day of Resurrection
t.arberry: Whosoever turns away from it, upon the Day of Resurrection He shall bear a fardel
t.hilali-khan: Whoever turns away from it (this Quran i.e. does not believe in it, nor acts on its orders), verily, they will bear a heavy burden (of sins) on the Day of Resurrection
t.itani: Whoever turns away from it will carry on the Day of Resurrection a burden.
t.mubarakpuri: Whoever turns away from it, verily, they will bear a heavy burden on the Day of Resurrection.
t.qarib: Whoever turns away from it will bear a heavy burden on the Day of Resurrection
t.wahiduddin: Whoever turns away from it will bear a heavy burden on the Day of Judgement
t.shakir: Whoever turns aside from it, he shall surely bear a burden on the day of resurrection
t.haleem: Whoever turns away from it will bear on the Day of Resurrection a heavy burden
t.maariful: Whoever turns away from it shall certainly bear a heavy burden on Doomsday
t.khattab: Whoever turns away from it will surely bear the burden ˹of sin˺ on the Day of Judgment
t.ghali: Whoever veers away from it, then surely upon the Day of the Resurrection he will carry an encumbrance
t.irving: Anyone who evades it will bear a burden on Resurrection Day
t.iwq: Whoever turns away from it - then verily, he will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden

t_id.kemenag: Barangsiapa berpaling dari pada Al qur'an maka sesungguhnya ia akan memikul dosa yang besar di hari kiamat,
t_id.sabiq: Barangsiapa menolak mempercayai dan berpetunjuk kepadanya, maka ia akan sesat dalam hidupnya. Di hari kiamat nanti, ia akan membawa dosa yang diperbuat dan akan dibalas dengan siksaan yang amat pedih.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Barang siapa berpaling daripada Alquran) artinya ia tidak beriman kepada Alquran (maka sesungguhnya ia akan memikul dosa yang besar di hari kiamat) beban yang sangat berat berupa dosa-dosa.
t_id.iwq: Barangsiapa berpaling dari pada Al qur'an maka sesungguhnya ia akan memikul dosa yang besar di hari kiamat,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «من أعرض عنه» فلم يؤمن به «فإنه يحمل يوم القيامة وزرا» حملا ثقيلا من الإثم
tafsir.siraj-ar: مَّنۡ أَعۡرَضَ عَنۡهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَحۡمِلُ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ وِزۡرًا (وِزْرًا: إِثْمًا عَظِيمًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Barang siapa berpaling daripada Alquran) artinya ia tidak beriman kepada Alquran (maka sesungguhnya ia akan memikul dosa yang besar di hari kiamat) beban yang sangat berat berupa dosa-dosa

ar: خَـٰلِدِینَ فِیهِۖ وَسَاۤءَ لَهُمۡ یَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِیَـٰمَةِ حِمۡلࣰا
ar_simple: خلدین فیه وساء لهم یوم ٱلقیمة حملا

t.sahih-int: [Abiding] eternally therein, and evil it is for them on the Day of Resurrection as a load
t.asad: they will abide in this [state], and grievous for them will be the weight [of that burden] on the Day of Resurrection
t.yusuf-ali: They will abide in this (state): and grievous will the burden be to them on that Day
t.pickthall: Abiding under it - an evil burden for them on the Day of Resurrection
t.arberry: therein abiding forever; how evil upon the Day of Resurrection that burden for them
t.hilali-khan: They will abide in that (state in the Fire of Hell), and evil indeed will it be that load for them on the Day of Resurrection
t.itani: Abiding therein forever. And wretched is their burden on the Day of Resurrection.
t.mubarakpuri: They will abide in that -- and evil indeed will it be that load for them on the Day of Resurrection.
t.qarib: carrying it eternally. What a terrible burden to carry on the Day of Resurrection
t.wahiduddin: which they shall bear forever. It will be a grievous burden for them on the Day of Judgement
t.shakir: Abiding in this (state), and evil will it be for them to bear on the day of resurrection
t.haleem: and will remain under it. What a terrible burden to carry on that Day
t.maariful: all such people living under it forever. And how evil for them will be that burden on the Doomsday
t.khattab: suffering its consequences forever. What an evil burden they will carry on Judgment Day
t.ghali: Eternally (abiding) therein; and (how) odious for them upon the Day of the Resurrection is it as a burden
t.irving: which he will remain with. Evil will be such a load for them on Resurrection Day
t.iwq: Abiding eternally therein, and evil it is for them on the Day of Resurrection as a load

t_id.kemenag: mereka kekal di dalam keadaan itu. Dan amat buruklah dosa itu sebagai beban bagi mereka di hari kiamat,
t_id.sabiq: Ia akan berada dalam siksaan itu selama-lamanya. Sungguh merupakan bawaan yang amat buruk di hari kiamat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka kekal di dalamnya) menanggung azab dosanya untuk selamanya. (Dan amat buruklah dosa itu sebagai beban bagi mereka di hari kiamat) lafal Himlan merupakan Tamyiz yang menafsirkan makna Dhamir dalam lafal Saa'a, sedangkan subjek yang dicelanya dibuang yaitu lafal Wizrahum, artinya dosa mereka. Huruf Lam yang ada pada lafal Lahum berfungsi untuk menerangkan. Kemudian lafal Yaumal Qiyaamah dijelaskan oleh ayat berikutnya, yaitu:
t_id.iwq: mereka kekal di dalam keadaan itu. Dan amat buruklah dosa itu sebagai beban bagi mereka di hari kiamat,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «خالدين فيه» أي في عذاب الوزر «وساء لهم يوم القيامة حملا» تمييز مفسر للضمير في ساء والمخصوص بالذم محذوف تقديره وزرهم، واللام للبيان ويبدل من يوم القيامة
tafsir.siraj-ar: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهِۖ وَسَآءَ لَهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ حِمۡلٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka kekal di dalamnya) menanggung azab dosanya untuk selamanya. (Dan amat buruklah dosa itu sebagai beban bagi mereka di hari kiamat) lafal Himlan merupakan Tamyiz yang menafsirkan makna Dhamir dalam lafal Saa'a, sedangkan subjek yang dicelanya dibuang yaitu lafal Wizrahum, artinya dosa mereka. Huruf Lam yang ada pada lafal Lahum berfungsi untuk menerangkan. Kemudian lafal Yaumal Qiyaamah dijelaskan oleh ayat berikutnya, yaitu

ar: یَوۡمَ یُنفَخُ فِی ٱلصُّورِۚ وَنَحۡشُرُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِینَ یَوۡمَئِذࣲ زُرۡقࣰا
ar_simple: یوم ینفخ فی ٱلصور ونحشر ٱلمجرمین یومئذ زرقا

t.sahih-int: The Day the Horn will be blown. And We will gather the criminals, that Day, blue-eyed
t.asad: on the Day when the trumpet is blown: for on that Day We will assemble all such as had been lost in sin, their eyes dimmed [by terror]
t.yusuf-ali: The Day when the Trumpet will be sounded: that Day, We shall gather the sinful, blear-eyed (with terror)
t.pickthall: The day when the Trumpet is blown. On that day we assemble the guilty white-eyed (with terror)
t.arberry: On the day the Trumpet is blown; and We shall muster the sinners upon that day with eyes staring
t.hilali-khan: The Day when the Trumpet will be blown (the second blowing): that Day, We shall gather the Mujrimun (criminals, polytheists, sinners, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, etc.) Zurqa: (blue or blind eyed with black faces)
t.itani: On the Day when the Trumpet is blown—We will gather the sinners on that Day, blue.
t.mubarakpuri: The Day when the Sur will be blown: that Day, We shall gather the criminals blue-eyed.
t.qarib: On that Day the Horn will be blown and We will gather those who force others to reject Our messages, blinded [with terror]
t.wahiduddin: the Day when the trumpet shall be blown: We shall gather all the sinners on that Day. Their eyes will turn blue with terror
t.shakir: On the day when the trumpet shall be blown, and We will gather the guilty, blue-eyed, on that day
t.haleem: When the trumpet is sounded and We gather the sinful, sightless
t.maariful: The Day when the Horn (Sūr) will be blown, and We shall gather them together while they will have turned blue on that day
t.khattab: ˹Beware of˺ the Day the Trumpet will be blown, and We will gather the wicked on that Day blue-faced ˹from horror and thirst˺
t.ghali: The Day the Trumpet will be blown, and We will muster the criminals upon that day blue (with terror)
t.irving: the day when the Trumpet shall be blown. We shall summon bleary-eyed criminals on that day
t.iwq: The Day the Horn will be blown. And We will gather the criminals, that Day, blue-eyed

t_id.kemenag: (yaitu) di hari (yang di waktu itu) ditiup sangkakala dan Kami akan mengumpulkan pada hari itu orang-orang yang berdosa dengan muka yang biru muram;
t_id.sabiq: Ingatkanlah kepada umatmu, Muhammad, suatu hari kala Kami memerintahkan malaikat untuk meniup sangkakala, sebuah tiupan untuk menghidupkan dan membangkitkan kembali manusia dari kuburnya untuk kemudian Kami seru agar berkumpul di padang Mahsyar. Orang-orang yang berbuat jahat akan Kami seret dengan muka yang biru: cemas dan takut.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu di hari yang di waktu itu ditiup sangkakala) untuk tiupan yang kedua kalinya (dan Kami akan mengumpulkan orang-orang yang berdosa) yaitu orang-orang kafir (pada hari itu dengan mata yang biru muram) yakni mata mereka tampak membiru dan muka mereka tampak hitam karena muram.
t_id.iwq: (yaitu) di hari (yang di waktu itu) ditiup sangkakala dan Kami akan mengumpulkan pada hari itu orang-orang yang berdosa dengan muka yang biru muram;

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يوم ننفخ في الصور» القرن، النفخة الثانية «ونحشر المجرمين» الكافرين «يومئذ زرقا» عيونهم مع سواد وجوههم
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَوۡمَ يُنفَخُ فِي ٱلصُّورِۚ وَنَحۡشُرُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ يَوۡمَئِذٖ زُرۡقٗا (الصُّورِ: القَرْنِ الَّذِي يَنْفُخُ فِيهِ إِسْرَافِيلُ - عليه السلام -, وَنَحْشُرُ: نَسُوقُ, زُرْقًا: زُرْقَ العُيُونِ مَعَ سَوَادِ وُجُوهِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu di hari yang di waktu itu ditiup sangkakala) untuk tiupan yang kedua kalinya (dan Kami akan mengumpulkan orang-orang yang berdosa) yaitu orang-orang kafir (pada hari itu dengan mata yang biru muram) yakni mata mereka tampak membiru dan muka mereka tampak hitam karena muram

ar: یَتَخَـٰفَتُونَ بَیۡنَهُمۡ إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا عَشۡرࣰا
ar_simple: یتخفتون بینهم إن لبثتم إلا عشرا

t.sahih-int: They will murmur among themselves, "You remained not but ten [days in the world]
t.asad: whispering unto one another, "You have spent but ten [days on earth]
t.yusuf-ali: In whispers will they consult each other: "Yet tarried not longer than ten (Days)
t.pickthall: Murmuring among themselves: Ye have tarried but ten (days)
t.arberry: whispering one to another, 'You have tarried only ten nights
t.hilali-khan: In whispers will they speak to each other (saying): "You stayed not longer than ten (days)
t.itani: Murmuring among themselves: “You have lingered only for ten.”
t.mubarakpuri: They will speak in a very low voice to each other (saying): "You stayed not longer than ten."
t.qarib: and they will whisper to each other, "You have spent only ten [days]
t.wahiduddin: and they shall murmur to one another, "You stayed only ten days on the earth
t.shakir: They shall consult together secretly: You did tarry but ten (centuries)
t.haleem: they will murmur to one another, ‘You stayed only ten days [on earth]’
t.maariful: and will be whispering among themselves, “You did not remain (in graves) more than ten (days)”
t.khattab: They will whisper among themselves, “You stayed no more than ten days ˹on the earth˺.”
t.ghali: (Speaking) among themselves in a hushed voice, "Decidedly you lingered (nothing) except ten (days)
t.irving: They will mutter to one another: "Did you only stay away ten [days]
t.iwq: They will murmur among themselves, "You remained not but ten days in the world

t_id.kemenag: mereka berbisik-bisik di antara mereka: "Kamu tidak berdiam (di dunia) melainkan hanyalah sepuluh (hari)"
t_id.sabiq: Mereka saling berbisik satu sama lain, dalam suasana penuh hina dan galau, tentang betapa pendeknya kehidupan dunia. Begitu pendeknya, hingga mereka seolah hidup dan merasakan kenikmatan dunia hanya sepuluh hari.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berbisik-bisik di antara mereka sendiri) yaitu berbicara dengan suara yang pelan-pelan ("Tidaklah) (kalian tinggal) di dunia (melainkan sepuluh) hari."
t_id.iwq: mereka berbisik-bisik di antara mereka: "Kamu tidak berdiam (di dunia) melainkan hanyalah sepuluh (hari)"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يتخافتون بينهم» يتسارون «إن» ما «لبثتم» في الدنيا «إلا عشرا» من الليالي بأيامها
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَتَخَٰفَتُونَ بَيۡنَهُمۡ إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا عَشۡرٗا (يَتَخَافَتُونَ: يَتَسَارُّونَ، وَيَتَهَامَسُونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berbisik-bisik di antara mereka sendiri) yaitu berbicara dengan suara yang pelan-pelan ("Tidaklah) (kalian tinggal) di dunia (melainkan sepuluh) hari

ar: نَّحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَا یَقُولُونَ إِذۡ یَقُولُ أَمۡثَلُهُمۡ طَرِیقَةً إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا یَوۡمࣰا
ar_simple: نحن أعلم بما یقولون إذ یقول أمثلهم طریقة إن لبثتم إلا یوما

t.sahih-int: We are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner will say, "You remained not but one day
t.asad: [But] We know best what they will be saying when the most perceptive of them shall say, "You have spent [there] but one day
t.yusuf-ali: We know best what they will say, when their leader most eminent in conduct will say: "Ye tarried not longer than a day
t.pickthall: We are Best Aware of what they utter when their best in conduct say: Ye have tarried but a day
t.arberry: We know very well what they will say, when the justest of them in the way will say, 'You have tarried only a day
t.hilali-khan: We know very well what they will say, when the best among them in knowledge and wisdom will say: "You stayed no longer than a day
t.itani: We are fully aware of what they say, when the most exemplary of them in conduct will say, “You have lingered only a day.”
t.mubarakpuri: We know very well what they will say, when the best among them in knowledge and wisdom will say: "You stayed no longer than a day!"
t.qarib: We know best what they will say when the most perceptive [among them] will say, "[In fact] you have spent only one day
t.wahiduddin: We know best what they will say. The most perceptive of them will say, "You stayed only one day
t.shakir: We know best what they say, when the fairest of them in course would say: You tarried but a day
t.haleem: We know best what they say- but the more perceptive of them will say, ‘Your stay [on earth] was only a single day.’
t.maariful: We know very well what they say, when the best of them in his ways will say, “You did not remain more than one day.”
t.khattab: We know best what they will say—the most reasonable of them will say, “You stayed no more than a day.”
t.ghali: We know best what they say, as the most exemplary of them in (their) mode (of life) say, "Decidedly you have lingered (nothing) except a day
t.irving: We are quite Aware of what they will say when the best behaved among them will say,. "You have only stayed away a day
t.iwq: We indeed are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner will say, "You remained not but one day

t_id.kemenag: Kami lebih mengetahui apa yang mereka katakan, ketika berkata orang yang paling lurus jalannya di antara mereka: "Kamu tidak berdiam (di dunia), melainkan hanyalah sehari saja".
t_id.sabiq: Namun bisik-bisik mereka itu terdengar pula. Kami lebih tahu tentang apa yang mereka bisikkan dan tentang apa yang dikatakan oleh salah seorang di antara mereka yang paling dekat menggambarkan perasaannya mengenai dunia, yang mengatakan bahwa mereka tinggal di dunia hanya sehari saja.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kami lebih mengetahui apa yang mereka katakan) dalam bisikan mereka itu. Maksudnya, keadaannya tidaklah seperti apa yang mereka katakan (ketika berkata orang yang paling tepat di antara mereka) yakni paling lurus (penilaiannya) sehubungan dengan hal ini, ("Kalian tidak berdiam di dunia melainkan hanya sehari saja") mereka menilai minim sekali masa tinggal mereka di dunia, hal ini disebabkan kengerian-kengerian yang mereka saksikan di alam akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Kami lebih mengetahui apa yang mereka katakan, ketika berkata orang yang paling lurus jalannya di antara mereka: "Kamu tidak berdiam (di dunia), melainkan hanyalah sehari saja".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «نحن أعلم بما يقولون» في ذلك: أي ليس كما قالوا «إذ يقول أمثلهم» أعدلهم «طريقة» فيه «إن لبثتم إلا يوما» يستقلون لبثهم في الدنيا جدا بما يعاينونه في الآخرة من أهوالها
tafsir.siraj-ar: نَّحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذۡ يَقُولُ أَمۡثَلُهُمۡ طَرِيقَةً إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا يَوۡمٗا (أَمْثَلُهُمْ طَرِيقَةً: أَعْلَمُهُمْ، وَأَوْفَاهُمْ عَقْلًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kami lebih mengetahui apa yang mereka katakan) dalam bisikan mereka itu. Maksudnya, keadaannya tidaklah seperti apa yang mereka katakan (ketika berkata orang yang paling tepat di antara mereka) yakni paling lurus (penilaiannya) sehubungan dengan hal ini, ("Kalian tidak berdiam di dunia melainkan hanya sehari saja") mereka menilai minim sekali masa tinggal mereka di dunia, hal ini disebabkan kengerian-kengerian yang mereka saksikan di alam akhirat

ar: وَیَسۡءَلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلۡجِبَالِ فَقُلۡ یَنسِفُهَا رَبِّی نَسۡفࣰا
ar_simple: ویسءلونك عن ٱلجبال فقل ینسفها ربی نسفا

t.sahih-int: And they ask you about the mountains, so say, "My Lord will blow them away with a blast
t.asad: AND THEY WILL ask thee about [what will happen to] the mountains [when this world comes to an end]. Say, then: "My Sustainer will scatter them far and wide
t.yusuf-ali: They ask thee concerning the Mountains: say, "My Lord will uproot them and scatter them as dust
t.pickthall: They will ask thee of the mountains (on that day). Say: My Lord will break them into scattered dust
t.arberry: They will question thee concerning the mountains. Say: 'My Lord will scatter them as ashes
t.hilali-khan: And they ask you concerning the mountains, say; "My Lord will blast them and scatter them as particles of dust
t.itani: And they ask you about the mountains. Say, “My Lord will crumble them utterly.”
t.mubarakpuri: And they ask you concerning the mountains. Say: "My Lord will blast them and scatter them as particles of dust."
t.qarib: And [should] they ask you [Prophet] about the mountains, say, "My Lord will blow them away into dust
t.wahiduddin: They ask you about the mountains. Say, "My Lord will scatter them as dust
t.shakir: And they ask you about the mountains. Say: My Lord will carry them away from the roots
t.haleem: They ask you [Prophet] about the mountains: say, ‘[On that Day] my Lord will blast them into dust
t.maariful: They ask you about the mountains. So, say (to them), “My Lord will crush them into dust thoroughly
t.khattab: And ˹if˺ they ask you ˹O Prophet˺ about the mountains, ˹then˺ say, “My Lord will wipe them out completely
t.ghali: And they ask you about the mountains. Then say, "My Lord will crush them into powder (Literally: crush them "an utter" crushing)
t.irving: They will ask you about the mountains, SO SAY: "My Lord will crumble them into powder
t.iwq: And they ask you about the mountains, so say, "My Lord will blow them away with a blast

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu tentang gunung-gunung, maka katakanlah: "Tuhanku akan menghancurkannya (di hari kiamat) sehancur-hancurnya,
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang yang mengingkari hari kebangkitan itu bertanya kepadamu, Muhammad, tentang nasib gunung-gunung pada hari kiamat yang kamu bicarakan itu. Jawablah bahwa Allah akan menghancur- leburkannya bagai pasir, lalu menerbangkannya dengan tiupan angin hingga bertebaran. (?)
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu tentang gunung-gunung,) bagaimana jadinya di hari kiamat nanti? (maka katakanlah) kepada mereka ("Rabbku akan menghancurkannya sehancur-hancurnya,) seumpamanya Dia meleburkannya menjadi debu yang lembut kemudian diterbangkan-Nya dengan angin.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu tentang gunung-gunung, maka katakanlah: "Tuhanku akan menghancurkannya (di hari kiamat) sehancur-hancurnya,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ويسألونك عن الجبال» كيف تكون يوم القيامة «فقل» لهم «ينسفها ربي نسفا» بأن يفتتها كالرمل السائل ثم يطيرها بالرياح
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَيَسۡـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلۡجِبَالِ فَقُلۡ يَنسِفُهَا رَبِّي نَسۡفٗا (يَنسِفُهَا: يُزِيلُهَا عَنْ أَمَاكِنِهَا, نَسْفًا: يَجْعَلُهَا هَبَاءً مُنْبَثًّا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu tentang gunung-gunung,) bagaimana jadinya di hari kiamat nanti? (maka katakanlah) kepada mereka ("Rabbku akan menghancurkannya sehancur-hancurnya,) seumpamanya Dia meleburkannya menjadi debu yang lembut kemudian diterbangkan-Nya dengan angin

ar: فَیَذَرُهَا قَاعࣰا صَفۡصَفࣰا
ar_simple: فیذرها قاعا صفصفا

t.sahih-int: And He will leave the earth a level plain
t.asad: and leave the earths level and bare
t.yusuf-ali: He will leave them as plains smooth and level
t.pickthall: And leave it as an empty plain
t.arberry: then He will leave them a level hollow
t.hilali-khan: Then He shall leave it as a level smooth plain
t.itani: And leave them desolate waste.
t.mubarakpuri: "Then He shall leave it as a level smooth plain."
t.qarib: and He will level them into empty plains
t.wahiduddin: and leave the earth level and bare
t.shakir: Then leave it a plain, smooth level
t.haleem: and leave a flat plain
t.maariful: then will turn them into a leveled plain
t.khattab: leaving the earth level and bare
t.ghali: So He will leave them behind as a desolate spacious plain
t.irving: and leave them as flat as a prairie
t.iwq: And He will leave the earth a level plain

t_id.kemenag: maka Dia akan menjadikan (bekas) gunung-gunung itu datar sama sekali,
t_id.sabiq: Dengan demikian, setelah gunung-gunung itu dihancurkan, bekas tempatnya menjadi rata dengan tanah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Dia akan menjadikan bekas gunung-gunung itu datar) merata (dengan tanah) yakni rata sama sekali tiada bekasnya.
t_id.iwq: maka Dia akan menjadikan (bekas) gunung-gunung itu datar sama sekali,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فيذرها قاعا» منبسطا «صفصفا» مستويا
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَيَذَرُهَا قَاعٗا صَفۡصَفٗا (فَيَذَرُهَا: فَيَتْرُكُهَا, قَاعًا: أَرْضًا مَلْسَاءَ لَا نَبَاتَ بِهَا, صَفْصَفًا: مُسْتَوِيَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Dia akan menjadikan bekas gunung-gunung itu datar) merata (dengan tanah) yakni rata sama sekali tiada bekasnya

ar: لَّا تَرَىٰ فِیهَا عِوَجࣰا وَلَاۤ أَمۡتࣰا
ar_simple: لا ترى فیها عوجا ولا أمتا

t.sahih-int: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation
t.asad: [so that] thou wilt see no curve thereon, and no ruggedness
t.yusuf-ali: Nothing crooked or curved wilt thou see in their place
t.pickthall: Wherein thou seest neither curve nor ruggedness
t.arberry: wherein thou wilt see no crookedness neither any curving
t.hilali-khan: You will see therein nothing crooked or curved
t.itani: You will see in them neither crookedness, nor deviation.”
t.mubarakpuri: "You will see therein no crookedness nor curve."
t.qarib: where you will not see a dip or an elevation
t.wahiduddin: with neither hollows nor upthrust mounds to be seen
t.shakir: You shall not see therein any crookedness or unevenness
t.haleem: with no peak or trough to be seen
t.maariful: in which you will see neither a curve nor an uneven place.”
t.khattab: with neither depressions nor elevations to be seen.”
t.ghali: Wherein you will see no crookedness nor any curving
t.irving: You will see no rough spots nor any unevenness on them
t.iwq: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation

t_id.kemenag: tidak ada sedikitpun kamu lihat padanya tempat yang rendah dan yang tinggi-tinggi.
t_id.sabiq: Kamu tak akan lagi melihat dataran rendah atau dataran tinggi di muka bumi. Bumi seolah-olah belum pernah dijamah dan dibangun sebelumnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tidak ada sedikit pun kamu lihat padanya tempat yang melengkung) tempat yang rendah (dan tidak pula tempat yang tinggi) tempat yang tinggi-tinggi.
t_id.iwq: tidak ada sedikitpun kamu lihat padanya tempat yang rendah dan yang tinggi-tinggi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لا ترى فيها عوجا» انخفاضا «ولا أمْتا» ارتفاعا
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَّا تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجٗا وَلَآ أَمۡتٗا (عِوَجًا: انْخِفَاضًا, أَمْتًا: ارْتِفَاعًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tidak ada sedikit pun kamu lihat padanya tempat yang melengkung) tempat yang rendah (dan tidak pula tempat yang tinggi) tempat yang tinggi-tinggi

ar: یَوۡمَئِذࣲ یَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلدَّاعِیَ لَا عِوَجَ لَهُۥۖ وَخَشَعَتِ ٱلۡأَصۡوَاتُ لِلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ فَلَا تَسۡمَعُ إِلَّا هَمۡسࣰا
ar_simple: یومئذ یتبعون ٱلداعی لا عوج له وخشعت ٱلأصوات للرحمن فلا تسمع إلا همسا

t.sahih-int: That Day, everyone will follow [the call of] the Caller [with] no deviation therefrom, and [all] voices will be stilled before the Most Merciful, so you will not hear except a whisper [of footsteps]
t.asad: On that Day, all will. follow the summoning Voice from which there will be no escape; and all sounds will be hushed before the Most Gracious, and thou wilt hear nothing but a faint sough in the air
t.yusuf-ali: On that Day will they follow the Caller (straight): no crookedness (can they show) him: all sounds shall humble themselves in the Presence of (Allah) Most Gracious: nothing shalt thou hear but the tramp of their feet (as they march)
t.pickthall: On that day they follow the summoner who deceiveth not, and voices are hushed for the Beneficent, and thou hearest but a faint murmur
t.arberry: On that day they will follow the Summoner in whom is no crookedness; voices will be hushed to the All-merciful, so that thou hearest naught but a murmuring
t.hilali-khan: On that Day mankind will follow strictly (the voice of) Allah's caller, no crookedness (that is without going to the right or left of that voice) will they show him (Allah's caller). And all voices will be humbled for the Most Beneficent (Allah), and nothing shall you hear but the low voice of their footsteps
t.itani: On that Day, they will follow the caller, without any deviation. Voices will be hushed before the Merciful, and you will hear nothing but murmur.
t.mubarakpuri: On that Day mankind will follow strictly (the voice of) Allah's caller, no crookedness (that is without going to the right or left of that voice) will they show him (Allah's caller). And all voices will be humbled for the Most Gracious (Allah), and nothing shall you hear except Hamsa.
t.qarib: On that Day, everyone will follow the Caller from whom there is no escape. Then, all voices will be quieted before the Merciful-to-all and only whispers will be heard
t.wahiduddin: On that Day all will follow the summoning voice from which there is no escape; and all voices will be hushed before the Lord of Mercy, and nothing will be heard except a subdued murmur
t.shakir: On that day they shall follow the inviter, there is no crookedness in him, and the voices shall be low before the Beneficent Allah so that you shall not hear aught but a soft sound
t.haleem: On that Day, people will follow the summoner from whom there is no escape; every voice will be hushed for the Lord of Mercy; only whispers will be heard
t.maariful: That day they will follow the caller who will find no deviation (from anyone). And the voices will turn low in awe for the RaHmān (All-Merciful, Allah). So, you will hear not but whispering
t.khattab: On that Day all will follow the caller ˹for assembly˺, ˹and˺ none will dare to deviate. All voices will be hushed before the Most Compassionate. Only whispers will be heard
t.ghali: Upon that Day, they will closely follow the caller in whom is no crookedness; and voices will submit to The All-Merciful so that you hear nothing except a (faint) muttering
t.irving: On that day they will follow the Crier who has no crookedness in him either; their voices will be hushed before the Mercy-giving and you will hear only [people] shuffling past
t.iwq: That Day, everyone will follow the call of the Caller with no deviation therefrom, and all voices will be stilled before the Ever-Merciful, so you will not hear except a whisper of footsteps

t_id.kemenag: Pada hari itu manusia mengikuti (menuju kepada suara) penyeru dengan tidak berbelok-belok; dan merendahlah semua suara kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu tidak mendengar kecuali bisikan saja.
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari kiamat, setelah bangkit dari kuburnya, manusia akan mengikuti dengan pasrah seruan yang mengajak mereka berkumpul di padang Mahsyar. Tak seorang pun dapat berbelok ke kanan atau ke kiri. Saat itu, semua suara pun merendah tenang dan takut akan kebesaran Sang Maha Pengasih. Yang terdengar hanyalah bisikan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari itu) pada hari ketika gunung-gunung itu dihancurkan (manusia mengikuti) manusia semuanya mengikuti sesudah mereka dibangunkan dari kuburannya (penyeru) yang menggiring mereka dengan suaranya ke padang Mahsyar, dia adalah malaikat Israfil. Dia mengatakan, "Kemarilah kamu sekalian ke hadapan Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah" (dengan tidak berbelok-belok) sewaktu mereka menuruti panggilan suara itu. Atau dengan kata lain mereka tidak mempunyai kemampuan untuk tidak mengikuti anjuran suara itu (dan merendahlah) yakni menjadi tenanglah (semua suara kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu tidak mendengar melainkan hanya bisikan saja) suara telapak kaki mereka sewaktu berjalan menuju ke padang Mahsyar bagaikan suara teracak kaki unta bila berjalan.
t_id.iwq: Pada hari itu manusia mengikuti (menuju kepada suara) penyeru dengan tidak berbelok-belok; dan merendahlah semua suara kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu tidak mendengar kecuali bisikan saja.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يومئذ» أي يوم إذ نسفت الجبال «يتبعون» أي الناس بعد القيام من القبور «الداعي» إلى المحشر بصوته وهو إسرافيل يقول: هلموا إلى عرض الرحمن «لا عوج له» أي لاتباعهم: أي لا يقدرون أن لا يتعبوا «وخشعت» سكنت «الأصوات للرحمن فلا تسمع إلا همسا» صوت وطء الأقدام في نقلها إلى المحشر كصوت أخفاف الإبل في مشيها
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَوۡمَئِذٖ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلدَّاعِيَ لَا عِوَجَ لَهُۥۖ وَخَشَعَتِ ٱلۡأَصۡوَاتُ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ فَلَا تَسۡمَعُ إِلَّا هَمۡسٗا (لَا عِوَجَ لَهُ: لَا مَحِيدَ عَنْ دَعْوَةِ الدَّاعِي, وَخَشَعَتْ: سَكَنَتْ خُضُوعًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari itu) pada hari ketika gunung-gunung itu dihancurkan (manusia mengikuti) manusia semuanya mengikuti sesudah mereka dibangunkan dari kuburannya (penyeru) yang menggiring mereka dengan suaranya ke padang Mahsyar, dia adalah malaikat Israfil. Dia mengatakan, "Kemarilah kamu sekalian ke hadapan Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah" (dengan tidak berbelok-belok) sewaktu mereka menuruti panggilan suara itu. Atau dengan kata lain mereka tidak mempunyai kemampuan untuk tidak mengikuti anjuran suara itu (dan merendahlah) yakni menjadi tenanglah (semua suara kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu tidak mendengar melainkan hanya bisikan saja) suara telapak kaki mereka sewaktu berjalan menuju ke padang Mahsyar bagaikan suara teracak kaki unta bila berjalan

ar: یَوۡمَئِذࣲ لَّا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةُ إِلَّا مَنۡ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنُ وَرَضِیَ لَهُۥ قَوۡلࣰا
ar_simple: یومئذ لا تنفع ٱلشفعة إلا من أذن له ٱلرحمن ورضی له قولا

t.sahih-int: That Day, no intercession will benefit except [that of] one to whom the Most Merciful has given permission and has accepted his word
t.asad: On that Day, intercession shall be of no avail [to any] save him in whose case the Most Gracious will have granted leave therefor, and whose word [of faith) He will have accepted
t.yusuf-ali: On that Day shall no intercession avail except for those for whom permission has been granted by (Allah) Most Gracious and whose word is acceptable to Him
t.pickthall: On that day no intercession availeth save (that of) him unto whom the Beneficent hath given leave and whose word He accepteth
t.arberry: Upon that day the intercession will not profit, save for him to whom the All-merciful gives leave, and whose speech He approves
t.hilali-khan: On that day no intercession shall avail, except the one for whom the Most Beneficent (Allah) has given permission and whose word is acceptable to Him
t.itani: On that Day, intercession will not avail, except for him permitted by the Merciful, and whose words He has approved.
t.mubarakpuri: On that day no intercession shall avail, except the one for whom the Most Gracious has given permission and whose word is acceptable to Him.
t.qarib: On that Day, no intercession will be of value except from one to whom the Merciful-to-all has given permission and was pleased with his speech
t.wahiduddin: On that Day no intercession will avail, except from one who has received the sanction of the Merciful and of whose words He approves
t.shakir: On that day shall no intercession avail except of him whom the Beneficent Allah allows and whose word He is pleased with
t.haleem: On that Day, intercession will be useless except from those to whom the Lord of Mercy has granted permission and whose words He approves
t.maariful: That day no intercession will be of any use to anyone, except the one whom the RaHmān (All-Merciful, Allah) has permitted and was pleased with his word
